Hawkroot connects businesses with pre-vetted data and technology professionals for project-based engagements. We help organisations across multiple sectors deliver measurable outcomes in analytics, reporting, automation, and digital transformation. We work with clients and professionals across Europe, including the Netherlands, supporting data and analytics project delivery remotely. Our team gives members direct exposure to real client projects from day one.

Hawkroot is seeking a Graduate Data Analyst to join our remote team on a temporary basis. This is a temporary, part-time opportunity for recent graduates based in the Netherlands who are looking to apply their analytical skills to real-world data projects and build practical experience in a professional environment.
Working hours: 20 hours per week, Monday to Friday
- Organise, clean, and prepare datasets to ensure accuracy and readiness for analysis
- Write and execute SQL queries to extract and manipulate data from relevant sources
- Perform data analysis using Microsoft Excel and summarise findings in a clear format
- Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports using Power BI or similar tools
- Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies within datasets and communicate findings to the team
- Assist in preparing data outputs for client-facing reports and internal documentation
- Maintain clear records of analytical processes and contribute to data quality improvements
- A recent graduate with a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
- No prior professional experience required — strong coursework, personal projects, or certifications are welcomed
- Working knowledge of SQL and proficiency in Microsoft Excel
- Exposure to Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualisation platforms is beneficial
- Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and structured thinking
- Confident written and verbal communication skills in English
- Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a remote environment
- Based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands
- Direct involvement in live data projects across multiple client engagements
- Exposure to end-to-end data workflows including extraction, analysis, and reporting
- Practical experience with SQL, Excel, and Power BI in a professional environment
- Remote-first working culture with a supportive and experienced team
- A meaningful addition to your professional portfolio and CV
- Gross hourly rate of €18.00 per hour

Hawkroot is committed to equal treatment and non-discrimination across all stages of hiring. We welcome applications from recent graduates of all backgrounds, nationalities, and experiences. All candidates based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands are encouraged to apply.

About Hawkroot

Hawkroot connects businesses with pre-vetted data and technology professionals for project-based engagements. We help organisations across multiple sectors deliver measurable outcomes in analytics, reporting, automation, and digital transformation. We work with clients and professionals across Europe, including the Netherlands, supporting data and analytics project delivery remotely. Our team gives members direct exposure to real client projects from day one.


Role Overview

Hawkroot is seeking a Graduate Data Analyst to join our remote team on a temporary basis. This is a temporary, part-time opportunity for recent graduates based in the Netherlands who are looking to apply their analytical skills to real-world data projects and build practical experience in a professional environment.


Contract: Temporary (3 months)

Working hours: 20 hours per week, Monday to Friday

Hourly rate: €18.00 gross per hour

Work arrangement: Fully remote

Location: Netherlands


What You Will Be Doing

- Organise, clean, and prepare datasets to ensure accuracy and readiness for analysis

- Write and execute SQL queries to extract and manipulate data from relevant sources

- Perform data analysis using Microsoft Excel and summarise findings in a clear format

- Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports using Power BI or similar tools

- Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies within datasets and communicate findings to the team

- Assist in preparing data outputs for client-facing reports and internal documentation

- Maintain clear records of analytical processes and contribute to data quality improvements


What We Are Looking For

- A recent graduate with a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field

- No prior professional experience required — strong coursework, personal projects, or certifications are welcomed

- Working knowledge of SQL and proficiency in Microsoft Excel

- Exposure to Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualisation platforms is beneficial

- Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and structured thinking

- Confident written and verbal communication skills in English

- Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a remote environment

- Based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands


What This Role Offers

- Direct involvement in live data projects across multiple client engagements

- Exposure to end-to-end data workflows including extraction, analysis, and reporting

- Practical experience with SQL, Excel, and Power BI in a professional environment

- Remote-first working culture with a supportive and experienced team

- A meaningful addition to your professional portfolio and CV

- Gross hourly rate of €18.00 per hour


Equal Opportunity Statement

Hawkroot is committed to equal treatment and non-discrimination across all stages of hiring. We welcome applications from recent graduates of all backgrounds, nationalities, and experiences. All candidates based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands are encouraged to apply.

Our client is a fast-growing Dutch SaaS company that lets consumers find and buy insurance through a smart app. With roughly 50 people and ambitions to expand across Europe, they're building out the data team that powers every business decision.
Build and own the data model that sits underneath every dashboard, report, and analysis in the company.
Set up and maintain data marts so the business always has clean, ready-to-use data at their fingertips.
Own the numbers - from financial close reports to operational updates - and make sure the right people have the right information at the right time.
Dig into the data for Finance and Insurance: spot patterns before anyone asks, agree on what the key metrics mean, and answer the questions that move decisions forward.
3–6 years of experience as a data analyst or in a similar role
Python for data transformation and ad-hoc analysis
Experience building dashboards (QuickSight, Power BI, Tableau or similar)
Comfortable working independently and owning your output
€80,000 - €85,000 gross per year, based on experience
Real ownership - small team, visible impact
Working in an environment where things actually get built

The role is office-based in Amsterdam (3 days on-site). They operate in a high-performance environment where you'll be expected to work autonomously, get into the detail when needed, and keep up with a team that moves quickly and changes direction when necessary.
Interested and would you like to hear more? Please send me a direct message on either LinkedIn or email me with your most recent CV to: jan@doghouse-recruitment.nl

Do you want to be part of a globally operating team of innovators? Do you also think bigger than big? And, do you have what it takes to make an impact? Then we might be looking for you. Find out more about our vacancy for a Junior Data Analyst.

We are Huisman. We develop step changing technical solutions and translate these into the design and construction of the largest onshore and offshore installations. For decades, we have been working all over the world for offshore wind, geothermal energy, oil & gas, leisure, civil and port logistics industries.
With our passionate workforce and worldwide production, service & sales facilities we are equipped for impact in these times of energy transition.
“Huisman combines engineering and digitalisation to create solutions for the future of renewable energies. It's a great place to be!”
About The Position Of Junior Data Analyst

As a Junior Data Analyst, you will analyse equipment performance data and help develop reliable, reusable analytics for our engineers and customers. You will work closely with senior programmers and mechanical/maintenance engineers on complex, real-world engineering challenges. In addition, you will play a key role in clearly communicating complex findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, ensuring that your insights deliver a lasting impact.
What do we expect of you?
As a Data Analyst at Huisman Digital, you will transform equipment performance data into actionable insights for our clients and internal engineers. Your responsibilities will include:
Maintaining and improving our codebook and project configuration files
Collaborating with mechanical and maintenance engineers to identify improvement opportunities and deliver data-driven solutions
Maintaining, validating, and enhancing analytical tools and models that address complex engineering challenges
Communicating findings clearly to stakeholders to drive meaningful action.
Wondering if you are 'equipped for impact'?

If you enjoy mechanical and control engineering challenges and love analysing equipment data to generate insights, tackling programming challenges, dashboards, JSON files, or presentations, then you are a good match for this role.
Proactive, with a focus on results and practical, hands-on problem-solving
A good team player with a collaborative spirit
Passionate about continuous improvement and development.
A bachelor’s or master’s degree in mechanical engineering or a related technical engineering field.
Hands-on experience with programming, statistical analysis, and dashboard development.
Proficiency in Python for data analysis and familiarity with SQL (PostgreSQL).
Experience maintaining and improving codebooks and project configuration files.
Experience contributing to the maintenance, validation, and enhancement of analytical tools and models.
Additionally, It Is Nice To Have
Basic version control (Git), Jupyter Notebooks, and simple ETL/data-cleaning workflows.
Interest in heavy equipment, offshore/wind, or industrial IoT/telemetry.
Some Practical Things We Need You To Have
An EU passport/valid work permit for The Netherlands
A good command of the English language verbally and in writing.

We design and produce in-house and deliver our turn-key products to clients worldwide. Our clients choose us for our entrepreneurial spirit, skills, reliability, and innovative solutions. Examples? Consider the Fibre Rope System. So, if you join Huisman make sure you are equipped for impact. In other words, surprise us, our clients and… yourself!
What do we have to offer?
An excellent benefits package tailored to your experience and education level
Open, informal, no-nonsense culture with short communication lines.
Apply online for the position of Junior Data Analyst by using the application form at the bottom of this page. We are curious to see what kind of impact you will make.
Still need a bit more info or looking for something that suits you better?
please contact us via our contact form for more information or sign up for our job alert to receive details of new opportunities in your inbox.
Disclaimer For Recruitment And Selection Agencies

We don’t accept any unsolicited applications or CVs from recruitment and selection agencies. Proposing candidates without invitation is not appreciated and will not be considered by Huisman. Applications received are property of Huisman. We don’t appreciate acquisition and this also applies to Huisman colleagues or associated Huisman companies.

We're supporting a major enterprise data transformation program and are looking for an experienced Data Steward / Data Governance Consultant to help shape and improve customer domain data products within a modern Data Mesh environment.

This is a hands-on role focused on data quality, governance frameworks, metadata management, and stakeholder enablement - ensuring customer data products are trusted, discoverable, and built to consistent standards across the organization.
You'll work closely with data engineers, product teams, and business stakeholders to improve data reliability, define governance best practices, and embed a strong data-as-a-product mindset.
Define and maintain data quality rules, validation tests, and monitoring dashboards (primarily using SQL, dbt & Elementary)
Lead root cause analysis on data quality issues, driving corrective actions through to resolution
Maintain and improve business definitions, metadata, and harmonization rules across customer datasets
Review data models, data contracts, and data mappings to ensure alignment with governance standards
Champion data governance best practice, improving discoverability, ownership, and usability of data products
Present data quality insights and governance recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate closely with Data Engineers, Product Owners, Software Engineers, and Country Data Leads
Support adoption of Data Mesh principles, embedding ownership and accountability into domain data products
Strong hands-on experience in data quality management and implementation
Advanced SQL skills, with experience writing and evaluating dbt data tests
Experience working with data quality frameworks, including rule definition, testing, issue investigation, and remediation
Experience using data visualization tools such as Power BI or Looker Studio
Experience working with customer data (profiles, transactions, behavioral events) highly desirable
What Makes Someone Successful in This Role:
✔ Ability to translate business requirements into measurable data quality standards
✔ Comfortable influencing stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams
✔ Proactive, collaborative mindset - someone who can help define the playbook, not just follow it
✔ Passion for Data Governance, Data Mesh, and Data as a Product principles
Rate: 65 per hour or equivalent to €80k per year (including holiday, pension and expenses)

  • Lead root cause analysis on data quality issues, driving corrective actions through to resolution
  • Maintain and improve business definitions, metadata, and harmonization rules across customer datasets
  • Review data models, data contracts, and data mappings to ensure alignment with governance standards
  • Champion data governance best practice, improving discoverability, ownership, and usability of data products
  • Present data quality insights and governance recommendations clearly to stakeholders
  • Collaborate closely with Data Engineers, Product Owners, Software Engineers, and Country Data Leads
  • Support adoption of Data Mesh principles, embedding ownership and accountability into domain data products


    • Required Experience

    • Strong hands-on experience in data quality management and implementation
    • Advanced SQL skills, with experience writing and evaluating dbt data tests
    • Experience working with data quality frameworks, including rule definition, testing, issue investigation, and remediation
    • Strong understanding of:
    • Data modeling
    • Data warehousing
    • Metadata management
    • Data cataloging
    • Data governance frameworks
    • Experience using data visualization tools such as Power BI or Looker Studio
    • Familiarity with cloud environments (ideally GCP)
    • Python / Regex experience beneficial
    • Experience working with customer data (profiles, transactions, behavioral events) highly desirable


    What Makes Someone Successful in This Role:

    ✔ Strong analytical and problem-solving capability

    ✔ Ability to translate business requirements into measurable data quality standards

    ✔ Comfortable influencing stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams

    ✔ Proactive, collaborative mindset - someone who can help define the playbook, not just follow it

    ✔ Passion for Data Governance, Data Mesh, and Data as a Product principles


    Contract Length: 3 months (extension likely)

    Location: Netherlands / Hybrid-Remote

    Start: ASAP

    Rate: 65 per hour or equivalent to €80k per year (including holiday, pension and expenses)

    Interim payroll contract - No ZZP

Allowance: 625 euro per month (40hrs, 5 days) & full reimbursement of travel expenses

You will be part of an international small team with the ambition to drive digital technology forward for Kraft Heinz R&D International Zone. Expect to be part of the team from day one. Strong learning experience on data visualization with many practical deliverables which will be used directly by the organization.
Support dashboard creation to support day to day work of R&D functions
Support digital transformation across the R&D team (e.g. communication about changes- identification of digital tools required by R&D team)
Investigate new tools and trends in Digital world
Participate & contribute into meetings related to Digital strategy
You should have strong communication skills and fluency in English, both written and verbal (Dutch is not a requirement but other languages are a plus)
EU - Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch university for at least 50% of the duration of the internship.
Non - EU Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch University for the whole duration of the internship.
NOTE: EU working rights are required (Either you have EU Citizenship, you are studying in the Netherlands or have relevant student work visa)
Master’s or Bachelor’s student in Business Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
Data visualization skills (Power BI is a big plus)
Being comfortable with working with big data
Having a structured personality with ability to learn fast
Previous internship with data visualization or experience with data analytics is a plus
CV review – We screen applications for key eligibility criteria (e.g., EU working rights).
Screening interview – An initial interview conducted via Microsoft Teams.
Offer – Successful candidates will receive an offer to join the program.
Please note: The recruitment process can take 4–6 weeks in total, from application to offer.

We take interns seriously. You can expect us to give you 1-on-1 mentorship from one of our experienced colleagues. We will work together on your personal learning goals and are keen to hear all the good ideas you have! We will give you the space and support to make mistakes and grow. Become a part of a close intern community as well as a young and ambitious work environment (including online).
As a Kraft Heinz intern you’ll be the owner of your own projects and especially of your own career. At Kraft Heinz, we provide the playing field. You change the game. Are you up for the challenge?

The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. We’re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands.

We spark joy around mealtime with our iconic brands, including Heinz, Kraft, Bull's-Eye, HP, Lea and Perrins, Quero, ABC, Master, Banquete, Orlando, Benedicta, Honig, Pudliszki, Wattie’s among others.

No matter the brand, we’re united under one vision: To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 39,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we’re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion – and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen – there’s only one thing to do: join our table and let’s make life delicious!
Our Culture of Ownership, Meritocracy & Collaboration

We're not afraid to think differently. Embrace new ideas. Dream big. We empower our people at every level – from entry-level intern to senior leader – to own their work. We share a responsibility to think like Owners – to be mindful of the collective and sustained success of Kraft Heinz – which we apply to every situation, every day. As part of Kraft Heinz, you're supported to grow and achieve. You’re expected to bring your authentic self to work every day, to lead with humility, and drive outstanding performance at every level – and you’ll be rewarded. You’re given opportunities to leave a mark and build a legacy. But you won’t do it alone.  You’re supported by passionate teammates along the way, and our collective, collaborative spirit fuels our incredible progress.
Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes.

4385458772,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-at-nielsen-4385458772?position=9&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=EsSIes1VHFpgWhrLk1%2BBiw%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst,Nielsen,"Rotterdam, South Holland, Netherlands","Company Description

At Nielsen, we are passionate about our work to power a better media future for all people by providing powerful insights that drive client decisions and deliver extraordinary results. Our talented, global workforce is dedicated to capturing audience engagement with content - wherever and whenever it’s consumed. Together, we are proudly rooted in our deep legacy as we stand at the forefront of the media revolution. When you join Nielsen, you will join a dynamic team committed to excellence, perseverance, and the ambition to make an impact together. We champion you, because when you succeed, we do too. We enable your best to power our future.

In this position, you contribute to the successful data update of our software solutions for advertisers, media owners and media agencies. Our solutions help these companies in making decisions around their marketing communication budgets. Based upon research data combined with analytical and econometric approaches our solutions answer questions like ""What budget is needed to generate an increase of 10% in brand preference? What media mix contributes the most to the revenue?""

You will be working in (small) project teams on the design, execution, implementation and operational management of projects. This involves analyzing data, setting up surveys, designing a good way to integrate data (survey data, modeling and industry knowledge) into our software tools and maintaining contact with clients – both internal and external. In your role, you will focus on the technical implementation of the project and will be responsible for the quality and delivery of the project on the technical aspect. Additionally, you will act as a liaison between various parts of our business: data processing and software development, and have a chance to be involved in the entire project from client request and solution design to tool delivery and training.
Education: A Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Econometrics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a similar area.

Professional Experience: 5+ years of relevant experience in a similar analytical role. Good understanding of the Media industry. Experience in data analysis and software configuration projects. Experience in similar companies (media agencies, media analytics) is preferred.
Scripting Proficiency: Strong, hands-on experience using Python, with a particular emphasis on data manipulation and transformation.
Data Integration: Proven experience in designing, building, and maintaining data integrations, including data pipelines.

Technical leadership within the team: comfortable with troubleshooting and in leading process improvement for our automations (looking for new solutions to increase efficiency and quality and actively implementing them)
SaaS Environment: Demonstrable experience working with and within a SaaS (Software as a Service) environment.
Tech Stack: Proficiency with Python, proficient with SQL and with modern collaboration and development tools, including Jira, GitLab, Git, Confluence, AWS Airflow experience and Google Suite.
Global Collaboration: A track record of working effectively and collaboratively in geographically distributed teams.
Communication & leadership skills: ability to coordinate and mentor a team
column2: also ability to cross collaborations with software development teams. Ability to translate non technical stakeholders.

Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands


This is an opportunity to step into a highly visible Data Analyst role where your insights directly influence commercial and operational decisions. You will sit at the heart of a growing data function, combining hands-on analysis with project ownership to help embed data-driven thinking across the business.


The Company

They are a large, internationally operating organisation with a strong focus on digital transformation and operational excellence. Data plays a central role in how teams make decisions, optimise performance, and improve customer experience. The environment is collaborative, modern, and focused on long-term impact rather than short-term reporting.


The Role

You will work closely with stakeholders across commercial and operational teams to turn data into actionable insight. This role blends analytics delivery with project leadership, giving you ownership from problem definition through to implementation.


Key responsibilities include:

  • Delivering clear, actionable insights that support data-driven decision-making across the business
  • Leading end-to-end data projects, ensuring timely delivery and alignment with strategic goals
  • Collecting, cleaning, and analysing data from multiple sources
  • Building dashboards, reports, and visualisations for a wide range of stakeholders
  • Partnering with cross-functional teams to define requirements and prioritise initiatives
  • Ensuring data governance, quality, and compliance standards are consistently met
  • Identifying opportunities for automation and process improvement
  • Documenting methodologies and outcomes to support transparency and knowledge sharing


Your Skills and Experience

To succeed in this role, you will bring:

  • Strong commercial experience in data analysis and business intelligence
  • Advanced SQL skills and working proficiency in Python
  • Experience with data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau
  • A solid understanding of data modelling and ETL processes
  • The ability to manage data projects and multiple priorities effectively
  • Strong communication skills, with confidence in data storytelling and stakeholder engagement
  • A structured, analytical mindset with a focus on practical business impact


What They Offer

  • A competitive salary package
  • The chance to work on high-impact, business-critical data initiatives
  • Clear opportunities for career progression within a growing data function
  • A collaborative culture that values quality, ownership, and continuous improvement


How to Apply

If you are a Data Analyst who enjoys combining hands-on analytics with real business impact, apply now to learn more about this opportunity.

Are you passionate about both advanced analytics and empowering others to grow in their analytical capabilities? Do you have the expertise to lead by example while fostering a culture of statistical thinking? If so, we have the perfect role for you!
Channel Security is seeking a Lead Data Analyst to join our team
column2: Fraud Monitoring. In this role, you will not only be a technical expert and a strong individual contributor but also a key leader in advancing the analytics maturity of a team with a business-driven background. You will serve as a pillar for analytics standards and help shape the way we use data to make impactful decisions.

column1: As a Lead Data Analyst within the Fraud Monitoring team, you will play a central role in ensuring that our analysis and detection logic are grounded in solid reasoning and supported by evidence. You will work closely with Fraud Experts (FEs), Data Scientists, and Machine Learning Engineers to strengthen our fraud detection capabilities across payment channels to catch frauds such as phishing, account takeover, lost or stolen cards, and other emerging threats.

Your work focuses on deep-dive analysis, understanding fraud behaviour, evaluating the effectiveness of detection logic, and validating whether proposed changes are analytically sound. You will help the team distinguish clear signals from noise, recognise when evidence is insufficient, and ensure our decisions are based on robust data rather than anecdotal cases.
This is a hands-on analytical role with significant influence: your insights and your methodological judgment will directly shape how we detect fraud and protect our customers.
Perform in-depth analyses to understand fraud patterns, attack vectors, seasonality, behavioural shifts, and detection performance across payment channels.
Evaluate the strength of proposed detection logic (features, thresholds, rules) and identify when ideas are overly narrow, unsupported by evidence, or prone to false positives.
Assess the value and reliability of data fields and signals used in fraud detection.
Help ensure our reasoning, conclusions, and decisions are analytically sound and well-supported.
Guide Fraud Experts and junior analysts in applying good analytical practices and critical thinking.
Coach colleagues on interpreting data responsibly, avoiding common analytical pitfalls, and understanding basic statistical concepts such as variance, sample bias, and signal strength.
Promote a culture of analytical rigor — ensuring the team adopts more mature, data-driven ways of working.
Partner with Data Scientists to transform analytical insights into hypotheses, experiments, and feature ideas for model development.
Act as a bridge between domain experts and technical experts, ensuring clear understanding on both sides.
Identify opportunities to improve how we measure fraud impact, effectiveness of controls, and operational efficiency.
Drive improvements in how we use data in the team — from investigation workflows to decision support tools — focusing on quality, clarity, and insight rather than dashboard volume.

Grid Channel Security within Corporate Information Security Office (CISO) is responsible for the prevention and detection of payment fraud, and deciding the policy and strategy in the area of fraud. The Fraud Monitoring team that you are going to be part of, is a motivated multidisciplinary group of approximately 25 colleagues working with a focus on protecting customers. We work in a hybrid way and our day to day work is organized by the agile and scrum principles. CISO is recognised as having an informal work atmosphere where everyone has a heart for their job and is prepared to help others.
A master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or similar).

At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role involving hands-on data exploration, problem solving, and interpretation (e.g., data analyst, fraud analyst, risk analyst, business analyst with strong technical skills, data scientists in analytical track).
Strong SQL skills for exploratory analysis and investigation.
Hands-on experience in Python for data analysis.
PySpark or experience working with large datasets.
Basic familiarity with visualization tools for communication — not for dashboard engineering.
Ability to frame analytical questions, interpret results correctly, and identify flawed logic or insufficient evidence.
Familiarity with basic statistical concepts (e.g., distributions, variance, seasonality, sampling, simple backtesting principles). Not as a statistician — but as a critical thinker.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
You are an energetic Firestarter, proactively looking for and finding relevant data analysis questions
Strong critical thinking and the confidence to challenge weak assumptions.
Ability to clearly articulate reasoning, uncertainties, and trade-offs.

A teacher and mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration
Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work-life balance
An industry-competitive salary based on a 36 or 40-hour work week
A supplementary benefit budget of 11%, which you can spend on pre-tax fringe benefits
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year
Does this opportunity sound like something you’d be interested in? Please send us your application as soon as possible. We look forward to meeting you.
Please note: This vacancy is a repost of a position previously published on 27 November 2025

  • Strong critical thinking and the confidence to challenge weak assumptions.
  • Ability to clearly articulate reasoning, uncertainties, and trade-offs.
  • A teacher and mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
  • Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration


We are offering

  • Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work-life balance

Assignment description


Are you a data analyst who thrives in ambiguity? Someone who can connect fragmented systems, build a structure where there isn't any, and turn raw HR data into decisions that can move the needle?


A Dutch national infrastructure company is looking for a Business Data Analyst to lead the data-driven transformation of their talent and recruitment function. This is a high-impact role sitting at the intersection of HR, recruitment, and data strategy.


The challenge


Inflow, throughput, and outflow data live alone. ATS and HR systems do not talk to each other. Strategic decisions are being made without trustable insight. You come in and fix that, end to end.


What you will be doing


  • Mapping intern to permanent conversion flows and full employee journeys
  • Building Power BI dashboards that actually get used by leadership
  • Bridging ATS and HR systems and connecting fragmented data sources
  • Defining KPIs, cleaning recruitment data, and setting up structural reporting
  • Advising senior stakeholders and translating insights into concrete action
  • Running ad hoc analyses on labour market and recruitment topics, often via SAS


What we are looking for


  • Experience as a data or business analyst
  • Strong Power BI skills, building dashboards independently rather than just maintaining them
  • Experience with HR and/or recruitment data
  • Comfortable with web or marketing data
  • Process mapping experience in Visio or a similar tool


Why this role?


This is not a dashboard maintenance role. You are laying the analytical foundation for how one of the Netherlands' largest public organizations plans its workforce for the next decade. Real scope. Real impact.


Interested? Drop me a message or apply directly and I would love to tell you more.

Collaborate closely with the commercial teams to translate business questions into analyses, synthesise insights and develop recommendations to drive business results.

Support commercial teams' OKR planning and measurement.

Our Commercial Analytics and Business Intelligence team is formed by 4 analysts from diverse backgrounds and experience levels. We collaborate closely with data engineers, analytics engineers, and data scientists to extract insights and drive informed decision-making.
4+ years experience as a Data Analyst, or equivalent role with a strong data/analytics component.
Analytical mindset, combined with good business sense and orientation.

Previous experience in a high-growth, fast-paced tech company is a big plus.
Experience creating interactive dashboards in Tableau.

Personal equipment, including laptop and ergonomic setup.

Dan is dit jouw kans om aan de start van je carrière impact te maken.

Als Junior Data Analist binnen Team NOVA werk je op het snijvlak van data, techniek en klantcontact. Je helpt klanten en collega’s bij het verzamelen, controleren en voorbereiden van data, zodat analyses en controles goed en efficiënt uitgevoerd kunnen worden. Daarbij krijg je al vroeg verantwoordelijkheid, heb je direct contact met klanten en bouw je mee aan klantspecifieke analyses.

Team NOVA ondersteunt onze accountants en adviseurs bij het verzamelen, valideren en voorbereiden van data voor klantwerkzaamheden. Denk aan het ophalen van administratieve data, het controleren van bestanden op kwaliteit en volledigheid en het gereedmaken van datasets voor analyses.

Daarnaast werkt team NOVA mee aan de ontwikkeling van klantspecifieke analyses. Daarmee lever je niet alleen een bijdrage aan de voorbereiding van werkzaamheden, maar ook aan de verdere ontwikkeling van datagedreven dienstverlening voor onze klanten.

Je werkt in een multidisciplinair team met collega's uit verschillende vakgebieden, van infrastructuur en data tot front-end development. Daarna leer je snel, krijg je begeleiding van verschillende specialisten en zie je van dichtbij hoe techniek en praktijk samenkomen in echte klantoplossingen.
Een afgeronde wo-opleiding in een data-gerelateerde richting
Een nauwkeurige en gestructureerde manier van werken
Je maakt nieuwe systemen en processen snel eigen
Een proactieve houding en goede communicatieve vaardigheden
De motivatie om jezelf te blijven ontwikkelen
Een uitstekende beheersing van het Nederlands en goede beheersing van het Engels
Interesse in accountancy, samenstel of fiscaliteit
Een salaris vanaf €3200 op basis van 40-urige werkweek
28 vakantiedagen op basis van full time dienstverband

Bij Londen & Van Holland staat een gezonde work-life balance centraal. We bieden flexibele werktijden, zowel bij het begin als het einde van de dag, om aan individuele behoeften te voldoen. Erkenning van prestaties en het delen van succes dragen bij aan een positieve werkomgeving. Bovendien streven we naar een beleid waarin iedereen de ruimte en vrijheid krijgt om je werk naar vrijheid kan invullen zodat je succesvol kunt zijn in zowel je professionele als persoonlijke leven.

Binnen Londen & Van Holland staan opleiding, begeleiding en ontwikkeling centraal. Je werkt samen met ervaren collega's die je helpen om het vak en onze manier van werken goed te leren kennen. Je krijgt de ruimte om te leren, vragen te stellen en jezelf stap voor stap verder te ontwikkelen.

Voor deze functie zijn er bovendien volop doorgroeimogelijkheden. Afhankelijk van jouw interesses en talenten kun je je verder ontwikkelen richting data-analyse, klantgerichte projecten of een meer technische rol binnen het team.

4405640199,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-vertom-group-4405640199?position=21&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=NqdysoUaKn4X49aqO%2FTN2A%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Vertom Group,"Rhoon, South Holland, Netherlands","Krijg jij energie van het omzetten van ruwe data in inzichten die impact hebben op beslissingen? Haal je energie uit het vertalen van inzichten naar concrete verbeteringen, begrijp je de vraag achter de vraag en bouw je oplossingen die het werk van je collega’s efficiënter maken?

Binnen Vertom zijn we bezig met het opzetten van een BI & Analytics‑team om onze organisatie te helpen de juiste stappen te maken door middel van data. Als Data-analist werk je hands‑on met dashboards, analyses en automatisering. Je werkt nauw samen met onze verschillende divisies om data op een efficiënte manier voor hen te laten werken.
Je krijgt de ruimte om te groeien, te experimenteren en mee vorm te geven aan de manier waarop data binnen het bedrijf wordt ingezet, met zichtbare impact vanaf de eerste dag.
Ben je op zoek naar een dynamische functie waarin technische en sociale aspecten samenkomen en waarin je écht het verschil kan maken? Dan eindigt je zoektocht hier.
In deze rol ben je verantwoordelijk voor de volgende taken en verantwoordelijkheden:
Ontwerpen, bouwen en onderhouden van dashboards en rapportages (voornamelijk in Power BI) ter ondersteuning van commerciële, operationele en financiële besluitvorming.
Vertalen van businessvragen naar data-oplossingen door nauw samen te werken met stakeholders en deze om te zetten in heldere datamodellen en inzichten.
Analyseren van data om trends en verbetermogelijkheden te identificeren en deze overtuigend te presenteren aan de business.
Afhandelen en prioriteren van ad-hoc data- en rapportageverzoeken.
Verbeteren en automatiseren van rapportage- en dataprocessen (bijvoorbeeld via Power Automate) om efficiëntie te verhogen.
Ondersteunen van de adoptie van data-oplossingen door dashboards toe te lichten, gebruikers te begeleiden en documentatie op orde te houden.
Bijdragen aan datakwaliteit en dataintegraties tussen systemen, en meewerken aan verdere ontwikkeling van analytics (bijvoorbeeld pilots of voorspellende toepassingen).
Wat je van ons kan verwachten:

Binnen de Vertom Groep worden medewerkers als belangrijkste kapitaal gezien. De Vertom Groep investeert daarom graag in een positieve werkomgeving, waarbij betrokkenheid van medewerkers wordt gemotiveerd en gestimuleerd.
Wij bieden een uitdagende functie met doorgroeimogelijkheden en prima primaire en secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden binnen een groeiende, internationale en dynamische organisatie.
Bachelor- of masterdiploma in IT, Data, Business IT, Economie, Engineering of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
Vloeiend Nederlands en comfortabel met Engels
Ruime ervaring met Power BI (datamodellering, DAX, visualisaties)
Goede kennis van datastructuren en ETL‑concepten
Ervaring met of interesse in automatisering (Power Automate of vergelijkbaar)
In staat om duidelijk te communiceren met business stakeholders
Ervaring in logistiek of affiniteit met de maritieme sector
Interesse in geavanceerde analytics of voorspellende modellen

  • Ondersteunen van de adoptie van data-oplossingen door dashboards toe te lichten, gebruikers te begeleiden en documentatie op orde te houden.
  • Bijdragen aan datakwaliteit en dataintegraties tussen systemen, en meewerken aan verdere ontwikkeling van analytics (bijvoorbeeld pilots of voorspellende toepassingen).


Wat je van ons kan verwachten:

Binnen de Vertom Groep worden medewerkers als belangrijkste kapitaal gezien. De Vertom Groep investeert daarom graag in een positieve werkomgeving, waarbij betrokkenheid van medewerkers wordt gemotiveerd en gestimuleerd.

Wij bieden een uitdagende functie met doorgroeimogelijkheden en prima primaire en secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden binnen een groeiende, internationale en dynamische organisatie.


Wat verwachten wij van jou:

  • Bachelor- of masterdiploma in IT, Data, Business IT, Economie, Engineering of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
  • Vloeiend Nederlands en comfortabel met Engels
  • Ruime ervaring met Power BI (datamodellering, DAX, visualisaties)
  • Ruime ervaring met SQL
  • Goede kennis van datastructuren en ETL‑concepten
  • Ervaring met of interesse in automatisering (Power Automate of vergelijkbaar)
  • Sterke analytische en probleemoplossende vaardigheden
  • In staat om duidelijk te communiceren met business stakeholders
  • Proactief, nieuwsgierig en leergierig


Pré:

  • Ervaring in logistiek of affiniteit met de maritieme sector
  • Ervaring met systeemintegraties (API’s)
  • Interesse in geavanceerde analytics of voorspellende modellen

Als Data Analist bij EIFFEL haal je het maximale uit data en breng je teams in beweging met jouw heldere analyses. Je vertaalt complexe gegevens naar inzichten die direct toepasbaar zijn voor de business. Je werkt in een team van data enthousiastelingen waar kennisdeling, groei en experimenteren centraal staan, en waar jij de ruimte krijgt om jezelf te ontwikkelen in de richting die bij je past.

Als Data Analist Vertaal Je Ruwe Data Naar Inzichten Waar Organisaties Direct Mee Aan De Slag Kunnen. Je Ontdekt Trends Die Anderen Niet Zien, Maakt Informatie Toegankelijk En Ondersteunt Teams Bij Het Nemen Van Betere, Datagedreven Beslissingen. Je Werkt Aan Projecten Bij Verschillende Organisaties En Bouwt Dashboards Die Écht Impact Maken. Dit Ga Je Doen
Analyseren van grote datasets en het ontdekken van trends, patronen en kansen.
Ontwikkelen en onderhouden van dashboards en rapportages met tools zoals Power BI.
Ondersteunen van klanten bij het invoeren van datagedreven besluitvorming.
Optimaliseren en structureren van data zodat deze makkelijker te gebruiken is voor analyses.
Adviseren over en verbeteren van dataprocessen en datastrategieën om duurzame resultaten te behalen.
Je bent fit for the job als je...

Je hebt een sterke analytische blik en duikt graag in complexe puzzels. Je maakt data begrijpelijk voor iedereen, van specialisten tot niet technische stakeholders, en schakelt moeiteloos tussen analyse, visualisatie en advies. Met jouw communicatieve vaardigheden verbind je teams en zorg je dat data écht gebruikt wordt in de organisatie.
Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring als data analist, business intelligence specialist of in een vergelijkbare rol.
Ervaring met Python, R, SQL en BI tools zoals Power BI
column2: daarnaast heb je een rijbewijs B.

Groot in interim capaciteit en sterk in consultancy, dat is EIFFEL. We versterken organisaties precies waar dat nodig is, of het nu gaat om een bank, gemeente of ziekenhuis. Dat doen we door slimmer te werken, ideeën van papier naar praktijk te brengen en de juiste mensen op de juiste plek te zetten. Voor grote transformaties én snelle oplossingen. Van start up tot multinational: als professional bij EIFFEL kom je overal binnen.

Binnen de vertical Technology werken we in drie focusgebieden: Data & Analytics, IT en GEOS. Jij maakt onderdeel uit van Team Data & Analytics, samen met zo’n 35 professionals binnen een vertical van 100 specialisten. Onze cultuur is business casual: hard werken, veel humor en volop ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
Het mooie van Technology? We werken niet sectorgebonden. IT en data raken elke sector, daardoor vind je bij ons altijd een project waar jouw kennis, ideeën en energie perfect tot hun recht komen.

Op vrijdag werken we samen in ons clubhuis in Arnhem. Deze dag staat in het teken van kennisdeling, sparren met collega’s en het versterken van de verbinding met Team EIFFEL. Verwacht een opdrachtgever je die dag op locatie? Dan gaat dat natuurlijk voor.

EIFFEL is onderdeel van Team EIFFEL: een community van 3.000+ experts, verspreid over 24 kennisdomeinen en actief bij 600+ opdrachtgevers. Groot in schaal, maar persoonlijk in aanpak. Ons clubhuis staat open voor iedereen die zichzelf wil blijven ontwikkelen en voor wil blijven.

Je duikt in data en komt boven met verhalen die iedereen begrijpt. Met jouw talent voor visualisatie maak je ingewikkelde concepten helder en toegankelijk. Je luistert goed naar de vraag achter de vraag en blijft nieuwsgierig doorvragen tot je de kern te pakken hebt. Je communiceert zonder jargon, brengt gegevens tot leven en helpt anderen écht begrijpen wat de data zegt.

I am currently seeking for a Marketing Data Analyst to join a dynamic team and drive impactful marketing decisions through analytics, audience insights, and data-driven strategy.
This is a role where you will have a big impact within a global organisation, you will be resposible for a number of different Marketing and innovative projects.
Dutch speaking is required for this role.
Develop and maintain Marketing Mix Models (MMM) to measure the effectiveness of campaigns and optimize media spend.
Support the design and execution of marketing strategies, using data to inform channel allocation, messaging, and creative decisions.
Conduct audience segmentation to identify high-value customer groups and uncover opportunities for growth.
Implement audience targeting strategies, ensuring campaigns reach the right people at the right time.
Analyze campaign performance and provide actionable insights to improve ROI and overall marketing effectiveness.
Collaborate closely with marketing, product, and analytics teams to deliver integrated, data-driven solutions.
Proven experience in marketing analytics, preferably with exposure to Marketing Mix Modelling.
Strong knowledge of audience segmentation, targeting, and personalization techniques.
Proficiency in data analysis tools (e.g., SQL, Python, R, Excel, Tableau/Power BI).
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.
Opportunity to shape marketing strategy with data at the core.
Career development in a fast-growing, innovative company.
If you’re ready to leverage data to drive smarter marketing decisions and deliver measurable impact, we want to hear from you!

Eindhoven, The Netherlands


I am currently seeking for a Marketing Data Analyst to join a dynamic team and drive impactful marketing decisions through analytics, audience insights, and data-driven strategy.

4403602573,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analist-bi-specialist-at-cimsolutions-4403602573?position=27&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=bpYYM%2FL%2FDLWkMevmzJ5HDA%3D%3D,Data Analist / BI Specialist,CIMSOLUTIONS,"North Holland, Netherlands","Als Data Analist / BI Specialist zorg jij ervoor dat organisaties hun data omzetten in waardevolle inzichten. Je analyseert complexe datasets, vertaalt deze naar heldere rapportages en dashboards en adviseert stakeholders over datagedreven beslissingen. Met moderne BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik maak je informatie toegankelijk en begrijpelijk. Daarmee help je onze klanten – zoals de Belastingdienst, Gemeente Den Haag, Centraal Justitieel Incassobureau en VGZ – om hun beleid en processen te verbeteren en toekomstgericht te sturen.
Waarom past deze vacature bij jou?

Je hebt een passie voor data en ziet het als jouw missie om cijfers en informatie te vertalen naar verhalen die impact maken. Je bent analytisch sterk, communicatief vaardig en kunt schakelen tussen business en techniek. Je vindt het leuk om samen te werken met collega’s en stakeholders en voelt je thuis in een omgeving waar data centraal staat.
Wat kan je van ons verwachten?

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS krijg je de kans om te werken aan uitdagende en innovatieve data- en BI-projecten bij toonaangevende organisaties. Wij investeren in jouw ontwikkeling en bieden naast een inspirerende werkomgeving:
Een vast dienstverband voor onbepaalde tijd, een max. salaris van € 6.000, bonusregeling, leaseauto, pensioenregeling en maandelijkse onkostenvergoeding
Opdrachtkeuzevrijheid: Jij bepaalt in overleg waar je aan werkt, met de begeleiding van een personal coach
Per jaar € 5.000,- opleidingsbudget en daarnaast ook nog eens 5-10 dagen studietijd
Kennissessies met vakgenoten en inhoudelijke events
Minimaal 25 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om extra dagen op te bouwen of te verkopen
Sociale events zoals sportdagen, weekend weg en gezellige borrels
Een vestiging bij jou in de buurt.

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS geloven we dat leren de sleutel is tot succes. Learn, create and make it work is niet alleen onze slogan, maar zit in ons DNA. Daarom investeren we continu in jouw groei. Je krijgt de ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen tot bijvoorbeeld Senior Data Analist, BI Architect of Data Platform Specialist. Je wordt begeleid door ervaren collega’s, krijgt toegang tot kennisplatforms en blijft altijd scherp op de nieuwste ontwikkelingen binnen jouw vakgebied.

CIMSOLUTIONS heeft een actief diversiteitsbeleid en streeft naar een evenwichtige verdeling van professionals naar onder andere leeftijd, gender, etnische achtergrond en cultuur, arbeidsvermogen, kennis en competenties. Daarbij benadrukken wij dat gelijke kansen voor iedereen een belangrijk uitgangspunt zijn in onze selectie.
Afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding in Bedrijfskunde, Econometrie, Data Science, Business Intelligence of vergelijkbaar
Minimaal 4 jaar ervaring als Data Analist of BI Specialist
Ervaring met BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik
Sterke kennis van SQL en ervaring met het analyseren van grote datasets
Affiniteit met datavisualisatie, datakwaliteit en rapportage
Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal.

4412120193,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/external-reporting-analyst-at-michael-bailey-associates-4412120193?position=28&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=W%2FWX969Zf%2Fy1OjjLX47GEw%3D%3D,External Reporting Analyst,Michael Bailey Associates,"Utrecht, Netherlands","🚨 Reporting Analyst | External Reporting | Utrecht / Hybrid 🚨
We are currently supporting a leading banking organization in the search for an experienced Reporting Analyst to join an External Reporting team.

In this role, you will operate at the intersection of regulatory reporting, data analysis, stakeholder management, and resolution reporting. You will play a key role in delivering high-quality resolution disclosures towards regulators including the Single Resolution Board (SRB), with a strong focus on data quality, analysis, and continuous process improvement.
You will work in a highly analytical and data-driven environment, collaborating closely with stakeholders across Reporting, Risk, Control, Finance, and Data Foundation teams.
Collect, validate, and analyze complex datasets related to resolution reporting
Investigate and resolve data quality issues and inconsistencies
Prepare Valuation Data Sets and analyze movements within reporting figures
Create reporting outputs and management summaries
Align with stakeholders on requirements and documentation across 30+ resolution subject areas
Support the setup and maintenance of Valuation Playbooks, governance frameworks, and self-assessments
Interpret new regulatory developments and translate them into reporting solutions
Contribute to automation initiatives and process improvements within reporting.
Minimum of 5 years’ experience as a Data Analyst or Reporting Analyst
Hands-on experience with SQL and reporting environments
Experience within regulatory reporting is highly preferred
Strong affinity with data quality and data governance
Proactive mindset with strong stakeholder management skills
Professional proficiency in English, both written and spoken.
Candidates with experience within banking, finance, or regulatory environments
Professionals who enjoy working with complex data challenges
Individuals who take ownership and proactively improve processes
Analytical team players with a hands-on mentality.

4413029836,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-bestseller-4413029836?position=29&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=B29omB%2Fv5LFCXmHdMhLgZA%3D%3D,Data Analyst,BESTSELLER,"Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands","At BESTSELLER, we believe better decisions start with better data. And better data comes from people who know how to turn complexity into clarity.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our international E-commerce team in Amsterdam. Someone who enjoys digging into data, building solid foundations, and helping teams make smarter decisions every day.
If you like connecting business questions with data solutions and want to work where fashion meets tech, keep reading.

From raw data to real impact, you’ll own the full journey.
At BESTSELLER, we believe in freedom with responsibility. You’ll have the space to shape your work, take ownership, and grow your skills along the way.
Partner with stakeholders to turn business questions into clear analytical problems
Build and maintain data models that teams can trust and use daily
Transform raw data into actionable insights
Connect the dots between systems, teams, and data sources
Own technical requirements and keep documentation clear and structured
Help teams make better, faster decisions through data
You don’t need to know everything, but you do need curiosity, structure, and a strong sense of ownership.
3–5 years’ experience in a Data Analyst or similar role
Experience with modern data tools like Snowflake, dbt, and Airflow
Strong skills in data visualisation (Power BI or similar)

Exposure to AI tools in data workflows
You’ll join a diverse, international team working across brands like VERO MODA, ONLY, SELECTED, and JACK & JONES all supported from our Amsterdam hub.

Because here, you can own your work. You’ll have the freedom to explore, improve, and build while being supported by a team that values trust, feedback, and growth.
You’ll work in a fast-moving environment where data has real impact, not just dashboards, but decisions.
Hybrid working model and flexible hours
Collective health insurance or a monthly allowance
Plenty of learning and development opportunities
Please note that for this role, you need to be eligible to work in the EU.
We are BESTSELLER. And together, we own it.

At BESTSELLER, we are more than 25,000 people working for over 25 fashion brands such as JACK & JONES, SELECTED, VERO MODA, ONLY, PIECES, VILA, OBJECT, MAMALICIOUS, NAME IT, NOISY MAY and Y.A.S. At BESTSELLER E-Commerce, we are an international team of 170 people from more than 40 nationalities with the purpose to drive all e-commerce functions at BESTSELLER. With our office in the heart of Amsterdam as well as locations in Poland and Denmark, we are supporting all fashion brands of the BESTSELLER family and sell online in 15 countries around the world. Challenging ourselves to offer the best experience to our customers and stakeholders and to work with the latest technologies is what drives us. As a team, we value autonomy and flexibility. At BESTSELLER, we believe better decisions start with better data. And better data comes from people who know how to turn complexity into clarity.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our international E-commerce team in Amsterdam. Someone who enjoys digging into data, building solid foundations, and helping teams make smarter decisions every day.

If you like connecting business questions with data solutions and want to work where fashion meets tech, keep reading.

What’s the role about?

As a Data Analyst, you’ll sit right in the middle of business and tech. You’ll work with teams across Marketplaces, Logistics, and Finance, helping them understand what’s happening, why it’s happening, and what to do next.

From raw data to real impact, you’ll own the full journey.

At BESTSELLER, we believe in freedom with responsibility. You’ll have the space to shape your work, take ownership, and grow your skills along the way.

What you’ll be working on

  • Partner with stakeholders to turn business questions into clear analytical problems
  • Build and maintain data models that teams can trust and use daily
  • Transform raw data into actionable insights
  • Connect the dots between systems, teams, and data sources
  • Own technical requirements and keep documentation clear and structured
  • Help teams make better, faster decisions through data

What you bring

You don’t need to know everything, but you do need curiosity, structure, and a strong sense of ownership.

You’ll likely bring:

  • 3–5 years’ experience in a Data Analyst or similar role
  • Experience with modern data tools like Snowflake, dbt, and Airflow
  • Strong skills in data visualisation (Power BI or similar)
  • The ability to explain complex topics in a clear, simple way
  • A structured mindset because you like building things that last
  • A proactive attitude: you take ownership and don’t wait to be told what to do

Nice to have:

  • Experience with Python or scripting
  • Exposure to AI tools in data workflows
  • Knowledge of e-commerce business models

Your team

You’ll join a diverse, international team working across brands like VERO MODA, ONLY, SELECTED, and JACK &
column2:
JONES all supported from our Amsterdam hub.

We collaborate closely, challenge each other, and move fast, always with the goal of building better solutions together.

Why BESTSELLER?

Because here, you can own your work. You’ll have the freedom to explore, improve, and build while being supported by a team that values trust, feedback, and growth.

You’ll work in a fast-moving environment where data has real impact, not just dashboards, but decisions.

What you’ll get

  • Hybrid working model and flexible hours
  • 27 holidays + option to buy 5 more
  • 50% discount on all BESTSELLER brands
  • Travel allowance
  • Collective health insurance or a monthly allowance
  • Access to OpenUp (wellbeing support)
  • Daily lunch at the office
  • Plenty of learning and development opportunities

Please note that for this role, you need to be eligible to work in the EU.

We are BESTSELLER. And together, we own it.

column3: JONES, SELECTED, VERO MODA, ONLY, PIECES, VILA, OBJECT, MAMALICIOUS, NAME IT, NOISY MAY and Y.A.S. 4405230747,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-demand-planning-at-tesla-4405230747?position=30&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=f159pWI5ABGF83g0hzc8Jw%3D%3D,"Data Analyst, Demand Planning",Tesla,"Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands","What To Expect

The Data Analyst – Demand Planning, EMEA Service Parts role is responsible for Analytics to support the Demand Planning for Service Parts in EMEA. The role will be in charge of performing analytical deep dives, building new data models and generating insights to support strategic decision making for the team. The role will be a link between the Demand Planners and Data Scientists in the team and will work with a wide range of stakeholders from Material and Inventory Planning to Parts and Service teams.

The role requires solid technical skills and the ability to bridge the gap between business requirements and a complex data landscape. Additionally, a successful candidate needs to be able to communicate insights clearly to a wide audience.
Perform analyses and propose solutions to improve the overall quality of our forecasts
Build KPIs and data models to support the analytical requests of our stakeholders and automate existing workflows where possible
Collaborate with stakeholders from Material/Inventory Planning, NPI, Reliability Engineering and Parts and Service teams
Communicate insights clearly to a wide audience including senior leadership
Degree in an analytical area such as Operations Research, Statistics or Data Science
Continuous improvement & growth mindset. Strong intellectual curiosity, and ability to structurally analyze problems and propose data-driven solutions
Fluent in written and spoken English

What To Expect

The role will be a link between the Demand Planners and Data Scientists in the team and will work with a wide range of stakeholders from Material and Inventory Planning to Parts and Service teams.

The role requires solid technical skills and the ability to bridge the gap between business requirements and a complex data landscape. Additionally, a successful candidate needs to be able to communicate insights clearly to a wide audience.

  • Build KPIs and data models to support the analytical requests of our stakeholders and automate existing workflows where possible
  • Collaborate with stakeholders from Material/Inventory Planning, NPI, Reliability Engineering and Parts and Service teams
  • Communicate insights clearly to a wide audience including senior leadership

  • Proficiency in SQL and Python
  • Continuous improvement &

4412917126,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analist-at-ista-nederland-b-v-4412917126?position=33&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=EZlpBsHt8DW66eSIJDrL9A%3D%3D,Data-analist,ista Nederland B.V.,"Schiedam, South Holland, Netherlands","Bij ista Nederland bevinden we ons midden in een fundamentele transformatie. De komende jaren zetten we grote stappen in digitalisering, datagedreven werken en de verdere professionalisering van onze organisatie. Data speelt hierin een steeds belangrijkere rol.

Binnen het team Business Insights werken we eraan om data toegankelijker, begrijpelijker en relevanter te maken voor de hele organisatie. Om deze ontwikkeling te versnellen, zoeken wij een Data-Analist die data weet te vertalen naar concrete inzichten en hiermee bijdraagt aan betere besluitvorming binnen het Management Team en de organisatie.
In deze rol werk je samen met een klein en ervaren team, waarin je de ruimte krijgt om te leren, impact te maken en actief bij te dragen aan de verdere ontwikkeling van data binnen ista.

Wij geven gebouwen een toekomst. Dat doen we met producten en diensten die helpen de CO₂-uitstoot van gebouwen te verminderen. We beheren gegevens en processen die ervoor zorgen dat vastgoed duurzamer, efficiënter, veiliger en comfortabeler wordt.

Onze oplossingen zijn digitaal en ondersteunen vastgoedeigenaren, beheerders en bewoners bij de overstap naar duurzame en toekomstbestendige technologieën. Daarbij staat gebruiksgemak centraal: we maken duurzame keuzes toegankelijk en eenvoudig, zodat iedereen kan bijdragen aan de bescherming van het klimaat.

Wereldwijd hebben we meer dan 48 miljoen draadloze apparaten in gebruik en werken ruim 6.600 medewerkers in 21 landen aan energie-efficiënte gebouwen. Onze producten en diensten worden toegepast in meer dan 14 miljoen woningen en commerciële gebouwen bij ruim 470.000 klanten. In 2025 realiseerde de ista Group een omzet van € 1.287 miljoen.

Bij ista Nederland bevinden we ons midden in een fundamentele transformatie. De komende jaren zetten we grote stappen in digitalisering, datagedreven werken en de verdere professionalisering van onze organisatie. Data speelt hierin een steeds belangrijkere rol.


Binnen het team Business Insights werken we eraan om data toegankelijker, begrijpelijker en relevanter te maken voor de hele organisatie. Om deze ontwikkeling te versnellen, zoeken wij een Data-Analist die data weet te vertalen naar concrete inzichten en hiermee bijdraagt aan betere besluitvorming binnen het Management Team en de organisatie.

NS business card or bike lease.

Job Description

At AFS Energy, we are committed to building long-term relationships, upholding high standards, and fostering strong teamwork. As our organization continues to grow, we are looking for a Business Data Analyst who can integrate data from multiple sources (CRM, Finance, Risk, and internal trading system) to support multiple disciplines within the company among others, management, front office, finance and risk, enable data-driven decision making across the business. In this role, you will be part of the Operations Team, while maintaining close collaboration with Front Office, directors and the Management Team.

About AFS Energy

AFS Energy as part of AFS Group is an internationally oriented scale-up headquartered in the heart of Amsterdam. We are active in the fast-evolving world of Renewable Energy, working daily to identify and act on market opportunities. As more organizations commit to reducing emissions, we now serve over 1,450 clients in more than 50 countries. Our diverse team of 175+ professionals from 35+ nationalities reflects our global reach and our continuous growth in the Environmental Commodities market.

Your Role

As a Business Data Analyst, You Will Support Various Front Office Energy Desks And Their Supporting Teams, Including Risk, Finance, And Back Office. Your Focus Will Be To Provide Actionable Insights To Management By

  • Developing interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI and CRM.
  • Periodically reporting on team and company (financial) performance.
  • Supporting the forecasting &
column2:
budgeting process with reliable data.
  • Defining, measuring, and monitoring KPIs.
  • Handling ad-hoc data requests from stakeholders.
  • Possible development &
column3:
CRM improvements (ZOHO).


This is a cross-functional role that provides exposure to strategic decision-making and the opportunity to make a tangible impact across the organization. As a central point of contact for all data-related activities, you will support the Business Management Team in mapping data flows and contributing to the development of new features and databases that shape the organization’s evolving data architecture.

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor's or master's in a relevant field
  • 2+ years of relevant experience
  • Advanced Excel (formulas, pivot tables, data modelling)
  • Hands-on Power BI or similar BI tool experience
  • Able to translate complex business questions into actionable insights
  • CRM, finance or trading platform experience is a plus
  • Strong English communication skills, comfortable in cross-functional teams and stakeholder management
  • Proactive, independent and entrepreneurial mindset


What We Offer

  • A unique office at the Amsterdam Stock Exchange.
  • Performance-based bonus scheme.
  • 27 paid vacation days per year.
  • International events and congresses to expand your network.
  • Training, coaching, and personal development opportunities.
  • NS business card or bike lease.
  • In-house gym, covered by AFS.


Interview process

The process for this position is excising of the following steps:

  • Recruiter interview
  • 2nd interview with team members / stakeholders
  • Final interview team members / stakeholders


Interested?

If you are interested, please submit your resume via the application form. If there are any questions about the role, please don’t hesitate to reach out to snihot@afsgroup.nl

Je hebt een passie voor data en ziet het als jouw missie om cijfers en informatie te vertalen naar verhalen die impact maken. Je vindt het leuk om samen te werken met collega’s en stakeholders en voelt je thuis in een omgeving waar data centraal staat.

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS krijg je de kans om te werken aan uitdagende en innovatieve data- en BI-projecten bij toonaangevende organisaties. Wij investeren in jouw ontwikkeling en bieden naast een inspirerende werkomgeving:

Opdrachtkeuzevrijheid: Jij bepaalt in overleg waar je aan werkt, met de begeleiding van een personal coach
Per jaar € 5.000,- opleidingsbudget en daarnaast ook nog eens 5-10 dagen studietijd
Kennissessies met vakgenoten en inhoudelijke events
Minimaal 25 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om extra dagen op te bouwen of te verkopen
Sociale events zoals sportdagen, weekend weg en gezellige borrels
Een vestiging bij jou in de buurt.

4414171445,https://nl.linkedin.com/jobs/view/global-bi-analytics-internship-at-coty-4414171445?position=40&pageNum=0&refId=kLIkWOBookXXHJ%2BwmAb76g%3D%3D&trackingId=C1D3HHsNpgCuPVsmVqAVuA%3D%3D,Global BI & Analytics Internship,Coty,"Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands","GLOBAL BI & ANALYTICS INTERNSHIP

At Coty, we are seeking motivated Global BI & Analytics Interns to join our team as part of Coty’s Data Talent Factory, a structured 6-month rotation program designed for students eager to build hands-on skills in data, analytics, and generative AI. You’ll spend your first 2 months training with the Global BI team at our HQ in Amsterdam-Zuid, then rotate through 2 business analytics teams (2 months each), applying your skills to real projects across the organization.

We’re Coty, one of the world’s largest beauty companies with an iconic portfolio of brands across fragrance, color cosmetics, and skin and body care. Coty is the global leader in fragrance and number three in color cosmetics. Coty’s products are sold in over 150 countries around the world. Coty and its brands are committed to a range of social causes as well as seeking to minimize its impact on the environment.
About The Business Facing BI Team In Amsterdam

The Business Facing BI team plays a pivotal role in implementing a variety of business intelligence and data analytics solutions to enable data-driven decision-making globally. We actively set and refine Power BI guidelines to ensure scalable, secure, and innovative BI practices across Coty. Although our team is small, we are diverse with members based in Amsterdam and New York City. We embrace continuous learning and rapid adoption of cutting-edge technologies. We are actively exploring how generative AI is reshaping the way we build and deliver analytics. As an intern, you’ll be part of that journey.
At Coty, we offer an exceptional opportunity for our Global BI & Analytics interns to gain hands-on experience in a vibrant and dynamic environment.
Months 1-2: Training in Power BI and Databricks fundamentals, with a focus on Coty’s standards and best practices. Includes exploration of generative AI capabilities within these platforms.
Months 3-4: First project rotation with a business analytics team.
Months 5-6: Second project rotation with a different business analytics team.

By the end of the program, you’ll have a portfolio of real deliverables, cross-functional exposure, and a strong foundation for a career in data & analytics. You will receive direct mentorship from the Business Facing Power BI Manager, fostering both personal and professional growth. Additionally, you’ll benefit from an inclusive, supportive work environment that encourages collaboration and innovation.
Read a testimonial from a former intern about their time with the Global BI & Analytics team:

“My internship with the Global BI & Analytics team was truly unique. It went far beyond dashboards and data, offering a deep dive into strategy, stakeholder management, and the complexities behind scalable BI products. I was exposed to projects across finance, HR, and transformation teams, which helped me understand the company better while building a strong foundation that supports my current role as a Supply Finance Analyst.” – Esmat Fayaz, Supply Finance Analyst
Student (Bachelor/Master), it is important that you remain enrolled with your university.
Demonstrated passion for data and an interest in specializing in Power BI and Databricks.
Strong analytical skills and proficiency with numbers.
Familiarity with data analysis tools such as Excel, Python, SQL, and R is highly advantageous.
Strong interest in generative AI and its practical applications in data & analytics.
Adaptability and willingness to work across different teams and business domains.
Keen eye for detail to ensure high-quality, aesthetically pleasing outputs.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.
Great opportunities to work across multiple analytics teams and manage end-to-end projects.
Internship allowance, NS business card (if you were not eligible for a student public transport card), staff discount of Coty products, access to Coty Academy.
Convenient office location (near Amsterdam Zuid station) with lots of fun in-house events.
First-hand knowledge about the beauty industry.

This internship is full-time (40 hours per week), for a duration of 5-6 months and starts from Summer 2026. Remote working is possible up to 50% of your time, in alignment with your line manager. You will receive an internship allowance and NS Business Card if you do not have a student public transport card. The internship can be combined with a thesis but be aware that this will largely have to be done in your own time.
At Coty, we believe in equality and inclusion for all, and stand together with those fighting for freedom, justice, and equal rights regardless of who you are or who you love.

Our people make us who we are. They are the brightest minds bringing the boldest ideas to life every day. If you’re the kind of person who refuses to settle, if you want to think bigger, learn faster and go further than you ever thought possible – we’d love to hear from you.
Please send us your motivation letter together with your CV, stating your availability and for how long.
