Do you want to work at the intersection of product, business, and technology and create real impact through data?


ABOUT US

Toptracer Technologies have users and viewers all over the world. Our mission is to help people enjoy the wonderful game of golf even more by tracing contributions to TV and gamification to ranges. Our range technology is used in 38 countries and the broadcast is aired over 270 times yearly. All of our products are developed, tested and deployed from our R&
column2:
D office in Danderyd, Stockholm.


THE ROLE

In this role, you will work at the intersection of data platform and analytics, helping bridge technical capabilities with real business impact. Our modern data platform is already in place and supported by a team of data engineers. Your focus will be to strengthen the analytical layer, ensuring data is meaningful, reliable, and actionable across the organization.


You will play a central role in shaping how data is used by defining relevant KPIs, supporting decision-making, and making insights accessible to stakeholders across product, sales, finance, and leadership. The role combines hands-on data work with a business focus, where you are expected not only to respond to needs but also to identify opportunities and improvements proactively.


You will work closely with product leadership on key metrics, support financial and business follow-ups, and contribute to making data more accessible, secure, and performant across the organization.


TECH STACK

SQL | Power BI | Grafana | Python


POSSIBILITIES

  • Support internal monitoring and reporting across the organization
  • Define and develop KPIs and business metrics that reflect true performance
  • Structure and prepare data so it can be used effectively by multiple stakeholders
  • Identify anomalies and ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability
  • Drive initiatives both proactively and based on business needs
  • Extract, transform, and analyze data using SQL
  • Use Python for data processing and analysis
  • Build dashboards and visualizations (e.g., Power BI, Grafana)
  • Translate business needs into clear analytical outputs
  • Communicate insights through structured presentations and visual storytelling
  • Opportunity to shape how data is used across the organization
  • Work close to the business and see the impact of your work
  • A role that combines analytics, technology, and communication


WE BELIEVE YOU HAVE

  • Strong experience with SQL (e.g., PostgreSQL)
  • Experience using Python for data analysis
  • Experience defining and working with KPIs and business metrics
  • Experience working with analytical datasets, reporting layers, or data warehouses


NICE TO HAVE

  • Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Grafana, or similar
  • Experience building dashboards and monitoring solutions
  • Understanding of data pipelines and data platforms


We believe you are someone who

  • Has a strong analytical mindset and enjoys exploring data from different angles
  • Challenges assumptions and proactively identifies improvements
  • Is communicative and comfortable presenting insights to stakeholders
  • Works in a structured and methodical way
  • Enjoys collaborating closely with both team members and stakeholders
  • Can “hit the ground running” with limited onboarding
  • Are motivated by understanding the bigger picture and connecting data to real business impact.


MORE ABOUT TOPTRACER


Workplace


We value fully engaged associates that come to work because they enjoy their work and the colleagues they have. We strive to create a diverse and inclusive work environment where everybody can reach their full potential. We provide opportunities for all colleagues to develop internally and to meet each other. We have a strong commitment and dedication at Toptracer. That comes from the activities and moments we share together and the product we are developing.


In addition to that, our technology is unique and world-leading which makes the journey extra exciting. We create a product that encourages people to be outside and get activated which enables a healthier lifestyle. As a colleague at Toptracer, you can either go to a range nearby your home to experience the product you have developed or just turn on the TV to see it in the biggest golf tournaments.


Culture

Our culture is driven by our mission to create moments that matter for everyone and our values for Caring, One Team, Fun, Excellence and Edgy Spirit. If you want to learn more about them visit: Core Values | Topgolf.


Activities

  • Morning Fikas
  • After-work
  • Pair/Mob programming
  • Conference
  • Dart
  • Gym sessions
  • Table-tennis
  • Golf simulator
  • Golf tournaments


Benefits

  • Competitive salary and benefits package.
  • Opportunity to work on cutting-edge technologies and projects.
  • Collaborative and innovative work environment.
  • Professional development and growth opportunities.
  • A hybrid work environment with the primary workplace at our cozy and welcoming office in Mörby Centrum, Danderyd.
  • And of course, play golf!


The position is permanent and full-time, located at our office in Danderyd. Hit the link at the bottom of the page to read more about the team. We hope to hear from you soon!

Are you a talented digital analyst seeking an exciting Nordic opportunity? In this role, you'll have the opportunity to use and develop your expertise within digital sales and marketing analytics which will have a strong impact on our business in the Nordics. If you have a data and insights driven mindset with strong communication skills and a proactive approach to digital analysis, then you should apply for this position.
Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.

As a Digital Analyst, you will develop and support the business by providing insights into the success of digital sales and marketing, and play a crucial role in the unfolding of our ambitious digital customer acquisition strategy. In this role, you will work closely with your team and internal stakeholders in Finland, Denmark, Norway and Sweden. Your responsibilities will include analysing and optimizing digital traffic, journeys, sales and behaviour as well as providing reporting to support the business. Experience with Adobe Analytics and/or Adobe Journey Optimizer and your passion for utilizing analytics to develop the business will ensure your success in this role.

The Nordic Digital Customer Acquisition team has team members in all Nordic locations such as Helsinki, Copenhagen, Stockholm and Oslo, and therefore you can have your workplace in any of these locations.
Analysis and optimization of traffic, conversions, customer journeys and sales
Work together with content managers as well as Marketing automation specialists to optimise the customer journeys
Develop and maintain reporting to stakeholders and management
Data management and analysis to support the business
Proficiency in Adobe Analytics, Excel and PowerPoint.
Knowledge of Adobe Customer Journey Analytics.
Excellent in communicating, visualising and presenting your results and analytics assignments
Bachelor’s or Master´s degree in a relevant field
Fluent in both written and spoken English.
It would be ideal if you also:
A team player who can contribute with ideas, feedback and implement new ideas
A Nordic role where you work with our Business Banking digital activities in Finland, Denmark, Sweden and Norway
Develop and set the direction for our analytics, reporting and Adobe Analytics and Adobe Customer Journey Analytics usage
A supportive and friendly team environment where we focus on collaboration and job-satisfaction

People come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.

A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.

Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
If this sounds like you, get in touch!
Submit your application no later than 21/05/2026. Please notice that we might start interviews already during the application period.
For more information, you’re welcome to contact Stefan Brieghel on stefan.brieghel@nordea.com.
We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.
We build relationships . We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.

We learn and develop. We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.

We lead change . We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.

We are Nordea . We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
Only for candidates in Finland: A security clearance will be performed for the person selected for this position.
Only for candidates in Sweden: For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com .

To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.

We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.

Are you passionate about leveraging data to drive business impact and take analytics to the next level? Join Mentimeter as our Data Analyst, where you will set new standards for our analytics craft within the Data & Analytics (DNA) team and play a pivotal role in advancing our analytics capabilities to drive impactful, data-informed strategies for our SaaS-based business. Data is not only central, but crucial, to how Mentimeter will reach success.

At Mentimeter, the Data & Analytics team is on a mission to catalyse our company strategy and drive innovation, growth and impact throughout our company and user journey by using data. We work at the intersection of analytics, data science, and business to deliver actionable insights and solutions that enable better decisions across departments, including Product, Marketing, Sales, and Revenue Operations. Your contributions will directly influence key business outcomes such as customer retention, engagement, and expansion.

In this role, you’ll collaborate with and be supported by cross-functional teams of data scientists, analytics engineers, user researchers, product managers and revenue leaders who are passionate about Mentimeter’s mission and using data to achieve it.
Partner with senior leaders and stakeholders to define key questions, success metrics, and analytical approaches that enable strategic decisions.
Deliver high-impact analyses and decision support that connect insights across our functions and company.
Be a key part of our Analytics team to align priorities and ensure solutions are relevant and impactful.
Collaborate closely with Analytics Engineering and other close teams to enable the business to proactively act on data and create business impact.
Elevate the analytics craft by setting new standards, implementing innovative approaches and best practices.
Act as a trusted thought partner and mentor to analysts and stakeholders alike, raising analytical maturity across the organization.
Translate complex business questions and analytical findings into compelling narratives and visualizations that resonate with diverse audiences.
A minimum of 5 years of relevant work experience.
Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to convey complex concepts through clear narratives and visualizations.
Strong business acumen and experience applying analytics within a domain such as product, sales or marketing.
Proven expertise in analytics and data exploration, with proficiency in tools such as SQL and BI/visualization platforms.
A builder mindset and interest to create analysis and systems that scale, leveraging automation, generative AI, and data to drive Mentimeter’s growth and efficiency.
Comfort working with analytics engineering practices and collaborating with data engineers on data models and pipelines.
A track record of delivering high-quality, actionable insights aligned with business objectives.
Experience partnering closely with senior stakeholders and leadership teams.
Familiarity with modern data tools (e.g. Looker, Hex, Snowflake).
Experience applying AI in analytical workflows, such as setting up AI agents or automation to accelerate analysis, insight generation, and decision support.
Experience working within a SaaS environment, with a strong understanding of the unique business challenges and opportunities it presents.
Swedish is not required (as daily work is carried out in English and the Mentimeter team currently boasts 50+ different nationalities)

At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.
All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page

At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.
AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.

AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.

At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.
Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.
Review our Privacy Policy for more information.

4412645469,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-bi-temporary-position-at-h-m-group-4412645469?position=8&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=%2FUHlybo1R2JgQT2U3YpdqQ%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst - BI (Temporary Position),H&M Group,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Job Description

As a Business Intelligence Analyst within Central Business Analytics at H&M Group, you will play a key role in transforming data into accessible, impactful insights for decision-makers across the organization. Working close to the core business, you will help shape and scale H&M’s analytical toolkit by building intuitive, reliable, and business‑relevant BI solutions that empower thousands of users globally.

In this role, you combine strong technical BI skills with a genuine curiosity for the business. You will collaborate closely with stakeholders across functions to understand their analytical needs and translate them into clear, actionable reporting and datasets that support better, faster decisions.
To succeed in this role, you will…​
Build, maintain, and continuously improve reports, data models, and BI solutions using the Microsoft Power BI platform.
Work closely with business stakeholders to understand their questions, challenges, and priorities, ensuring insights delivered have real business impact.
Transform large and complex datasets into clear, user‑friendly visualizations and data stories.
Collaborate with IT and data platform teams to ensure the data environment supports scalable and reliable BI solutions.
Contribute to best practices, knowledge sharing, and capability building within the BI and data community.

Business Analytics is a central unit working closely with Group Controlling, Strategy, and Business Development functions. Our mission is to deliver relevant and reliable decision support that accelerates H&M’s strategic plans and improves our decision making. Our function plays a key role in enabling fast, data-driven, and trustworthy insights that support H&M Group’s top priorities.
We are looking for people with…​
A degree in a quantitative field such as engineering, business, economics, or statistics.
At least 5 years of experience in data, insights, or BI roles, in-house or as a consultant.
A genuine interest in creating business value through data and analytics.
Experience working with large datasets and complex analytical problems.
Strong skills in Power BI (or similar tools), including data modelling and visualization.
Good knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
A passion for data visualization and clear data storytelling.
Self‑driven, structured, and pragmatic in their way of working.
Communicative team players who enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders.
Curious and eager to learn, actively following developments in BI and analytics.
Motivated by making insights accessible and useful for others.
Aligned with H&M’s values, with a passion for growth, innovation, and creating customer value.​

H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​
​All our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program (HIP)

All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET.
A dynamic, entrepreneurial environment where your insights directly influence strategic decisions.
A collaborative culture that values innovation, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.

Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.

This is a temporary position, starting as soon as possible according to the agreement. The role is based in our office in Stockholm, Sweden. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.
Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. We are eager to meet you!​
*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

4413293363,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/risk-data-modeller-to-finance-risk-and-compliance-seb-stockholm-at-seb-4413293363?position=9&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=nrvxcTXTobd0AcLGbs9%2B9w%3D%3D,"Risk Data Modeller to Finance, Risk, and Compliance | SEB, Stockholm",SEB,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.

Finance, Risk, and Compliance are undergoing a major transformation—driven by business and regulatory changes as well as significant shifts in architectural design. We are therefore looking for a Risk Data Modeller with experience in data modelling to develop an integrated data architecture that meets regulatory requirements and business needs. You will be a vital part of our community driving the Finance, Risk, and Compliance agenda in the bank. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, this opportunity is for you!

As a Risk Data Modeller in our team, you will take responsibility for the data architecture within the risk area. Your work will involve a high degree of complexity, both from a business and data perspective as well as from a people and process perspective. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and architects to:
Capture and interpret business and regulatory requirements driven by our major initiatives.

Develop an integrated data architecture for Risk that supports all risk types, ensuring data is consistent, integrated, traceable, and fit for regulatory and business use, while connecting to the domain‑wide data architecture.

Develop business information and logical data models that align with business needs and integrate with business and application architectures, supporting metadata capture, consistency, lineage, and traceability across the data landscape.
Support the definition of business terms, and ensure they are correctly represented, consistently used, and traceable across models, data flows, and solutions.
Ensure compliance with BCBS 239, Basel/CRR, IFRS, and other applicable regulatory requirements, and alignment with the domain architecture.
Collaborate with stakeholders from the risk organisation, data management professionals, and architects across Finance, Risk, and Compliance.
To thrive in this role, we believe you have:
Strong professional background in risk management within a banking or financial services (e.g., credit, market, counterparty, or liquidity risk).

Competence in developing ontologies, business information models, and logical data models (including normalised and dimensional models), as well as data definitions, data mapping specifications, and data flows.
Good understanding of data warehouse architectures and methodologies, and familiarity with the Data Mesh framework and data products.
Knowledge of regulatory and data management requirements in Finance and Risk areas, and how data architecture enables compliance and governance.
Experience with BCBS 239 (Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting, RDARR) would be an advantage.
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
Ability to collaborate effectively with a positive, solution‑oriented mindset.
Excellent communication and documentation skills to convey complex ideas effectively to both business and IT professionals.
A key role in one of SEB’s most challenging areas
Innovative working environment with highly motivated colleagues
Agile and modern ways of working
Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
Attractive compensation and access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits
Do you want to be part of SEB?

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than May 30th.

Risk, Quant, Data Architecture, Data Modelling, Conceptual Modelling, Business Information Modelling, Logical Modelling, Data Warehousing, Data Mesh, Data Product, Ontology, Taxonomy, Agile, SAFe

SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.

Finance, Risk, and Compliance are undergoing a major transformation—driven by business and regulatory changes as well as significant shifts in architectural design. We are therefore looking for a Risk Data Modeller with experience in data modelling to develop an integrated data architecture that meets regulatory requirements and business needs. You will be a vital part of our community driving the Finance, Risk, and Compliance agenda in the bank. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, this opportunity is for you!

About the role:

As a Risk Data Modeller in our team, you will take responsibility for the data architecture within the risk area. Your work will involve a high degree of complexity, both from a business and data perspective as well as from a people and process perspective. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and architects to:

  • Capture and interpret business and regulatory requirements driven by our major initiatives.
  • Develop an integrated data architecture for Risk that supports all risk types, ensuring data is consistent, integrated, traceable, and fit for regulatory and business use, while connecting to the domain‑wide data architecture.
  • Develop business information and logical data models that align with business needs and integrate with business and application architectures, supporting metadata capture, consistency, lineage, and traceability across the data landscape.
  • Support the definition of business terms, and ensure they are correctly represented, consistently used, and traceable across models, data flows, and solutions.
  • Ensure compliance with BCBS 239, Basel/CRR, IFRS, and other applicable regulatory requirements, and alignment with the domain architecture.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders from the risk organisation, data management professionals, and architects across Finance, Risk, and Compliance.

To thrive in this role, we believe you have:

  • Strong professional background in risk management within a banking or financial services (e.g., credit, market, counterparty, or liquidity risk).
  • Competence in developing ontologies, business information models, and logical data models (including normalised and dimensional models), as well as data definitions, data mapping specifications, and data flows.
  • Good understanding of data warehouse architectures and methodologies, and familiarity with the Data Mesh framework and data products.
  • Knowledge of regulatory and data management requirements in Finance and Risk areas, and how data architecture enables compliance and governance.
  • Experience with BCBS 239 (Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting, RDARR) would be an advantage.
  • Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with a positive, solution‑oriented mindset.
  • Excellent communication and documentation skills to convey complex ideas effectively to both business and IT professionals.
  • Fluency in English.

What we offer:

  • A key role in one of SEB’s most challenging areas
  • Innovative working environment with highly motivated colleagues
  • Agile and modern ways of working
  • Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
  • Attractive compensation and access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits

Do you want to be part of SEB?

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than May 30th.

Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.

4412645472,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-temporary-position-at-h-m-group-4412645472?position=12&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=57wQ%2F6T%2FhUA5Z%2BCSZRLIqw%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst (Temporary position),H&M Group,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Job Description

As a Senior Data Analyst within Central Business Analytics at H&M Group, you will provide insights and recommendations that shape our company’s direction. Positioned at the heart of the company’s analytical capability, you will take on some of our most complex and impactful problems and opportunities in close collaboration with stakeholders across the organization. In addition to a solid technical contribution, you will be expected to drive strategic initiatives and provide insights that shape H&M’s key priorities.
Work with a wide range of stakeholders within central functions including group Controlling, Strategy, and Business development.
Lead and execute end-to-end analytical projects focused on areas such as retail strategy, store and online growth, customer behaviour, product assortment and store planning.
Translate complex business questions into structured analysis plans and use both internal and external datasets to deliver clear data-driven recommendations to senior stakeholders.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to quickly address urgent business questions and support fast decision making.
Contribute to the development of best practices, analytical frameworks, and ways of working within the Business Analytics team.

Business Analytics is a central unit working closely with Group Controlling, Strategy, and Business Development functions. Our mission is to deliver relevant and reliable decision support that accelerates H&M’s strategic plans and improves our decision making. Our function plays a key role in enabling fast, data-driven, and trustworthy insights that support H&M Group’s top priorities.
We are looking for people with…​
A degree in a quantitative field such as engineering, business, economics, finance, or statistics.
At least 5 years of experience in data, insights, or business analyst roles, either in-house or as a consultant.
A proven ability to lead and deliver end-to-end analytical projects that drive business impact.
Experience translating complex business questions into structured analysis plans and actionable insights.
Experience from working with SQL to extract and transform data from large and complex datasets as well as using R or Python to analyse data further.
Strong communicators who can clearly present findings and influence stakeholders at all levels.​
Business-savvy and quick to interpret data in a broader commercial context.
Proactive and self-driven, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently.
Collaborative team players who enjoy sharing knowledge and building capabilities across teams.
Aligned with H&M’s values, with a passion for growth, innovation, and creating customer value.​

H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​

We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​
​All our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program (HIP)

All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET.
A dynamic, entrepreneurial environment where your insights directly influence strategic decisions.
A collaborative culture that values innovation, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.

Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.

This is a temporary position, starting as soon as possible according to the agreement. The role is based in our office in Stockholm, Sweden. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.
Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. We are eager to meet you!​
*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

WHAT YOU’LL DO​

Trivs du i en miljö som präglas av handlingskraft och samhällsengagemang?

Utbetalningsmyndigheten är en innovativ myndighet under utveckling. Genom att jobba hos oss får du vara med och forma en verksamhet som upptäcker, förhindrar och förebygger felaktiga utbetalningar och välfärdsbrott. Vi erbjuder en arbetsplats där du får stort ansvar och mandat att påverka. Hos oss gör din kompetens och ditt engagemang skillnad.

Inom vår myndighet är arbete med data och analys centralt. Nu söker vi nya medarbetare för att utöka myndighetens förmåga inom dataintag och dataanalys. Rollen som data engineer är placerad på avdelningen data och utveckling.

I rollen som data engineer kommer du att arbeta med utveckling av it-lösningar inom myndigheten med fokus på datahantering, datalagring och dataanalys. Du kommer arbeta i ett tvärfunktionellt utvecklingsteam och samarbeta med olika roller såsom andra data engineers, systemutvecklare, data analysts och DevOps engineers. I rollen är det huvudsakliga målet att utveckla lösningar för datahantering med direkt verksamhetsvärde. Vi arbetar kontinuerligt med att förbättra och effektivisera våra utvecklingsmiljöer samt utforma interna standarder kring kod och livscykelhantering.
Vi söker dig som håller dig uppdaterad inom området och vill bidra utifrån ditt specialistområde. Det här är en roll för dig som trivs i en föränderlig miljö där allt inte är färdigt.
I din roll kommer du behandla känsliga och sekretessreglerade uppgifter vilket ställer höga krav på rätt hantering och höga krav på informationssäkerhet.
Minst tre års aktuell erfarenhet av att arbeta med systemutveckling i Python
Minst tre års erfarenhet att bygga och underhålla stabila data pipelines i olika miljöer
Relevant erfarenhet av arbete med datastrukturer, algoritmer och principer för mjukvaruutveckling
Erfarenhet av SQL och arbete med komplexa datamängder
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med öppna källkodslösningar
DevOps/MLOps erfarenhet och vana av kodhantering i GIT och tillhörande arbetsflöden
God förmåga att kommunicera på svenska
Det är meriterande om du även har
Erfarenhet av arbete med on-premises system

Den statliga värdegrunden sammanfattar och synliggör de viktigaste kraven som gäller för statsanställda, att arbeta i staten innebär att följa den. Vi ställer även höga krav på säkerhetsmedvetenhet.

Tjänstgöringsorten är i Stockholm. Myndigheten finns i moderna lokaler i närheten av Gullmarsplan. Säkerhetsprövning kan bli aktuellt. Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid. Sex månaders provanställning kan bli aktuellt.
Vi strävar efter att alla medarbetare ska ha en hållbar arbetsmiljö och balans mellan jobb och fritid. Vi erbjuder många förmåner för dig som anställd.

We're supporting a major data initiative for a leading international business and looking for an experienced Data Modeller on a freelance basis to help shape next‑generation data products across a modern cloud environment.


What you’ll be doing:

  • Designing L2/L3 data models aligned to layered architecture standards
  • Building data products optimised for cloud analytics
  • Ensuring scalability, maintainability, and reusability
  • Producing clear documentation and metadata
  • Defining governance processes for long‑term model stewardship
  • Communicating modelling concepts to internal stakeholders
  • Knowledge transfer to internal teams


What you’ll bring:

  • Strong conceptual, logical, and physical data modelling skills
  • Dimensional modelling (star/snowflake)
  • Experience with ADLS, Databricks, and lakehouse architectures
  • Solid understanding of raw/curated/consumption layers
  • Strong SQL and experience with enterprise modelling tools
  • Ability to explain complex models to non‑technical audiences


This is a great opportunity to influence a high‑visibility programme within one of Sweden’s leading financial institutions.


If this sounds like the right next step, feel free to reach out.

4406081351,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-meritmind-ab-4406081351?position=15&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=IOm1AvOG5sCxYmUXeujGUg%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Meritmind AB,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Analytikerchef inom data science, Stockholm
Meritmind söker för kunds räkning en analytikerchef för ett konsultuppdrag för vår kund i Stockholm.

Vi söker en ledare som vill ta ett helhetsgrepp om den fortsatta resan inom Data & Analytics och spela en nyckelroll i att forma hur vi arbetar datadrivet i hela organisationen. I rollen får du möjlighet att kombinera strategiskt tänkande med operativ påverkan och arbeta nära verksamheten för att skapa verkligt affärsvärde. Tjänsten är ett konsultuppdrag på heltid som beräknas starta i juni och pågå till mars 2027.
Sätta riktning och driva utvecklingen av Data & Analytics som en central förmåga i organisationen
Omsätta affärsbehov till datadrivna initiativ som stärker tillväxt, effektivitet och lönsamhet
Leda och vidareutveckla ett kompetent team med starkt eget driv och tvärfunktionellt arbetssätt
Säkerställa relevanta analyser, insikter och beslutsstöd som gör skillnad i affären
Vara en nyckelspelare i digital utveckling och systeminitiativ
Arbeta nära affär och IT i en agil kontext, med tydlig målstyrning
Har en relevant akademisk bakgrund, exempelvis inom statistik, ekonomi, teknik eller motsvarande
Har flera års erfarenhet av att arbeta med avancerad analys och datadrivna arbetssätt i moderna analysverktyg
Är van att omsätta data till konkreta affärsresultat, såsom nya intäktsmöjligheter, ökad effektivitet och förbättrad lönsamhet
Gärna har erfarenhet från verksamheter med stora datamängder, exempelvis kopplat till kunder, produkter eller transaktioner
För att trivas och lyckas i rollen ser vi också att du:
Har en stark förmåga att identifiera verkliga behov i verksamheten och skapa engagemang för förändring över funktionella gränser
Har dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leda förändrings- och transformationsinitiativ inom Data & Analytics eller närliggande områden
Är en trygg och inspirerande ledare som bygger förtroende, utvecklar team och skapar förutsättningar för hög prestation
Leder med tydlighet och transparens, och kombinerar ett coachande förhållningssätt med förmågan att ge ansvar och mandat
Har mycket god samarbets- och kommunikationsförmåga, och trivs i en roll med många kontaktytor
I detta konsultuppdrag samarbetar kunden med Meritmind

Meritmind är ett av Sveriges ledande konsult- och rekryteringsbolag inom ekonomiområdet mm. Med ett stort nätverk av skarpa konsulter och träffsäkra rekryteringar hjälper Meritmind människor och företag att lyckas inom ekonomiområdet varje dag. 25 års erfarenhet har gjort oss till en ledstjärna - för verksamheter i behov av expertis och för ekonomer som värdesätter utveckling och omtanke. Från våra kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Uppsala, Linköping, Västerås och Jönköping sysselsätter vi 400 personer och verkar över hela Sverige.
För mer information om konsultuppdraget är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig konsultchef.

Intervjuer och urval kommer att ske löpande, så skicka gärna din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Se till att namn och kontaktuppgifter finns med i ditt CV och det är endast möjligt att bifoga ett dokument.
Meritmind arbetar kompetensbaserat för att främja en objektiv och icke-diskriminerande konsultprocess.
Tänk på detta innan du skickar in din ansökan (i enlighet med GDPR-lagstiftningen)

Din ansökan ska ej innehålla personuppgifter kring civilstatus, familjesituation/barn, etniskt ursprung, ålder, politiska åsikter, religiös eller filosofisk övertygelse, medlemskap i fackförening, hälsa eller sexuell läggning.

4411715923,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/product-analyst-merchant-information-at-visa-4411715923?position=19&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=6fIFopN3XrjQTSk99m280A%3D%3D,Product Analyst - Merchant Information,Visa,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","About Us

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.
At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.
Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Tink was founded in 2012, and a decade later became part of Visa in 2012. Tink was created with the aim of changing the banking industry for the better. We have built Europe’s most robust open banking platform – with the broadest, deepest connectivity and powerful services that create value out of the financial data. We offer the tools that allow anyone – from big banks and fintech to startups – to build the future of financial services across Europe.

The Data & Risk product family has a clear mission: transforming complex financial data into clear, actionable insights that empower institutions to make smarter decisions and consumers to improve their financial health. We are a tightly knit team of product managers, backend and frontend engineers, platform architects and data scientists working towards enabling data-driven decisions.

We are looking for a Product Analyst to support the product team working on Merchant Information. You will help translate customer feedback and product needs into clear insights, support day-to-day product execution, and strengthen our quality practices through testing and validation.

This role is a great fit for someone who enjoys working with data, product quality, and structured problem solving, and who wants plenty of room to grow into deeper analytical and modelling-oriented work over time.
Support in processing and structuring customer feedback, helping the team identify patterns, common issues, and opportunities to improve product quality and usability
Support in creating visualizations, querying data, and analyzing quality signals to help track performance and guide product improvements
Support in testing, validation, and QA, including helping validate behavior changes during migrations and ensuring outputs meet expected standards for accuracy and consistency
Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and other stakeholders to help clarify requirements, document expected behavior, and support reliable delivery across releases
Help maintain lightweight documentation, reports, and follow-ups that make it easier for teams to understand quality, progress, and where to focus next

About Us

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Job Description

Tink was founded in 2012, and a decade later became part of Visa in 2012. Tink was created with the aim of changing the banking industry for the better. We have built Europe’s most robust open banking platform – with the broadest, deepest connectivity and powerful services that create value out of the financial data. We offer the tools that allow anyone – from big banks and fintech to startups – to build the future of financial services across Europe.

The Data &
column2:
Risk product family has a clear mission: transforming complex financial data into clear, actionable insights that empower institutions to make smarter decisions and consumers to improve their financial health. We are a tightly knit team of product managers, backend and frontend engineers, platform architects and data scientists working towards enabling data-driven decisions.

What You Will Do

We are looking for a Product Analyst to support the product team working on Merchant Information. You will help translate customer feedback and product needs into clear insights, support day-to-day product execution, and strengthen our quality practices through testing and validation.

This role is a great fit for someone who enjoys working with data, product quality, and structured problem solving, and who wants plenty of room to grow into deeper analytical and modelling-oriented work over time.

Responsibilities

  • Support in processing and structuring customer feedback, helping the team identify patterns, common issues, and opportunities to improve product quality and usability
  • Support in creating visualizations, querying data, and analyzing quality signals to help track performance and guide product improvements
  • Support in testing, validation, and QA, including helping validate behavior changes during migrations and ensuring outputs meet expected standards for accuracy and consistency
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and other stakeholders to help clarify requirements, document expected behavior, and support reliable delivery across releases
  • Help maintain lightweight documentation, reports, and follow-ups that make it easier for teams to understand quality, progress, and where to focus next

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.
Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.
We are looking for Study Participants from all professional backgrounds across Sweden to take part in academic and applied research studies for global businesses and educational faculties.
Whether you are just starting your career or are a seasoned expert, your perspective is valuable. We are actively seeking a diverse range of educational backgrounds, including:
No High School Diploma / High School Graduates

If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute screening to assess your suitability. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to contribute to a wide range of projects.

Compensation: Researchers looking for your profile tend to pay up to $18/hr per study completed. While many tasks are short, you should be prepared for studies that require up to one hour of uninterrupted work.
What you'll be doing in the role
As a participant, you will contribute to high-quality research outcomes by responding thoughtfully to various tasks, including:
AI Training & Evaluation: helping refine Large Language Models by ranking responses or providing creative prompts.
Behavioral Experiments: participating in decision-making tasks and cognitive games.
User Research: providing feedback on new software, apps, or product concepts.
Academic Studies: assisting university researchers in fields like psychology, economics, and sociology.
Interviews: engaging in one-on-one deep dives regarding your professional expertise or lived experience.
Surveys & Questionnaires: sharing your opinions on current events, consumer habits, or workplace culture.
Diverse Perspective: a track record of relevant experience, knowledge, or lived context (from entry-level roles to executive or academic positions).
Attention to Detail: the ability to remain focused and provide high-quality data for the duration of a study.
Technical Setup: a reliable, fast internet connection and access to a desktop or laptop computer.
Financial Readiness: a PayPal account to receive payments and a willingness to self-declare earnings as a self-employed participant.

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to contribute to meaningful research used by universities, researchers, and global organizations. Once you pass our screening, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and organizations with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data helps researchers answer important questions, improve products and services, and advance knowledge across disciplines.

We believe that the best research outcomes come from diverse human perspectives and experiences. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of research innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

With over 245 hotels, 120 restaurants, 20 spas and more, we create thousands of experiences every day. With urban Comfort Hotel®, warm Quality Hotel™, stylish Clarion Hotel® and cosy Home Hotel and more than 40 unique independent hotels, our guests can pick and choose. Our team of 20,000 passionate individuals from more than 120 countries is what makes us grow! Strawberry is built on our core values: energy, courage and enthusiasm.
Shape the Future of Hospitality at Strawberry

At Strawberry, we're not just a hotel company anymore – we're building a universe of experiences. We're transforming the hospitality landscape through innovation and technology, and 2023 marked a major milestone with the launch of our new mother brand, Strawberry. This exciting evolution creates countless opportunities for growth and impact, and we're looking for a passionate and skilled Product Data Analyst to join our Strawberry Data & Insights Team and help us shape the future of travel.

Our main mission is to establish a scalable foundation for how we work with product analytics across our tech product portfolio, spanning HQ operations, hotel operations, and our independent websites for our Luxury, Lifestyle & Resort division. As part of our team, you will establish frameworks, ways of working, and taxonomy structures that our teams can continue to build and execute on. This role is a crucial bridge between our Product Managers and their stakeholders, to make sure we build products that have the impact that we expect them to have, empowering the product teams to make better data-informed decisions independently.

As a Product Data Analyst, you'll play a crucial role in empowering Strawberry to become a truly data-driven organization. You'll be instrumental in developing and maintaining robust analytical solutions, supporting the full product development lifecycle, and moving us toward self-serve analytics.

Product Analytics & Insights: Support the full product development lifecycle by partnering with PMs to identify key levers, analyzing funnels and cohorts, and translating data into actionable recommendations.
Metrics & Reporting: Define and maintain product KPIs, OKRs, and success metrics, and build dashboards and automated reporting.
Data Quality: Improve and create new  event taxonomy, tracking documentation, and data quality standards, collaborating with Product and BI teams on semantic models.
Frameworks & Enablement: Establish scalable frameworks and ways of working for product analytics, including taxonomy structures and experimentation practices.
Culture Champion: Actively coach product teams in data-driven methods and metrics, contributing to a self-serve insights culture day-to-day.
Solid background in product analytics, including funnel analysis, cohort analysis, and behavioural modelling.
A product-minded approach that balances speed with rigour and communicates clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Proven ability to coach and enable PMs, designers, and engineers to work more data-driven independently.
Comfort navigating ambiguity and legacy systems while building toward a scalable future state.
Proficiency in web analytics and event-based models (primarily GA4) and expert-level tag management (e.g., Google Tag Manager).
Strong understanding of experimentation design and hands-on experience with A/B testing platforms.
Proficiency in PowerBI and experience building semantic models (including structuring them to be AI-ready).
Advanced SQL skills and familiarity with Cloud Data Warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery).
Bonus: Python or R experience, familiarity with dbt, and experience with AI products or ML-adjacent environments.

What We Offer: This is a 9- to 12-month role for now to get us started. Strawberry is in a transformation journey and we have to be careful about our long term commitments. We however want to invest in you and offer you:
Real Impact: Be a part of a dynamic transformation and contribute directly to our success story.
Empowerment: Work in a flat organization with trust, autonomy, and the opportunity to make a real difference.
Growth & Development: Access professional development opportunities to enhance your skills and advance your career.
Fun & Community: Enjoy a vibrant work environment with plenty of social events and team-building activities.
Fantastic Perks: Benefit from great employee discounts, including staff and friends & family rates at our hotels.
Travel included: This role begins with a 9 to 12-month contract with your base at our HQ in Solna, Stockholm or Oslo (some travel between locations expected).
Summer vacation: We have summer code freeze June 29th to August 3rd so we expect you to take 3-4 weeks vacation in that period.

We believe that the best research outcomes come from diverse human perspectives and experiences. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of research innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Role: Data Modeller – One Data Ecosystem (ECP)

Location: Stockholm, Sweden

Type: Contract

Mode: Hybrid (2-3days/week)

Key Responsibilities

• Define and propose a governance process enabling continuous maintenance and development

• Communicate data modelling concepts and outcomes to internal stakeholders

4413714989,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/junior-analytiker-med-intresse-f%C3%B6r-startupf%C3%B6retag-%E2%80%93-sommarjobb-at-vinnova-4413714989?position=26&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=DJxfsMrATiJEQa%2FKwtKpFQ%3D%3D,Junior analytiker med intresse för startupföretag – sommarjobb,Vinnova,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Är du nyutexaminerad eller studerar vid universitet eller högskola? Är du nyfiken på vad som ligger bakom Sveriges ledande position inom innovativa startupföretag?
Nu söker vi två juniora analytiker till Vinnova för ett sommaruppdrag där du får arbeta med analyser av hur innovativa startupföretag utvecklas och samtidigt bidra till samhällsnytta.

Som junior analytiker på Vinnova får du ett kvalificerat och utvecklande uppdrag med unik inblick i Sveriges näringsliv och innovationssystem. Du får möjlighet att utveckla din analytiska förmåga i en professionell miljö, tillsammans med engagerade och kunniga kollegor.
Uppdraget inleds med en introduktion och du får en handledare som stöd under hela anställningsperioden.

Vinnova har finansierat ett stort antal innovativa startupföretag som utvecklar nya lösningar, affärsmodeller och tekniker. Nu vill vi fördjupa förståelsen för hur dessa företag har utvecklats över tid och vilka effekter finansieringen har haft.
I uppdraget kommer du bland annat att arbeta med följande analysfrågor:
Hur har de startupföretag som Vinnova finansierat utvecklats över tid?
Hur har företagens tillväxt och värdeskapande utvecklats?
I vilken utsträckning sker värdeskapandet i Sverige respektive internationellt?
Vilka faktorer har varit särskilt viktiga för företagens utveckling?
Vilka möjligheter och utmaningar finns för uppskalning i Sverige?

Du arbetar med både kvantitativa och kvalitativa analyser, baserade på externa datakällor och Vinnovas interna data. Arbetet sker i nära samarbete med din handledare och analysteamet, men kräver också att du kan planera och driva delar av arbetet självständigt.
Under uppdraget deltar du i interna möten och workshoppar. Uppdraget avslutas med att du sammanställer resultaten i en intern analysrapport och presenterar dina slutsatser muntligt.
Nyutexaminerad eller studerande på minst kandidatnivå, med relevant utbildningsinriktning, till exempel inom samhällsvetenskap, teknik eller naturvetenskap.
God förmåga att hantera, analysera och dra slutsatser utifrån både kvantitativa och kvalitativa data
Förmåga att strukturera, presentera och kommunicera information på ett tydligt och målgruppsanpassat sätt, både muntligt och skriftligt
Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift
Erfarenhet av att utveckla egna analysmodeller
Erfarenhet av dataanalys, statistiska metoder eller datavisualisering
Vi lägger stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper och förmågor.

Vi söker dig som är nyfiken, analytisk och har ett genuint intresse för lärande. Du arbetar metodiskt med stora informationsmängder och kan dra välgrundade slutsatser. Du har förmåga att strukturera och presentera resultat med hänsyn till målgrupp och sammanhang.

Eftersom arbetets detaljer och inriktning kan justeras under uppdragets gång är det viktigt att du är flexibel och trivs i en dynamisk miljö. Du samarbetar gärna med andra för att utveckla idéer, resonemang och metoder. Samtidigt kan du självständigt planera och driva ditt arbete. Du representerar organisationen på ett professionellt sätt.
Du delar Vinnovas värderingar – respekt, ansvarstagande och mod – och omsätter dem i ditt dagliga arbete.

Vi tillämpar hybridarbete, vilket innebär att du kan kombinera arbete hemifrån med arbete på vårt kontor på Mäster Samuelsgatan 56 i Stockholm, nära Centralstationen. Minst hälften av arbetstiden förläggs till kontoret.
Anställningen är en särskild visstidsanställning på heltid under sommaren 2026.
Vi ser gärna att du kan börja så snart som möjligt, men vi är också öppna för dialog utifrån dina förutsättningar. Det är en tydlig fördel om du kan arbeta större delen av sommaren.
Vi planerar att anställa två personer till uppdraget.
Vinnova är Sveriges innovationsmyndighet. Vi öppnar upp för innovation som ger hållbara lösningar och stärker svensk konkurrenskraft. Läs mer på vinnova.se
Vill du veta mer om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Göran Marklund, direktör vid enheten Strategisk omvärldsanalys:
Vi avböjer vänligen all rekryterings- och annonseringshjälp.
Välkommen att ansöka via vår rekryteringsportal senast 2026‑05‑26.
Inför din ansökan ber vi dig ta del av information om vår rekryteringsprocess här och om bisysslor här

Vinnova arbetar kompetensbaserat för att främja en objektiv och icke-diskriminerande rekryteringsprocess. Det innebär bland annat att du inte ska bifoga personligt brev och att arbetspsykologiska tester kan förekomma i rekryteringen.
Vi välkomnar sökande med olika bakgrunder och erfarenheter.

4413417026,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-to-credit-risk-seb-stockholm-at-seb-4413417026?position=27&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=eiAEv6q6NHlSlovFNI2Qvw%3D%3D,"Data engineer to Credit Risk | SEB, Stockholm",SEB,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Join SEB’s Credit Risk team as a Data Engineer to drive positive change by optimizing data pipelines, enhancing analytical capabilities, and ensuring data integrity for critical credit risk models, shaping the future of finance with us.

At SEB, we recognise that the world of finance is evolving at an extraordinary speed. That’s why we’re committed to driving towards a more sustainable future for everyone — people, businesses, and society. Since we welcomed our first customer in 1856, we have been a catalyst for positive change by providing reliable advice and capital to people with ideas for the future. If you are ready to make your mark on our industry and positively shape the world, you will thrive at SEB.

As a Data Engineer in our Credit Risk team, you will design, develop, and maintain crucial data infrastructure supporting our credit risk models. You’ll ensure data availability, accuracy, and reliability by collaborating with Quantitative modelers, experts, and stakeholders. Responsibilities include developing scalable data pipelines, integrating diverse sources, and ensuring high data quality and governance with Data Stewards. Your work will optimize and automate processes for efficient reporting and robust risk assessments, making a significant impact on our analytical capabilities.
To thrive in this role, we believe you have:
Proven experience as a Data Engineer, proficient in SQL, Python, and data modeling techniques, with a track record of working with large datasets.
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and best practices for data integration and storage.
A strong understanding of data quality, security, and governance best practices.
Familiarity with credit risk, Basel III, or other financial risk frameworks is a significant advantage.
Demonstrated problem-solving abilities and strong communication skills for effective cross-team collaboration.
A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Experience in Credit Risk: Experience with risk models, including credit scoring, financial regulations, and portfolio analysis, is highly desirable.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to work with complex datasets.
Experience with version control systems (e.g., Git).
A diverse, inclusive and equal environment
Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
Do you want to be part of SEB?

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than 2026-05-27. If you have questions about the position, please contact Indra Wilson, indra.wilson@seb.se, note that we will not process applications via email.

Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, which means that this position might be covered by internal redeployment.
Learn more about working at SEB on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.

4408031805,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analytics-solution-officer-based-in-luxembourg-at-european-investment-bank-eib-4408031805?position=28&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=0jutFaYpdW6cc8jQvlXTjg%3D%3D,Analytics Solution Officer - based in Luxembourg,European Investment Bank (EIB),"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence. The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The EIB, the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Group Information Systems Directorate (GIS), Data & Corporate Solutions Department (DCS), Data Management Division (DM), Analytics & Reporting Unit (AR) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, an Analytics Solution Officer*.
This is a full-time position at grade 5 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.
Panel interviews are anticipated for June/July 2026

The Analytics Solution Officer ensures the stable, efficient and high‑quality delivery, evolution and governance of business‑critical IT applications and services supporting EIB Group business processes. The role exists to safeguard continuity of service, strengthen service delivery governance, and support the Bank’s ongoing modernisation initiatives by coordinating internal teams and external service providers in line with IT strategy, policies and contractual commitments.
The role focuses on the ownership and continuous evolution of analytics solutions, ensuring reliable operations while supporting ongoing change.

The role sits within the Analytics and Reporting Unit, which is responsible for the end‑to‑end lifecycle of analytics and reporting solutions supporting EIB Group business processes. This includes solution ownership, service delivery, vendor coordination and contribution to change initiatives.
The post holder works closely with business stakeholders, IT counterparts, senior management and external providers, and reports directly to the Head of Unit.
Ensure end‑to‑end ownership and day‑to‑day delivery of analytics solutions and related services, maintaining availability, performance, security and compliance with internal standards.
Manage the full lifecycle of assigned solutions, from planning and sourcing through implementation, operation and controlled evolution, including roadmaps and service models.
Coordinate and steer external providers in a multi‑vendor setup, ensuring effective business‑as‑usual operations and measurable service performance through regular follow‑up, controls and reporting.
Support the definition and execution of externalisation initiatives, ensuring clear ownership, effective controls, service assurance and compliance with contractual obligations.
Liaise with business stakeholders, internal IT teams and external partners to translate functional needs into sustainable analytics solutions.

Contribute to project and delivery activities related to the acquisition of software and services, ensuring consistency with business priorities, enterprise architecture principles, IT security requirements and applicable policies.
Support continuous improvement of analytics platforms and services by identifying optimisation opportunities, promoting standardisation and reducing solution complexity where appropriate.
Maintain awareness of functional and technical evolutions of the platforms within the area of responsibility, advising stakeholders on relevant enhancements, options and implications.
Ensure appropriate governance, documentation, risk management and reporting across service delivery and change activities.
Support operational resilience through active involvement in incident handling, change coordination and issue escalation in collaboration with relevant parties.

Purpose

The Analytics Solution Officer ensures the stable, efficient and high‑quality delivery, evolution and governance of business‑critical IT applications and services supporting EIB Group business processes. The role exists to safeguard continuity of service, strengthen service delivery governance, and support the Bank’s ongoing modernisation initiatives by coordinating internal teams and external service providers in line with IT strategy, policies and contractual commitments.

The role focuses on the ownership and continuous evolution of analytics solutions, ensuring reliable operations while supporting ongoing change.

Operating Network

The role sits within the Analytics and Reporting Unit, which is responsible for the end‑to‑end lifecycle of analytics and reporting solutions supporting EIB Group business processes. This includes solution ownership, service delivery, vendor coordination and contribution to change initiatives.

The post holder works closely with business stakeholders, IT counterparts, senior management and external providers, and reports directly to the Head of Unit.

Accountabilities

  • Ensure end‑to‑end ownership and day‑to‑day delivery of analytics solutions and related services, maintaining availability, performance, security and compliance with internal standards.
  • Manage the full lifecycle of assigned solutions, from planning and sourcing through implementation, operation and controlled evolution, including roadmaps and service models.
  • Coordinate and steer external providers in a multi‑vendor setup, ensuring effective business‑as‑usual operations and measurable service performance through regular follow‑up, controls and reporting.
  • Support the definition and execution of externalisation initiatives, ensuring clear ownership, effective controls, service assurance and compliance with contractual obligations.
  • Liaise with business stakeholders, internal IT teams and external partners to translate functional needs into sustainable analytics solutions.
  • Contribute to project and delivery activities related to the acquisition of software and services, ensuring consistency with business priorities, enterprise architecture principles, IT security requirements and applicable policies.
  • Support continuous improvement of analytics platforms and services by identifying optimisation opportunities, promoting standardisation and reducing solution complexity where appropriate.
  • Maintain awareness of functional and technical evolutions of the platforms within the area of responsibility, advising stakeholders on relevant enhancements, options and implications.
  • Ensure appropriate governance, documentation, risk management and reporting across service delivery and change activities.
  • Support operational resilience through active involvement in incident handling, change coordination and issue escalation in collaboration with relevant parties.

Qualifications

  • University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in Engineering, Information Technology, Information Management, Economics/Finance or a related field.

  • Experience with other business intelligence and analytics tools such as SAP BusinessObjects, SAP Analytics Cloud or Tableau is considered an asset.
  • Good understanding of application lifecycle management, IT service management principles and delivery governance.
  • Experience in coordinating projects involving multiple stakeholders and disciplines.
  • Experience in Data Warehouse design and methodologies would be an asset.
  • Ability to work effectively in a multicultural, international organization.
  • Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (*) with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage).

4380528230,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-cgi-4380528230?position=32&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=Y2cIyO3LfM7gvs4XvRtLRw%3D%3D,Data Engineer,CGI,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Beskrivning av tjänsten

CGI fortsätter att expandera sin data engineering verksamhet i Stockholm och söker nu en erfaren och mångsidig dataingenjör som vill arbeta nära några av Sveriges mest betydande aktörer inom finansiella tjänster. Som en del av vår växande enhet inom Financial Services får du en central roll i att designa, utveckla och leverera moderna datalösningar i komplexa och reglerade miljöer. Rollen innebär att du arbetar i flera moln- och plattformsekosystem, däribland Azure, AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, Synapse, BigQuery och Fabric, beroende på kundernas behov och arkitektur. Du blir en viktig rådgivare och teknisk partner i uppdrag som spänner över allt från arkitektur och modellering till pipeline utveckling, optimering och styrning.

I rollen kommer du att arbeta nära kundernas intressenter för att förstå deras verksamhetsbehov och omsätta dem till skalbara och hållbara datalösningar. Du kommer att analysera och översätta komplexa regulatoriska, riskrelaterade och operativa krav till teknisk arkitektur och presentera rekommendationer och bästa praxis för olika dataplattformar. Arbetet innebär även att bidra i förstudier, offerter och estimat samt att delta i thought leadership aktiviteter inom data engineering området.

Du kommer att designa och bygga både batch- och streamingpipelines i flera tekniska miljöar, exempelvis Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse, AWS Glue, Redshift, Kinesis, BigQuery, Dataflow och Pub/Sub. Arbetet omfattar även att utveckla pipelines för verksamhetsområden som centralbank, betalningar, marknader, försäkring, AML/KYC och regulatoriska rapporteringsflöden. Du kommer att implementera medallion arkitekturer, stödja CDC mönster och schemautveckling samt skapa semantiska lager för analys i plattformar som Databricks SQL, Synapse SQL, Snowflake, BigQuery och Power BI.

I rollen ingår också att utveckla dimensionella modeller, breda tabeller och tidsseriemodeller samt att bygga kurerade och styrda datamängder för områden som ekonomi, risk, treasury och compliance. Du kommer att arbeta med dataprodukter inom Data Mesh ramverk och implementera plattformsspecifika arkitekturmönster såsom Delta Lake, Iceberg, BigQuery tabeller och Snowflake mikropartitioner.

En viktig del av arbetet är att säkerställa datakvalitet, lineage, metadata och katalogisering samt att tillämpa datakontroller som kryptering, tokenisering och maskering. Du kommer även att optimera pipelines och frågor för prestanda och kostnadseffektivitet, samt arbeta enligt etablerade konsultstandarder, agila metoder och CI/CD principer. Rollen innebär dessutom ansvar för dokumentation, utbildning och överlämning till kundernas team.
Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll

För att lyckas i rollen behöver du ha mycket goda kunskaper i SQL och Python, gärna med erfarenhet av PySpark. Du har praktisk erfarenhet av minst två större plattformar eller molnmiljöer, exempelvis Databricks, Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP eller Microsoft Fabric. Du har dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leverera datalösningar inom finansiella tjänster och är van att kommunicera med och påverka intressenter på olika nivåer i organisationen. Rollen kräver att du behärskar både svenska och engelska i tal och skrift och att du har en stark förmåga att skapa samarbete och samordning i komplexa miljöer.
Databricks certifierad Data Engineer, Azure Data Engineer Associate, Snowflake certifieringar eller motsvarande AWS/GCP certifieringar
Erfarenhet av Kafka, Kinesis eller Event Hubs för realtidspipelines
Erfarenhet av dbt, MLflow, Delta Sharing, Iceberg eller liknande tekniker
Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! Vi går igenom ansökningarna löpande. Har du några funderingar kring rollen vänligen kontakta Manu Oberoi på manu.oberoi@cgi.com.
Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.
Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.

Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.

Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.

Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.
