Du kommer att designa och bygga både batch- och streamingpipelines i flera tekniska miljöar, exempelvis Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse, AWS Glue, Redshift, Kinesis, BigQuery, Dataflow och Pub/Sub. Arbetet omfattar även att utveckla pipelines för verksamhetsområden som centralbank, betalningar, marknader, försäkring, AML/KYC och regulatoriska rapporteringsflöden. Du kommer att implementera medallion arkitekturer, stödja CDC mönster och schemautveckling samt skapa semantiska lager för analys i plattformar som Databricks SQL, Synapse SQL, Snowflake, BigQuery och Power BI.

I rollen ingår också att utveckla dimensionella modeller, breda tabeller och tidsseriemodeller samt att bygga kurerade och styrda datamängder för områden som ekonomi, risk, treasury och compliance. Du kommer att arbeta med dataprodukter inom Data Mesh ramverk och implementera plattformsspecifika arkitekturmönster såsom Delta Lake, Iceberg, BigQuery tabeller och Snowflake mikropartitioner.

En viktig del av arbetet är att säkerställa datakvalitet, lineage, metadata och katalogisering samt att tillämpa datakontroller som kryptering, tokenisering och maskering. Du kommer även att optimera pipelines och frågor för prestanda och kostnadseffektivitet, samt arbeta enligt etablerade konsultstandarder, agila metoder och CI/CD principer. Rollen innebär dessutom ansvar för dokumentation, utbildning och överlämning till kundernas team.

Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll

För att lyckas i rollen behöver du ha mycket goda kunskaper i SQL och Python, gärna med erfarenhet av PySpark. Du har praktisk erfarenhet av minst två större plattformar eller molnmiljöer, exempelvis Databricks, Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP eller Microsoft Fabric. Du har dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leverera datalösningar inom finansiella tjänster och är van att kommunicera med och påverka intressenter på olika nivåer i organisationen. Rollen kräver att du behärskar både svenska och engelska i tal och skrift och att du har en stark förmåga att skapa samarbete och samordning i komplexa miljöer.

Önskvärda kvalifikationer

  • Databricks certifierad Data Engineer, Azure Data Engineer Associate, Snowflake certifieringar eller motsvarande AWS/GCP certifieringar
  • Erfarenhet av Kafka, Kinesis eller Event Hubs för realtidspipelines
  • Erfarenhet av dbt, MLflow, Delta Sharing, Iceberg eller liknande tekniker

Låter det intressant?

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! Vi går igenom ansökningarna löpande. Har du några funderingar kring rollen vänligen kontakta Manu Oberoi på manu.oberoi@cgi.com.

Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.

Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.

Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.

Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.

Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.

Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

Trivs du i en miljö som präglas av handlingskraft och samhällsengagemang?

Utbetalningsmyndigheten är en innovativ myndighet under utveckling. Genom att jobba hos oss får du vara med och forma en verksamhet som upptäcker, förhindrar och förebygger felaktiga utbetalningar och välfärdsbrott. Vi erbjuder en arbetsplats där du får stort ansvar och mandat att påverka. Hos oss gör din kompetens och ditt engagemang skillnad.

På Utbetalningsmyndigheten är arbete med data och analys centralt. Nu söker vi dig som vill jobba med att utöka myndighetens förmåga inom dataintag och dataanalys. Rollen är placerad på avdelningen data och utveckling.

I rollen arbetar du aktivt med data och dataanalys i olika faser och skapar värde för vår verksamhet. Du kommer arbeta tvärfunktionellt med analys och förädling av data. Inhämtning av nya datakällor i hela kedjan, samt aktivt bidra i arbetet med datakvalitet och datadefinitioner. I rollen ingår utveckling och införande av processer för t.ex. datakvalitet, och spårbarhet. Arbetet kräver ett nära samarbete med andra myndigheter och aktörer.
Akademisk examen inom t.ex. informatik, statistik, systemvetenskap
Minst tre års erfarenhet av att formulera frågor mot större datamängder med SQL
Minst tre års erfarenhet av att arbeta med analys och dokumentation av data
Mycket god förmåga att formulera dig i tal och skrift på svenska
Det är meriterande om du även har
Erfarenhet av att arbeta i tvärfunktionella team
Erfarenhet av arbete med verktyg för metadata

För att lyckas i rollen är du strukturerad och mål- och resultatinriktad. Du har ett kreativt förhållningssätt och god initiativförmåga. Du har gott omdöme, god samarbetsförmåga och har en väl utvecklad problemlösande analysförmåga.
Den statliga värdegrunden sammanfattar och synliggör de viktigaste kraven som gäller för statsanställda, att arbeta i staten innebär att följa den. Vi ställer även höga krav på säkerhetsmedvetenhet.

Tjänstgöringsorten är i Stockholm. Myndigheten finns i moderna lokaler i närheten av Gullmarsplan. Befattningen är placerad i säkerhetsklass vilket kräver svenskt medborgarskap. Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid. Anställningen inleds med sex månaders provanställning. Du kan komma att krigsplaceras på myndigheten.
Vi strävar efter att alla medarbetare ska ha en hållbar arbetsmiljö och balans mellan jobb och fritid. Vi erbjuder många förmåner för dig som anställd.

I denna rekrytering söker du med ditt CV. Istället för att bifoga ett personligt brev vill vi att du svarar på några frågor kring din kompetens kopplat till jobbet. Vi ber dig svara utförligt på dessa frågor.
Vill du veta mer om tjänsten, kontakta avdelningschef: Niclas Cederberg på telefon 010-572 7576.
Vi tar gärna emot samtal från dig som är intresserad av jobbet, men tackar nej till dig som säljer annonser och rekryteringstjänster.
Kontaktpersoner för de fackliga organisationerna är:

column2: Mattias Kollberg (Seko), telefon 010-482 17 59
column3: och Michael Ohlsson (ST), telefon 010-5727512.

4413126370,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/sr-digital-analyst-at-nordea-4413126370?position=34&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=SwqqjMrcuYsL2lfBmzr%2BGQ%3D%3D,Sr. Digital Analyst,Nordea,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Job ID: 3224

The Nordic Asset Wealth Management Digital Sales and Engagement team is growing, and we are looking for a Senior Digital Analyst who enjoys working with data insights and building comprehensive analytics strategies that drive business decisions.

In AWM Digital Sales & Engagement we believe in a proactive forward-thinking approach to drive growth, efficiency and success. As part of a dynamic organization in AWM COO, we’re committed to delivering exceptional services and solutions, and we rely on a team of skilled professionals to achieve our ambitious goals. If you thrive in a dynamic environment and have a proven track record in digital analytics, we want to hear from you!

At Nordea, we see that the world is changing fast – and we want to be one step ahead of the curve. That’s why we’re deeply committed to providing the financial solutions of tomorrow to our customers. We’re creating an agile environment where we experiment and grow together – and we need your ideas and unique background. With us, you’ll be in good company with a chance to make your mark on something bigger.

You will be part of a Nordic team working closely across Finland, Sweden, Norway, and Denmark, enabling cross-market collaboration, alignment, and shared best practices. Together, we work across digital journeys, analytics, marketing technology, and customer engagement—continuously improving how we create value for our customers and the business.

As part of the AWM COO organization, you will join a strategic function focused on driving operational excellence, digital transformation, and scalable, insight-led ways of working across Asset & Wealth Management.
We are looking for someone with a strong analytical mindset who thrives in a dynamic environment and can translate complex data into clear business value.
Analyse web, app and campaign behaviour to identify actionable insights for targeting, content personalisation, and conversion optimisation.
Analyze and visualize digital performance data across channels and translate findings into actionable business insights
Develop comprehensive dashboards and reports to visualize key performance metrics and customer behavior through Adobe tools such as Adobe Analytics or Customer Journey Analytics.
Provide clear and actionable performance insights across multiple marketing channels.
Contribute in enhancing measurement framework development for digital channels to obtain better insights and to ensure validation and quality of data.
Collaborate with stakeholders to define KPIs and success metrics for digital initiatives
Collaborate with other analysts and work cross-functionally with various business units.
Deliver actionable insights to optimize flows, website performance, user experiences, content engagement and digital marketing campaigns
Enable and contribute towards strategic initiatives, such as standardization and benchmarking of digital activities.
Support A/B and multivariate testing across campaigns and digital experiences, from setup and measurement to insight and optimisation recommendations
The role can be based in Stockholm, Sweden or Helsinki, Finland.
This is the right role for you if you:
Analytically driven, with the ability to extract meaningful insights from complex digital data
Enjoy working closely with marketers, content teams, and product owner to design and deliver personalised and test‑driven digital experiences
Solution-oriented and creative when interpreting performance metrics
Skilled at collaborating with stakeholders to define and track meaningful KPIs
Proactive in identifying optimization opportunities through data analysis
Able to translate technical analytics concepts into business language
Someone who enjoys educating colleagues on the value of data-driven decision making
Proactive and self-driven individual, able to work on a Nordic scale
5+ years of experience in digital analytics or web analytics roles.
Strong proficiency with web analytics platforms (Adobe Analytics preferred)
Strong understanding of digital marketing channels and performance measurement
Proven experience supporting A/B testing and personalization initiatives
Strong written and verbal communication skills
column2: ability to present findings and recommendations clearly

column1: Experience with other Adobe platform tools including Adobe Target and Adobe Experience Platform, leveraging their capabilities to drive actionable insights and optimize digital strategies is preferred.
You have a relevant university degree, e.g. digital marketing, information technology, statistics, business analytics, or a related field
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English. Finnish or Swedish beneficial and dependent on location.
If this sounds like you, get in touch!
Submit your application no later than 24/05/2026.
For more information, you’re welcome to contact Anders Nygaard Jensen at anders.n.jensen@nordea.com
Only applications conducted through the system will be considered.
For union information, please contact finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACONordea@nordea.com.

About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Overview:

The Agoda Content department is currently looking for a curious and questioning self-starter and proactive analyst, who will help drive decision making with insights from content, marketing and partner performance data.

You will get the opportunity to own analytical projects to direct our department’s focus, and prioritize our actions. By joining Agoda, you will become part of a truly international team, with leading experts of different backgrounds coming from 50+ countries around the world. This is a genuine opportunity to gain valuable experience in a challenging and cutting-edge tech environment. The ideal candidate would have a passion for high-converting content, great user-experience, data and analysis tools and methods. This position reports to the Associate Director of Content Operations, in Bangkok, Thailand.

Main responsibilities:

  • Understand Content goals and KPIs and be able to align reports and insights based on them. Identify and investigate trends, anomalies and opportunities to improve Agoda’s Content strategy.
  • Identify content opportunities that drive customer value, bookings and conversion
  • Help build business cases around the opportunity and get buy-in from stakeholders
  • Ensure appropriate data/tools/dashboards to measure execution and enable deeper analysis
  • Track execution and report up in regular updates
  • Work with product, data/BI team and IT to create data resources and build appropriate reporting
  • Work with Content team leads to understand their business needs and identify opportunities in terms of team actions prioritization and focus.
  • Use multiple data sources to report Content projects insights and impact

Do you want to help build smart, scalable, and sustainable data solutions that truly make a difference—for both society and individuals? At Fujitsu, we combine our Japanese corporate culture, where people are at the centre, with the power of technology to create tangible value in the world. We are now looking for an experienced Data Engineer to join our growing Data & AI team.

As a Data Engineer at Fujitsu, you will have the opportunity to work with some of Sweden’s most exciting companies, government agencies, and municipalities. You will build modern data platforms, data lakes, and pipelines—often in the cloud—and become a key contributor to our customers’ data-driven journeys. You will work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders, helping to develop solutions that are robust, scalable, and business-critical.
We are looking for someone who not only enjoys coding pipelines but also has a holistic view of data architecture, performance, quality, and security.

Working as a consultant at Fujitsu requires you to be proactive, take responsibility, and work both independently and as part of a team. You have a strong customer focus and a clear business mindset, and you understand the context in which you operate. You thrive in social environments, are attentive, analytical, flexible, and good at networking. You have strategic insight and see the opportunities that digitalisation brings. You enjoy taking responsibility and can work both independently and collaboratively.
As our future Data Engineer, you will:
Act as an advisor to our customers regarding data infrastructure, integration, and best practices
Design and develop robust data pipelines and ETL/ELT workflows in cloud environments (primarily Microsoft Azure/Fabric—both cloud and on-premises
column2: experience with AWS, GCP, or Databricks is a plus)

column1: Architect and implement data lakes, data warehouses, and modelling layers that enable analytics, dashboards, and machine learning solutions
Work hands-on with Python (or similar), SQL, and tools such as Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse, Snowflake, or similar
Adapt to different customers and industries by understanding their data needs and tailoring solutions accordingly
Collaborate closely with BI developers, analysts, data scientists, and business stakeholders
Apply best practices in data quality, data governance, and security across different environments
Contribute to internal knowledge sharing and mentor junior engineers when needed

You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Systems Development, Engineering, or a related field, or have equivalent work experience, and have at least 4 years of experience working as a Data Engineer or analytical engineer.
Experience building modern data solutions in cloud environments such as Azure, AWS, or GCP
Strong experience working with ETL/ELT processes and integrating data from multiple sources
Experience with Microsoft Fabric and/or Azure Synapse, Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Lake, and Delta Lake
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL
Experience with data warehouse platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, Azure, BigQuery, or Redshift
Understanding of data modelling techniques such as dimensional modelling or Data Vault
Experience with version control tools such as Git
Strong communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts clearly in a business-oriented way
Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively in customer-facing environments
Fluency in Swedish and English, both spoken and written
A proactive mindset, strong customer focus, and the ability to take ownership and responsibility
It is considered a strong merit if you:
Have experience with Power BI, DAX, and Power Query M
Hold certifications in Azure, Microsoft Fabric, or other relevant technologies
know about data governance and data security
Have experience working in agile environments such as Scrum or Kanban
Have experience with CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and DevOps tools
Have experience working closely with analytics, BI, or machine learning teams
What is in it for you?

You will take on a strategically important consulting role where you will help customers design and implement modern data platforms and become truly data-driven organisations. You will work alongside experienced colleagues in Data & AI, consulting, and delivery, with access to continuous learning, flexible ways of working, and exciting projects across multiple industries. Competitive salary, strong benefits, and the opportunity to grow your career in a global technology leader are included.
Shape your world and achieve together

We want the best people on our team, so we welcome and encourage applications from people with a diverse variety of experiences, backgrounds, and identities. We are committed to equality of opportunity for all. If you want to work alongside people with different ideas, in an inclusive business that will welcome you and support you, come and co-create the future at Fujitsu.

Since being founded in Japan in 1935, we have continued to demonstrate our technological prowess and the persistent pursuit of innovation. As a world-leading digital transformation partner, our business structure is aligned with the modern digital world. We use our experience and the power of IT to shape the future of society with our customers.

Now, we are doing it again, building the next generation of banking in the cloud. We believe our success is rooted in our ability to leverage data and AI at scale, but more importantly, in the people who build it.
As a Data Engineer at Nordnet, you aren't just building pipelines
column2: you’re shaping how millions of people interact with their financial future. You’ll sit at the intersection of platform engineering, automation, and customer experience.

column1: While we tackle high-volume event tracking and intelligent AI workflows, how we work is just as important as what we build. We believe that the best technical solutions come from collective thinking, which is why we lean heavily into pair and mob programming. You won't be an island here
column2: you’ll have a team that has your back.

column1: Platform Innovation: Develop robust data integrations and infrastructure to drive engagement and platform automation.
Event-Driven Tracking: Maintain and evolve our tracking and event infrastructure that processes millions of daily signals to create a seamless, inspiring user journey.
AI & Agentic Systems: Building the tools and intelligence that turn complex data into real-time insights and automated customer experiences.
Data Mesh & Modeling: Use dbt and BigQuery to create high-quality data products that the whole organization can rely on.
Collaborative Problem Solving: Partner with Data Scientists, Product teams, and business partners to translate complex requirements into elegant, simple technical solutions.

You’ll join a collaborative community of Data Engineers, Analysts, and Scientists who are all working toward the same goal: making Nordnet a truly data-driven bank. We value a methodical, thoughtful approach that supports both the team’s healthy climate and our ability to deliver. We think you’ll fit right in if:
You have a professional background in building production-grade systems. We aren't hung up on titles
column2: if you have a strong grasp of software patterns and have worked extensively with data flows or backend systems, you'll have the foundation we're looking for.

column1: You take pride in the quality of your work and feel responsible for the team’s collective success.
You enjoy writing maintainable Python and SQL and care deeply about building reliable systems.
You bring a solid understanding of data modeling principles from your experience in data-centric roles.
You value solving problems through discussion and thrive in an environment of shared learning.
Skills You Have Or Will Grow With Us
Working with Google Cloud Platform, dbt, or Terraform.
Navigating event tracking or high-volume data ingestion.
Exploring MarTech architecture and customer platform data flows.
Experimenting with LLMs to build internal tools.
We Are Committed To Your Continuous Growth, Offering a Space Where You Can Learn, Contribute, And Take Full Ownership Of Your Technical Domain Over Time. At Nordnet, We Offer
We believe high-quality engineering requires balance. We offer a hybrid work model that gives you focused flexibility at home and creative collaboration in the office.
A 5,000 SEK wellness contribution (friskvårdsbidrag) and access to health resources.
A robust package including collective agreements (kollektivavtal), occupational pension (BTP), and comprehensive insurance to give you peace of mind.
Your work directly influences the financial well-being of millions of users across the Nordics.
At Nordnet, we know that the best products are built by teams with a mix of different backgrounds and perspectives.

If you are excited about the challenges we’re solving and see yourself contributing to our team culture, we’d love to hear from you. We prioritize core engineering principles and a collaborative mindset, so if your experience aligns with the spirit of the role, let’s start a conversation.
To ensure a fair and streamlined process for everyone, we only accept applications through our recruitment system. We interview continuously, so feel free to reach out as soon as possible!
Please note: Nordnet conducts credit and background checks on the final candidate as part of our standard process for a digital bank.
We look forward to meeting you!

Nordnet redefined the financial world as the first digital bank in Europe. Now, we are doing it again, building the next generation of banking in the cloud. We believe our success is rooted in our ability to leverage data and AI at scale, but more importantly, in the people who build it.

The Role: Engineering for Impact

As a Data Engineer at Nordnet, you aren't just building pipelines
column2:
you’re shaping how millions of people interact with their financial future. You’ll sit at the intersection of platform engineering, automation, and customer experience.

While we tackle high-volume event tracking and intelligent AI workflows, how we work is just as important as what we build. We believe that the best technical solutions come from collective thinking, which is why we lean heavily into pair and mob programming. You won't be an island here
column3:
you’ll have a team that has your back.

What We Do Together

  • Platform Innovation: Develop robust data integrations and infrastructure to drive engagement and platform automation.
  • Event-Driven Tracking: Maintain and evolve our tracking and event infrastructure that processes millions of daily signals to create a seamless, inspiring user journey.
  • AI &
column4:
Agentic Systems: Building the tools and intelligence that turn complex data into real-time insights and automated customer experiences.
  • Data Mesh &
column5:
Modeling: Use dbt and BigQuery to create high-quality data products that the whole organization can rely on.
  • Collaborative Problem Solving: Partner with Data Scientists, Product teams, and business partners to translate complex requirements into elegant, simple technical solutions.

    • Who you are

    You’ll join a collaborative community of Data Engineers, Analysts, and Scientists who are all working toward the same goal: making Nordnet a truly data-driven bank. We value a methodical, thoughtful approach that supports both the team’s healthy climate and our ability to deliver. We think you’ll fit right in if:

    • You have a professional background in building production-grade systems. We aren't hung up on titles
column6:
if you have a strong grasp of software patterns and have worked extensively with data flows or backend systems, you'll have the foundation we're looking for.
  • You take pride in the quality of your work and feel responsible for the team’s collective success.
  • You enjoy writing maintainable Python and SQL and care deeply about building reliable systems.
  • You bring a solid understanding of data modeling principles from your experience in data-centric roles.
  • You value solving problems through discussion and thrive in an environment of shared learning.

    • Skills You Have Or Will Grow With Us

    • Working with Google Cloud Platform, dbt, or Terraform.
    • Navigating event tracking or high-volume data ingestion.
    • Exploring MarTech architecture and customer platform data flows.
    • Experimenting with LLMs to build internal tools.

    What We Offer

    We Are Committed To Your Continuous Growth, Offering a Space Where You Can Learn, Contribute, And Take Full Ownership Of Your Technical Domain Over Time. At Nordnet, We Offer

    • We believe high-quality engineering requires balance. We offer a hybrid work model that gives you focused flexibility at home and creative collaboration in the office.
    • A 5,000 SEK wellness contribution (friskvårdsbidrag) and access to health resources.
    • A robust package including collective agreements (kollektivavtal), occupational pension (BTP), and comprehensive insurance to give you peace of mind.
    • Your work directly influences the financial well-being of millions of users across the Nordics.

    Application

    At Nordnet, we know that the best products are built by teams with a mix of different backgrounds and perspectives.

    If you are excited about the challenges we’re solving and see yourself contributing to our team culture, we’d love to hear from you. We prioritize core engineering principles and a collaborative mindset, so if your experience aligns with the spirit of the role, let’s start a conversation.

    To ensure a fair and streamlined process for everyone, we only accept applications through our recruitment system. We interview continuously, so feel free to reach out as soon as possible!

    Please note: Nordnet conducts credit and background checks on the final candidate as part of our standard process for a digital bank.

    We look forward to meeting you!

Allshares is a fast-growing SaaS company and a leading expert in equity and incentive design and management. We provide advanced advisory services, software, and reporting tools to assist over 900 organizations across 60+ countries design, communicate, and manage their employee reward and share-based incentive programs.

Our team of 250+ specialists are located in Finland, Sweden, Switzerland, Portugal, France and the United States. We’re dedicated to tailoring the desired incentive models for our clients as well as making compensation strategies simpler and more effective for companies of all sizes.

As a DataOps Specialist you will be joining our Analytics team based in Stockholm. You will own the transformation of our data landscape by designing our infrastructure, maintaining its integrity and continuously evolving the system as we grow.

In your work you will be responsible to build and maintain automated ETL processes to ensure data flows seamlessly and accurately, take full responsibility for the health, structure, and performance of the database, create easy-to-use views or tools that allow our team of consultants to query and analyze data without needing deep technical expertise, and develop a clear data dictionary so the team understands exactly what data is available.

You will work closely with our Analytics Business Unit and actively participate in various development projects. The work is mainly operational, however, you will also have the ability to plan and lead development initiatives to improve the database.
We are looking for someone at the start of their career who enjoys turning complexity into clarity and has a burning ambition to build systems that can make a true business impact.

You are a fast learner with a practical, hands-on mindset and the confidence to challenge current workflows by proposing better solutions. You thrive in a fast-paced environment where managing multiple priorities is key. You possess strong, solution-driven problem-solving skills, allowing you to navigate technical challenges with ease.
To Succeed in this role you must have
Expertise in Python, SQL and PowerBI
Ability to work effectively and with attention to detail
Experience from Google Suite (primarily Google Sheets)
Familiarity with modern ELT and orchestration workflows
An hourly salary ranging from 170 to 230 SEK (depending on experience)
Inclusive international culture and working environment
Great possibility to develop yourself in the role along with the company’s growth journey

The role is a full-time Summer Internship (minimum 8 weeks) with the possibility of extension into a part-time position during the Autumn. The position will be an integral part in supporting our Analytics Business Unit with maintaining a constant flow of data for our consultants to leverage into actionable insights.
Does this position seem like the perfect fit for you? Send in your application today!
We are looking forward to hearing from you. To apply for this position, please submit your application here on LinkedIn.
The application deadline is April 30th, but selection is ongoing.
If you have questions about the position, please reach out to careers@allshares.com.

4410564670,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analytiker-inom-kravanalys-och-verksamhetsutveckling-at-arbetsf%C3%B6rmedlingen-4410564670?position=46&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=ezyBUiwWfU%2F3qBbBz%2Fpd4Q%3D%3D,Analytiker inom kravanalys och verksamhetsutveckling,Arbetsförmedlingen,"Solna, Stockholm County, Sweden","Har du förmåga att översätta data och verksamhetsbehov till tydliga krav – och vill bidra till lösningar som gör verklig nytta? Hos oss får du kombinera din analytiska förmåga med tvärfunktionellt samarbete i ett uppdrag med stort utvecklingsfokus. Tillsammans får vi Sverige i arbete. Vill du bli en av oss?

Arbetsmarknadsavdelningen inom verksamhetsområde Samordnat stöd ansvarar för att leda och samordna verksamhetsutveckling – med eller utan IT-innehåll. Vi driver utvecklingsinitiativ med fokus på att samla in, strukturera och prioritera verksamhetens behov, och är just nu i en större omställning där kärnverksamheten tar ett tydligare ansvar i utvecklingsarbetet.
Hos oss får du vara en nyckelperson i ett växande team där vi arbetar målinriktat och agilt för att utveckla effektiva och träffsäkra lösningar.

Som analytiker arbetar du med att samla in, bearbeta och visualisera data som grund för att formulera krav och vägleda utveckling. Du tar fram underlag för beslut och omsätter insikter till konkreta behov i samverkan med andra roller – såsom verksamhetsutvecklare, produktägare och analytiker.

Du arbetar både strategiskt och operativt: du följer upp effekter av genomförda förändringar, bidrar till prioriteringar och säkerställer att utvecklingsarbetet grundas i rätt behov. Arbetet sker i nära samarbete med olika delar av verksamheten, och du är en drivande kraft i att utveckla Arbetsförmedlingens förmåga till kravanalys.

Som person är du professionell och engagerad. Du är kvalitetsmedveten, noggrann och har förmåga och intresse av att hitta nya lösningar när det uppstår ett förändrat behov. Du kan växla mellan att se till både detaljer och helheter och kan tolka och förklara avancerade sammanhang på ett lättbegripligt sätt.
Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid som kan komma att inledas med sex månaders provanställning. Placeringsorten är Solna.

Vi erbjuder dig möjligheten att arbeta på en arbetsplats där vi värdesätter balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Du får tillgång till ett generöst semesteravtal, flexibla arbetstider samt möjlighet att arbeta på distans upp till 49 procent på årsbasis. Hos oss ingår även friskvård under arbetstid och ett förmånligt friskvårdsbidrag.

Som anställd på Arbetsförmedlingen har du också tillgång till kompetensutveckling och karriärmöjligheter inom myndigheten. Genom vårt arbete bidrar vi till ett inkluderande och hållbart samhälle, där du får möjlighet att göra en verklig skillnad för individer och för samhället i stort.
Mer om att jobba hos oss
Universitets-/högskoleutbildning om lägst 180 hp inom relevant område
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av att bearbeta och analysera större datamängder
Erfarenhet av att ta fram och presentera analyser eller beslutsunderlag muntligt och/eller skriftligt till olika målgrupper
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med användarflöden
Mycket goda kunskaper att kommunicera på svenska i tal och skrift samt god läsförståelse
Erfarenhet av att arbeta i större organisationer (ex. myndighet eller koncern) med ansvar för analys, kravställning eller verksamhetsutveckling
Erfarenhet av att analysera verksamhetsdata och omsätta insikter till utvecklingsförslag eller beslutsunderlag
Erfarenhet av att arbeta i olika IT-verktyg

Den statliga värdegrunden guidar oss i vårt dagliga arbete. Som anställd hos oss förväntas du arbeta rättvist, opartiskt och med hög integritet, samt bidra till att upprätthålla och stärka förtroendet för vår myndighet.
Utöver detta har du personlig förmåga och fallenhet att arbeta i enlighet med Arbetsförmedlingens medarbetarkriterier.
Som medarbetare på Arbetsförmedlingen förväntas du vara:
Ansvarstagande – ta ansvar för ditt arbete, samarbeta och bidra till en god arbetsmiljö.
Handlingskraftig – ta initiativ, bidra till förbättringar och hantera förändringar.
Resultatfokuserad – planera, följa upp och bidra till verksamhetsnytta och resultat.
Låter det här som en tjänst för dig? Välkommen med din ansökan senast 2026 - 05 - 20.
Bifoga ditt CV där du specificerar årtal, månad och arbetsuppgifter för varje anställning, samt intyg som styrker kraven om utbildning och/eller arbetslivserfarenhet i din ansökan.

Det är viktigt att du besvarar urvalsfrågorna detaljerat och sanningsenligt, för att vi ska få en tydlig bild av din erfarenhet. Om efterfrågad information inte framgår riskerar du bli bortsållad i urvalet. Du behöver inte skicka in ett personligt brev.
Under rekryteringsprocessen kan urvalstester förekomma. Vi kommer att hålla dig informerad genom hela rekryteringsprocessen. Mer om vår rekryteringsprocess.
Har du skyddade personuppgifter kontaktar du den rekryteringspartner som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.

Vi arbetar aktivt för att vara en myndighet fri från diskriminering. Det är viktigt för oss att all kompetens tas tillvara och vi välkomnar alla sökande. Vi ser positivt på om du har kunskaper i något av våra nationella minoritetsspråk.

Välkommen till oss – vi jobbar för ett Sverige i arbete! Här arbetar du på regeringens uppdrag för en väl fungerande arbetsmarknad. Målet är att korta tiden i arbetslöshet genom att matcha arbetssökande med arbetsgivare. Våra insatser riktas i första hand till dem som står längst ifrån arbetsmarknaden och till de arbetsgivare som är öppna för att anställa dem med störst behov av stöd. Vi motverkar felaktiga utbetalningar och brott mot välfärdssystemen så att våra skattemedel används effektivt till det de är till för. Vi samarbetar med många andra aktörer och tillsammans hittar vi lösningar som gör skillnad - för ett Sverige i arbete.
Mer om vår organisation och vårat uppdrag

Har du förmåga att översätta data och verksamhetsbehov till tydliga krav – och vill bidra till lösningar som gör verklig nytta? Hos oss får du kombinera din analytiska förmåga med tvärfunktionellt samarbete i ett uppdrag med stort utvecklingsfokus. Tillsammans får vi Sverige i arbete. Vill du bli en av oss?



Vår arbetsplats

Arbetsmarknadsavdelningen inom verksamhetsområde Samordnat stöd ansvarar för att leda och samordna verksamhetsutveckling – med eller utan IT-innehåll. Vi driver utvecklingsinitiativ med fokus på att samla in, strukturera och prioritera verksamhetens behov, och är just nu i en större omställning där kärnverksamheten tar ett tydligare ansvar i utvecklingsarbetet.

Hos oss får du vara en nyckelperson i ett växande team där vi arbetar målinriktat och agilt för att utveckla effektiva och träffsäkra lösningar.
column2:
Om tjänsten

Som analytiker arbetar du med att samla in, bearbeta och visualisera data som grund för att formulera krav och vägleda utveckling. Du tar fram underlag för beslut och omsätter insikter till konkreta behov i samverkan med andra roller – såsom verksamhetsutvecklare, produktägare och analytiker.

Du arbetar både strategiskt och operativt: du följer upp effekter av genomförda förändringar, bidrar till prioriteringar och säkerställer att utvecklingsarbetet grundas i rätt behov. Arbetet sker i nära samarbete med olika delar av verksamheten, och du är en drivande kraft i att utveckla Arbetsförmedlingens förmåga till kravanalys.

Som person är du professionell och engagerad. Du är kvalitetsmedveten, noggrann och har förmåga och intresse av att hitta nya lösningar när det uppstår ett förändrat behov. Du kan växla mellan att se till både detaljer och helheter och kan tolka och förklara avancerade sammanhang på ett lättbegripligt sätt.
column3:
Vårt erbjudande

Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid som kan komma att inledas med sex månaders provanställning. Placeringsorten är Solna.

Vi erbjuder dig möjligheten att arbeta på en arbetsplats där vi värdesätter balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Du får tillgång till ett generöst semesteravtal, flexibla arbetstider samt möjlighet att arbeta på distans upp till 49 procent på årsbasis. Hos oss ingår även friskvård under arbetstid och ett förmånligt friskvårdsbidrag.

Som anställd på Arbetsförmedlingen har du också tillgång till kompetensutveckling och karriärmöjligheter inom myndigheten. Genom vårt arbete bidrar vi till ett inkluderande och hållbart samhälle, där du får möjlighet att göra en verklig skillnad för individer och för samhället i stort.
column4: Mer om att jobba hos oss
column6:
Grundläggande krav för tjänsten

  • Universitets-/högskoleutbildning om lägst 180 hp inom relevant område
  • Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av att bearbeta och analysera större datamängder
  • Erfarenhet av att ta fram och presentera analyser eller beslutsunderlag muntligt och/eller skriftligt till olika målgrupper
  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta agilt
  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta med användarflöden
  • Mycket goda kunskaper att kommunicera på svenska i tal och skrift samt god läsförståelse
column7:
Övriga meriter för tjänsten

  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta i större organisationer (ex. myndighet eller koncern) med ansvar för analys, kravställning eller verksamhetsutveckling
  • Erfarenhet av att analysera verksamhetsdata och omsätta insikter till utvecklingsförslag eller beslutsunderlag
  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta i olika IT-verktyg
column8: Den statliga värdegrunden guidar oss i vårt dagliga arbete. Som anställd hos oss förväntas du arbeta rättvist, opartiskt och med hög integritet, samt bidra till att upprätthålla och stärka förtroendet för vår myndighet.
column9: Mer om statliga värdegrunden
column10:
Utöver detta har du personlig förmåga och fallenhet att arbeta i enlighet med Arbetsförmedlingens medarbetarkriterier.

Som medarbetare på Arbetsförmedlingen förväntas du vara:

Ansvarstagande – ta ansvar för ditt arbete, samarbeta och bidra till en god arbetsmiljö.

Handlingskraftig – ta initiativ, bidra till förbättringar och hantera förändringar.

Resultatfokuserad – planera, följa upp och bidra till verksamhetsnytta och resultat.
column11:
Ansökan

Låter det här som en tjänst för dig? Välkommen med din ansökan senast 2026 - 05 - 20.

Bifoga ditt CV där du specificerar årtal, månad och arbetsuppgifter för varje anställning, samt intyg som styrker kraven om utbildning och/eller arbetslivserfarenhet i din ansökan.
column12:
Det är viktigt att du besvarar urvalsfrågorna detaljerat och sanningsenligt, för att vi ska få en tydlig bild av din erfarenhet. Om efterfrågad information inte framgår riskerar du bli bortsållad i urvalet. Du behöver inte skicka in ett personligt brev.

Under rekryteringsprocessen kan urvalstester förekomma. Vi kommer att hålla dig informerad genom hela rekryteringsprocessen. Mer om vår rekryteringsprocess.
column13:
Har du skyddade personuppgifter kontaktar du den rekryteringspartner som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.

Vi arbetar aktivt för att vara en myndighet fri från diskriminering. Det är viktigt för oss att all kompetens tas tillvara och vi välkomnar alla sökande. Vi ser positivt på om du har kunskaper i något av våra nationella minoritetsspråk.

Välkommen till oss – vi jobbar för ett Sverige i arbete! Här arbetar du på regeringens uppdrag för en väl fungerande arbetsmarknad. Målet är att korta tiden i arbetslöshet genom att matcha arbetssökande med arbetsgivare. Våra insatser riktas i första hand till dem som står längst ifrån arbetsmarknaden och till de arbetsgivare som är öppna för att anställa dem med störst behov av stöd. Vi motverkar felaktiga utbetalningar och brott mot välfärdssystemen så att våra skattemedel används effektivt till det de är till för. Vi samarbetar med många andra aktörer och tillsammans hittar vi lösningar som gör skillnad - för ett Sverige i arbete.

Mer om vår organisation och vårat uppdrag

4406083246,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/utredare-med-statistikkompetens-at-the-national-board-of-health-and-welfare-socialstyrelsen-4406083246?position=48&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=xJapVgO%2BK1%2BvFdahLyHSGg%3D%3D,Utredare med statistikkompetens,The National Board of Health and Welfare (Socialstyrelsen),"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Vill du utveckla hälsodataregister och statistik för uppföljning av svensk hälso- och sjukvård? Hälsodata befinner sig i en intressant och händelserik tid, inte minst i och med beredningen av en ny hälsodataregisterlag, införandet av nya kodverk och datadelning i Europa. Hos oss finns nu en spännande tjänst som omfattar varierande arbetsuppgifter inom produktion, förvaltning och utveckling av register och statistik, både inom myndigheten och i internationella samarbeten, inom områdena dödsorsaker, cancer och tandhälsa.
Tjänsten är placerad på Statistikavdelningen. Du arbetar i ett team som gemensamt ansvarar för att förvalta och utveckla register och statistik inom områdena cancer, dödsorsaker och tandhälsa.

Du kommer att arbeta med varierande arbetsuppgifter som rör utveckling av register, framtagning och publicering av statistik, internationella samarbeten inom Norden och EU, samt vid behov leda eller delta i regeringsuppdrag och egeninitierade projekt på myndigheten. I arbetet har du många interna och externa kontakter och du för löpande dialog med användare av register och statistik, t ex forskare, journalister, beslutsfattare, och allmänhet. Rollen och arbetsuppgifterna kommer att kunna formas efter din och teamets specifika kompetenser och intresseområden. Vi som blir dina kollegor eftersträvar att vara ett kompetent och engagerat team, som i nära samarbete och med hög grad av tillit driver arbetet framåt och skapar goda förutsättningar för personlig utveckling.

För att komma till din rätt i den här rollen är det viktigt att du har god kommunikations- och samarbetsförmåga och att du är bekväm med att representera Socialstyrelsen både i nationella och internationella sammanhang. Du behöver även vara driven, lösningsorienterad, analytisk och nyfiken. Eftersom arbetsuppgifterna är varierande behöver du kunna skifta fokus mellan detaljorienterat arbete och att kunna lyfta blicken och se helhetsperspektivet. Vid rekryteringen kommer vi att lägga stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
erfarenhet av arbete inom de områden som beskrivs ovan, tex. från forskning, statistik eller arbete

goda metodologiska kunskaper inom epidemiologi och statistik
flerårig erfarenhet av att behandla och analysera data med hjälp av statistisk programmering

Våren 2027 flyttar myndigheten till nyrenoverade lokaler i Solna strand. Din tjänstetitel blir utredare och din placering är på statistikavdelningen, enheten för statistik 2.

När du ansöker får du besvara ett antal urvalsfrågor som är kopplade till kravprofilen i annonsen. Dina svar används som en del i urvalsarbetet och kan avgöra om du går vidare i rekryteringsprocessen eller inte. Det är därför viktigt att du svarar så utförligt och tydligt som möjligt. Vi använder inte det personliga brevet vid urvalet, därför bifogar du endast ditt CV.

4406966761,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-eqt-group-4406966761?position=51&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=myamByJmu9eG2UUMQMHPXg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,EQT Group,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Join EQT as a Data Engineer in the Client Tech Value Stream, where you will build and scale our modern data platform. You will work across the full data lifecycle, enabling reliable, high-quality data products that support decision-making across the business.
About The Client Tech Value Stream Team

The Client Tech Value Stream focuses on delivering technology and data solutions that support client, investor, and fund-related processes. The team works closely with engineering, product, CRM, and business stakeholders to build scalable platforms that enable insights and operational efficiency across EQT.

You will play a key role in designing, building, and operating EQT’s cloud-based data platform. You will work across ingestion, transformation, and data modeling to ensure high-quality, reliable, and accessible data.
Design, build, and operate scalable data pipelines across multiple data sources.
Ingest data from APIs, external platforms, Fivetran connectors, and cloud storage.
Develop and maintain data models using dbt on Snowflake.
Structure data around core business entities such as clients, investors, funds, and transactions.
Enable analytics and reporting in tools such as Power BI, Salesforce CRM Analytics, Tableau and our AI stack.
Monitor and troubleshoot pipelines to ensure reliability and performance.
Collaborate with stakeholders across engineering, analytics, CRM, and business teams.
Contribute to platform improvements and modernization of legacy pipelines.
Support infrastructure and access management using GitHub, Terraform, and Snowflake.

You are an experienced Data Engineer who enjoys working across the full data stack and collaborating with diverse teams. You are comfortable working in a modern cloud environment and translating business needs into scalable data solutions. You are curious about AI and new technology, and can translate both human and machine needs into code.
Experience building data pipelines and working with cloud data platforms.
Experience collaborating with nontechnical stakeholders for requirements and maintenance.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data modeling for usage by reporting tools and LLM models.
Experience with Snowflake and dbt or similar tools.
Experience integrating data from multiple systems such as APIs and SaaS platforms.
Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Experience with version control and infrastructure tools such as Git and Terraform.
Experience working with CRM systems such as Salesforce.
Understanding of financial or private markets data.

You will be part of a collaborative and forward-looking team where data is central to how EQT creates value. We offer opportunities to work with modern technologies, contribute to impactful projects, and grow your skills in a global environment.

Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness
column2: We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.

column1: EQT is a purpose-driven global investment organization focused on active ownership strategies. With a Nordic heritage and a global mindset, EQT has a track record of over three decades of developing companies across multiple geographies, sectors and strategies. EQT has investment strategies covering all phases of a business’ development, from start-up to maturity. EQT has EUR ‌ 270 billion in total assets under management (EUR ‌ ‌141 billion in fee-generating assets under management), within two business segments – Private Capital and Real Assets.

With its roots in the Wallenberg family’s entrepreneurial mindset and philosophy of long-term ownership, EQT is guided by a set of strong values and a distinct corporate culture. EQT manages and advises funds and vehicles that invest across the world with the mission to future-proof companies, generate attractive returns and make a positive impact with everything EQT does. EQT has offices in more than 25 countries across Europe, Asia and the Americas and has more than 1,900 employees.
Follow EQT on LinkedIn, X, YouTube and Instagram

Join EQT as a Data Engineer in the Client Tech Value Stream, where you will build and scale our modern data platform. You will work across the full data lifecycle, enabling reliable, high-quality data products that support decision-making across the business.

About The Client Tech Value Stream Team

The Client Tech Value Stream focuses on delivering technology and data solutions that support client, investor, and fund-related processes. The team works closely with engineering, product, CRM, and business stakeholders to build scalable platforms that enable insights and operational efficiency across EQT.

About The Role

You will play a key role in designing, building, and operating EQT’s cloud-based data platform. You will work across ingestion, transformation, and data modeling to ensure high-quality, reliable, and accessible data.

  • Design, build, and operate scalable data pipelines across multiple data sources.
  • Ingest data from APIs, external platforms, Fivetran connectors, and cloud storage.
  • Develop and maintain data models using dbt on Snowflake.
  • Structure data around core business entities such as clients, investors, funds, and transactions.
  • Enable analytics and reporting in tools such as Power BI, Salesforce CRM Analytics, Tableau and our AI stack.
  • Monitor and troubleshoot pipelines to ensure reliability and performance.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders across engineering, analytics, CRM, and business teams.
  • Contribute to platform improvements and modernization of legacy pipelines.
  • Support infrastructure and access management using GitHub, Terraform, and Snowflake.

About You

You are an experienced Data Engineer who enjoys working across the full data stack and collaborating with diverse teams. You are comfortable working in a modern cloud environment and translating business needs into scalable data solutions. You are curious about AI and new technology, and can translate both human and machine needs into code.

Essential skills and experience:

  • Experience building data pipelines and working with cloud data platforms.
  • Experience collaborating with nontechnical stakeholders for requirements and maintenance.
  • Strong SQL skills and experience with data modeling for usage by reporting tools and LLM models.
  • Experience with Snowflake and dbt or similar tools.
  • Experience integrating data from multiple systems such as APIs and SaaS platforms.
  • Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
  • Experience with version control and infrastructure tools such as Git and Terraform.

Nice to have:

  • Experience with GCP and Fivetran.
  • Experience working with CRM systems such as Salesforce.
  • Understanding of financial or private markets data.

What We Offer

You will be part of a collaborative and forward-looking team where data is central to how EQT creates value. We offer opportunities to work with modern technologies, contribute to impactful projects, and grow your skills in a global environment.

Inclusion at EQT

Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing &
column2: engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness
column3:
We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.

About EQT

EQT is a purpose-driven global investment organization focused on active ownership strategies. With a Nordic heritage and a global mindset, EQT has a track record of over three decades of developing companies across multiple geographies, sectors and strategies. EQT has investment strategies covering all phases of a business’ development, from start-up to maturity. EQT has EUR ‌ 270 billion in total assets under management (EUR ‌ ‌141 billion in fee-generating assets under management), within two business segments – Private Capital and Real Assets.

With its roots in the Wallenberg family’s entrepreneurial mindset and philosophy of long-term ownership, EQT is guided by a set of strong values and a distinct corporate culture. EQT manages and advises funds and vehicles that invest across the world with the mission to future-proof companies, generate attractive returns and make a positive impact with everything EQT does. EQT has offices in more than 25 countries across Europe, Asia and the Americas and has more than 1,900 employees.

More info: www.eqtgroup.com

Follow EQT on LinkedIn, X, YouTube and Instagram

4408023706,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-air-apps-4408023706?position=53&pageNum=0&refId=T5Jd1IqN8%2FRjcDM4H8vVmw%3D%3D&trackingId=rT8lNnaZ%2BPbCLm7ns2UklA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Air Apps,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","About Air Apps

At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.

Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.

As a Data Analyst at Air Apps, you will be responsible for analyzing and interpreting data to drive business insights and optimize product performance. You will create reports, dashboards, and performance metrics to help teams make informed decisions and improve user experience.
This role requires a data-driven mindset, strong analytical skills, and experience with visualization tools to turn complex data into actionable insights.

This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.
Analyze user behavior, product performance, and key business metrics to identify trends and opportunities.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Google Data Studio.
Collect, clean, and structure large datasets to ensure accuracy and usability.
Define and track KPIs for marketing, product, and business performance.
Work with SQL, Python, or R to extract, analyze, and manipulate data.
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and marketing teams to provide data-driven insights.
Conduct A/B testing and experiment analysis to improve product features and user engagement.
Identify bottlenecks and opportunities in user journeys and customer funnels.
Present findings in a clear and compelling way to stakeholders and leadership teams.
Stay up to date with industry trends, analytics best practices, and emerging data tools.
Around 3+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL for data querying and manipulation.
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Google Data Studio).
Strong analytical skills with experience in Python or R for data analysis.
Knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis.
Experience with Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Amplitude, or similar user analytics tools.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Experience working with large datasets and cloud-based data warehouses (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake).
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipeline optimization is a plus.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, data-driven environment and manage multiple projects.
Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.

At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.

At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

  • This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.

  • Analyze user behavior, product performance, and key business metrics to identify trends and opportunities.
  • Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Google Data Studio.

Requirements

  • Strong analytical skills with experience in Python or R for data analysis.
  • Knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis.

Join Sweden's largest property portal to design the data solutions that power our business decisions.

As the most popular real estate portal in Sweden, Hemnet receives over 40 million visits every month from people looking for their next home. We are a data-heavy organization, and we strive to make data-informed decisions the standard for everything we do.

As an Analytics Engineer, you will be a key player in our journey to empower the organization to become more data-driven. Sitting within the Analytics team, you will act as a vital bridge between raw data and meaningful business logic. Because this role connects technical infrastructure with business needs, strong communication is essential.

By focusing on the ""back end"" of business intelligence to ensure our data is clean, reliable, and well-modeled, you will play a crucial part in helping Hemnet continuously improve and become better for everyone.
Build and develop data models, taking responsibility for structuring the data that forms the foundation for analysis, reporting, and future AI solutions.
Conduct analysis and ad-hoc explorations to answer complex business questions, helping stakeholders across Hemnet turn raw data into insights.
Improve our self-service capabilities, enabling more colleagues to find answers independently through robust data architecture.
Occasionally support the development of essential reports and dashboards to ensure data is visualized effectively.

In order to be successful in this role we believe that you have an educational background with a strong technical focus and a few years of experience working as an Analytics Engineer, Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Developer. As a bridge between technical and business teams, you excel at tailoring your communication and insights for everyone from Sales and Business Development to the leadership group.
Strong SQL and sound judgement in data modelling.
Passionate about using your data models to solve real-world business problems alongside your stakeholders.
Experience partnering across departments and strong business acumen.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Swedish.
Bonus if you have experience in:

My name is Thomas Hanusek. I joined Hemnet about four years ago. Prior to that, I drove analytics for more than 20 years across different companies. My passion at work is really about seeing how Data & analytics can drive product and business development in the best way possible.

As a leader, I am a huge fan of seeing how a team can come together and, through their different skills, create magic. My aim is to be supportive and humble, and to create an environment where you feel trusted, challenged, and empowered to grow.

At Hemnet, we believe that diversity in gender, sexual orientation, ability, neurodiversity, cultural background and life experience makes us stronger. We welcome applicants of all identities and backgrounds.
If you meet many but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value curiosity, a strong sense of purpose and the motivation to learn and grow, alongside your skills and experiences.

Our office in central Stockholm is the true hub of our company culture. We are proudly an office-first team because we deeply value face-to-face collaboration and believe our best work happens when we are together. While we understand the need for occasional flexibility, we strongly prioritize in-person teamwork over remote work. Because our office is the center of our daily operations, it is essential that you are based in or near Stockholm.
Please note that we're unable to provide relocation assistance for this opportunity.

Microsoft Specialised - Senior Data Engineer


Looking for a role where you can shape meaningful projects, grow your skills, and actually enjoy the people you work with?


We’re working with a specialist Microsoft data consultancy in Sweden that blends the best of both worlds:


“The opportunities of the big company. The thoughtfulness of the small company.”


They’re part of a larger tech group, which gives them access to a broad client base and long-term projects across industries like finance, defence, public sector, and retail. But day-to-day, you’ll be part of a close-knit 45-person consultancy that values autonomy, collaboration, and community.


This team is known for its award-winning culture - consistently ranked among Europe’s best workplaces, based on employee feedback. They invest heavily in knowledge-sharing, competence development, and creating an environment where engineers genuinely enjoy their work.


🔍 The Role

  • Work on client projects where you’ll build and run data platforms from the ground up
  • Collaborate with experienced engineers in a high-trust, low-hierarchy environment
  • Focus on backend data engineering (not front-end BI/reporting)
  • Tech stack includes Python, PySpark, Databricks, CI/CD, orchestration, and Terraform
  • Microsoft Fabric experience is a bonus, but not essential


✅ What They’re Looking For

  • 3+ years of experience in data engineering (especially from long-term projects at major banks, insurers, or public sector clients)
  • Strong backend engineering skills and a solid understanding of modern data platforms
  • A collaborative mindset and eagerness to contribute to a knowledge-sharing culture
  • Curiosity, autonomy, and a desire to grow in a supportive, people-first environment


🌟 Why Join?

  • 7 weeks vacation!!!
  • Full flexibility in how much you work and what you learn - no cap on development
  • Regular conferences abroad, social events, and a strong internal community
  • A simple, human interview process - just a conversation to see if there’s a mutual fit
  • A team that genuinely enjoys working together and takes pride in doing great work


If you’re looking for a role that combines meaningful projects with a genuinely great place to do them, this could be the one.

About The Job

Mercor connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include Benchmark, General Catalyst, Peter Thiel, Adam D'Angelo, Larry Summers, and Jack Dorsey.

Position: STEM PhDs

Type: Contract

Compensation: $60–$80/hour

Location: Remote

Commitment: 15–20 hours/week

Role Responsibilities

  • Develop challenging problems in your domain to enhance AI model training.
  • Evaluate and improve AI model outputs through rigorous data analysis.
  • Collaborate with AI research teams to refine training data quality.
  • Work independently and asynchronously to meet project deadlines.
  • Shape the future of AI innovation with your domain expertise.


Qualifications

Must-Have

  • STEM PhD from a US, UK, Canadian, or European university.
  • High attention to detail.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
  • Excellent proficiency in English.


Start Date

  • April

LTM — a Larsen & Toubro company — is an AI-centric global technology services company and the Business Creativity partner to the world’s largest and most disruptive enterprises. We bring human insights and intelligent systems together to help clients create greater value at the intersection of technology and domain expertise. Our capabilities span integrated operations, transformation, and business AI — enabling new ways of working, new productivity paradigms, and new roads to value. Together with over 87,000 employees across 40 countries and our global network of partners, LTM owns business outcomes for our clients, helping them not just outperform the market, but to Outcreate it.
Seeking a Senior Specialist with 7 to 11 years of experience in Dimensional Data Modelling to design and optimise scalable data warehouse solutions

The resource will mainly work on analysis of the solutions being prepared for tactical or strategic purposes. The resource will also work on designing the different layers of the datastore, understanding the Business requirements and documenting them in the required format as solution design.
Creating HLMs and mapping documents, be a bridge between the Product owner and developers developing the solution.

Consultant will get to work in fast pace development project where heshe will be working with multi culture team with highly skilled and dedicated resources The consultant will also have an opportunity to learn and grow as an individual He/she will be responsible for framework design He/She will get to learn a lot about trending technologies like Hadoop and KAFKA The test work the consultant will perform will have direct impact on project deliveries so the inputs from the resource will be valuable for the team.

  2. Creating HLMs and mapping documents, be a bridge between the Product owner and developers developing the solution.
  3.   • Dimensional Data Modelling

Om rollen

Som trainee kommer du successivt att introduceras till redovisningsarbetet och få möjlighet att arbeta nära både kunder och kollegor. Du kommer att stötta i den löpande redovisningen samtidigt som du bygger upp din kompetens inom redovisning och rådgivning.

  • Avstämningar och rapportering

Do you want to help build smart, scalable, and sustainable data solutions that truly make a difference—for both society and individuals? At Fujitsu, we combine our Japanese corporate culture, where people are at the centre, with the power of technology to create tangible value in the world. We are now looking for an experienced Data Engineer to join our growing Data & AI team.

As a Data Engineer at Fujitsu, you will have the opportunity to work with some of Sweden’s most exciting companies, government agencies, and municipalities. You will build modern data platforms, data lakes, and pipelines—often in the cloud—and become a key contributor to our customers’ data-driven journeys. You will work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders, helping to develop solutions that are robust, scalable, and business-critical.
We are looking for someone who not only enjoys coding pipelines but also has a holistic view of data architecture, performance, quality, and security.

Working as a consultant at Fujitsu requires you to be proactive, take responsibility, and work both independently and as part of a team. You have a strong customer focus and a clear business mindset, and you understand the context in which you operate. You thrive in social environments, are attentive, analytical, flexible, and good at networking. You have strategic insight and see the opportunities that digitalisation brings. You enjoy taking responsibility and can work both independently and collaboratively.
As our future Data Engineer, you will:
Act as an advisor to our customers regarding data infrastructure, integration, and best practices
Design and develop robust data pipelines and ETL/ELT workflows in cloud environments (primarily Microsoft Azure/Fabric—both cloud and on-premises
column2: experience with AWS, GCP, or Databricks is a plus)

column1: Architect and implement data lakes, data warehouses, and modelling layers that enable analytics, dashboards, and machine learning solutions
Work hands-on with Python (or similar), SQL, and tools such as Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse, Snowflake, or similar
Adapt to different customers and industries by understanding their data needs and tailoring solutions accordingly
Collaborate closely with BI developers, analysts, data scientists, and business stakeholders
Apply best practices in data quality, data governance, and security across different environments
Contribute to internal knowledge sharing and mentor junior engineers when needed

You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Systems Development, Engineering, or a related field, or have equivalent work experience, and have at least 4 years of experience working as a Data Engineer or analytical engineer.
Experience building modern data solutions in cloud environments such as Azure, AWS, or GCP
Strong experience working with ETL/ELT processes and integrating data from multiple sources
Experience with Microsoft Fabric and/or Azure Synapse, Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Lake, and Delta Lake
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL
Experience with data warehouse platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, Azure, BigQuery, or Redshift
Understanding of data modelling techniques such as dimensional modelling or Data Vault
Experience with version control tools such as Git
Strong communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts clearly in a business-oriented way
Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively in customer-facing environments
Fluency in Swedish and English, both spoken and written
A proactive mindset, strong customer focus, and the ability to take ownership and responsibility
It is considered a strong merit if you:
Have experience with Power BI, DAX, and Power Query M
Hold certifications in Azure, Microsoft Fabric, or other relevant technologies
know about data governance and data security
Have experience working in agile environments such as Scrum or Kanban
Have experience with CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and DevOps tools
Have experience working closely with analytics, BI, or machine learning teams
What is in it for you?

You will take on a strategically important consulting role where you will help customers design and implement modern data platforms and become truly data-driven organisations. You will work alongside experienced colleagues in Data & AI, consulting, and delivery, with access to continuous learning, flexible ways of working, and exciting projects across multiple industries. Competitive salary, strong benefits, and the opportunity to grow your career in a global technology leader are included.
Shape your world and achieve together

We want the best people on our team, so we welcome and encourage applications from people with a diverse variety of experiences, backgrounds, and identities. We are committed to equality of opportunity for all. If you want to work alongside people with different ideas, in an inclusive business that will welcome you and support you, come and co-create the future at Fujitsu.

Since being founded in Japan in 1935, we have continued to demonstrate our technological prowess and the persistent pursuit of innovation. As a world-leading digital transformation partner, our business structure is aligned with the modern digital world. We use our experience and the power of IT to shape the future of society with our customers.

For the consultant/employee selected for the role, checks of public registers and records relating to financial and legal status will be conducted. By submitting an application, the candidate consents to such checks being carried out.
