4410188453,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/enhetschef-banarbetsplanering-at-f%C3%A4rjerederiet-4410188453?position=5&pageNum=2&refId=52Qq6%2BXhTCIqCui9ev6naw%3D%3D&trackingId=AqPpxvPBzeUCFOr%2B%2B36%2F%2FA%3D%3D,Enhetschef Banarbetsplanering,Färjerederiet,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Nu söker vi en enhetschef som vill ta ett helhetsgrepp om banarbetsplaneringen, med blicken riktad utåt, och med fokus på att få Trafikverket att fungera som en sammanhållen aktör. Hos oss leder du en ny enhet med uppdrag att stärka samverkan, utveckla arbetssätt och hantera kapacitetsutmaningar i nära dialog med både interna och externa parter. Det här är en roll för dig som vill kombinera ett tydligt verksamhetsansvar med ett ledarskap som skapar riktning, samsyn och resultat.
Nu söker vi dig som vill vara med och utveckla vår nya enhet och fortsätta din resa inom ledarskap!

Vi är en avdelning som växer och vi söker nu en chef till vår nya enhet som med sitt ledarskap, engagemang, resultatfokus och driv för utveckling och förbättring vill vara med på vår resa. Vi är en nationell avdelning inom verksamhetsområde Underhåll som stöttar hela Trafikverkets utförande verksamhet kopplat till banarbeten med medarbetare placerade i hela landet. Vi har det övergripande ansvaret för samordning, koordinering och planering av trafikpåverkande banarbeten.

I rollen som enhetschef för banarbetsplanering kommer du tillsammans med dina sektionschefer och medarbetare vidareutveckla vår verksamhet och ansvara för enhetens leveranser och resultat. Enheten ansvarar för den övergripande samordningen och koordineringen av trafikpåverkande åtgärder och banarbetsplanering för järnvägsprojekt. Vi stöttar verksamheten både nationellt och lokalt, till exempel med koordinering tider i spår för genomförande av järnvägsprojekt. Du leder enheten genom starkt personalengagemang, samarbete och samverkan med medarbetarna, chefskollegor och andra samt en nyfikenhet och drivkraft kring förbättringsarbete. Du har personal- och budgetansvar och ingår i avdelningens ledningsgrupp. Som chef ansvarar du även för att etablera god samverkan och effektiva samarbetsformer tvärfunktionellt inom Trafikverket.

Enheten består av två sektionschefer och cirka 30 engagerade medarbetare med stort leverans- och utvecklingsfokus. Som enhetschef kommer du att bidra till god arbetsmiljö och utöva ett närvarande ledarskap på distans. Medarbetarna inom enheten är placerade på ett flertal orter i hela landet.

Jag som blir din chef heter Markus Bohlin. Min syn på ledarskap bottnar i individers behov och drivkrafter samt förståelse för hur det egna arbetet bidrar till den samlade leveransen. Jag tror på att öppenhet, tolerans för olikheter samt delaktighet är framgångsfaktorer som skapar trygghet och bidrar till engagemang och motivation. Mina förväntningar på dig som chef är att du bidrar till en kultur som präglas av tillit, samarbete och helhetssyn.

Som chef i Trafikverket drivs du av att få människor att växa genom ett närvarande ledarskap. Du skapar tillit och bygger förtroende med din problemlösande och kommunikativa förmåga. Du levererar resultat och driver samhällsutvecklingen framåt genom delaktighet och engagemang. Du utmanar och ifrågasätter och skapar en kultur som främjar ständiga förbättringar. Att inspirera, klargöra riktning och vara mottaglig för nya möjligheter och lösningar är en förutsättning för att lyckas.

I denna rekrytering används webbaserade tester som en del i urvalet. Vi planerar att hålla digitala intervjuer den 16-17/6.

Aktuella placeringsorter är Luleå, Gävle, Borlänge, Västerås, Solna, Jönköping, Göteborg, Kristianstad eller Malmö. Vill du ha en flexibel vardag med möjlighet att arbeta hemifrån? I den här rollen kan du jobba på distans upp till två dagar i veckan.
Anställningen inom Trafikverket är tillsvidare men chefsförordnandet är tidsbegränsat, inledningsvis på 4 år.

Inom verksamhetsområde Underhåll ansvarar vi för leveransen av säkra och tillgängliga vägar och järnvägar. Genom att förvalta, underhålla och utveckla det befintliga och framtida väg- och järnvägssystemet lägger vi grunden för näringslivets godstransporter och miljoner människors resande - varje dag, året om. Kompetensutveckling står högt på vår agenda, oavsett vilken roll du har eller var du befinner dig i yrkeslivet. Hos oss gör du skillnad!

At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.

Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.

At Wolt, our Merchant Operations teams are at the heart of what we do, ensuring that all merchant partners are set up for success with a seamless onboarding, menu, and photo experience. As Wolt continues to grow in scale and scope, our merchant engine must evolve alongside it. That’s why we’ve built a dedicated Catalog team, responsible for managing all items displayed on our platform and within our internal catalog systems. If you’re passionate about structured data, automation, and enabling local growth through high-quality content, this is your opportunity to shape the foundation of Wolt’s retail and restaurant experience.

As a Catalog Associate you will manage product data in Wolt’s catalog, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and alignment with quality standards. You’ll create, review, and update partner assortments to reflect in-store offerings. In this role, you’ll work closely with internal and external stakeholders to ensure consistent data standards and contribute to high-quality catalog and assortment outcomes.
Create and maintain product data in Wolt’s catalog, including names, descriptions, prices, availability, images, and compliance details.
Ensure product information meets quality and compliance standards across retail offerings.
Create, review, and update partner assortments to reflect in-store offerings.
Work with large datasets in Google Sheets and leverage AI tools to efficiently manage and improve catalog quality.
Upload and manage media content, such as images and logos, to enhance the customer experience.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g., Product, Account Management, Sales) to improve data quality and processes.
Contribute to process improvements to enhance data accuracy and operational performance.
A positive and proactive mindset with strong problem solving skills. Seeing challenges as opportunities and taking ownership of your work.
Customer & quality-centric mindset, with a strong commitment to data accuracy and quality.
Comfortable working in Excel or Google Sheets.

Som lagermedarbetare arbetar du i Tamros produktion med framplockning och övrig hantering av varor som sedan levereras till apotek, sjukhus och till privata vårdgivare. Arbetsuppgifterna innebär tex. plock av varor där även arbete i kylrum förekommer. För att kunna arbeta på vissa avdelningar behöver du ha truckkort. Det är också viktigt att du har en god fysisk förmåga och trivs med ett aktivt arbete.

Vi söker dig som värdesätter samarbete och tycker om att arbeta på ett strukturerat sätt. Som person tar du stort ansvar, driver ditt arbete framåt och ser möjligheter. Du är fokuserad och noggrann i ditt arbete även i stressiga situationer.
Du behöver vara minst 18 år och ha gymnasiekompetens eller erfarenhet som vi bedömer likvärdig. För att klara jobbet behöver du behärska svenska språket i tal och skrift.
Det är meriterande om du har.

Vi erbjuder en dynamisk arbetsmiljö i en spännande bransch under ständig utveckling och med ett tydligt och livsviktigt uppdrag. Det finns flera möjligheter till utveckling inom vår koncern och du blir en del av ett viktigt företag som verkligen gör skillnad i samhället. Om du dessutom brinner för att identifiera och driva förbättringsarbete kommer du trivas hos oss!
Vi värdesätter mångfald och strävar efter att skapa en inkluderande arbetsplats där alla medarbetare kan växa och utvecklas.
Kollektivavtal finns med HAF Handelsanställdas Förbund Lagring & Distribution.
De anställningar som erbjuds är vikariat fram till 2026-09-30 med möjlighet till förlängning.

Om du känner igen dig i beskrivningen och blir nyfiken, tveka då inte att skicka in din ansökan! Observera att vi inte tar emot ansökningar via e-post. Vi genomför bakgrundskontroller på slutkandidater som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess, detta i syfte att säkerställa en trygg och säker arbetsmiljö för våra medarbetare. Intervjuer och tester görs löpande och tjänsterna kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag så vänta inte med din ansökan.
Då vi tagit beslut om rekryteringskanal för denna tjänst undanber vi oss vänligen direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt försäljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.

Bakom varje leverans från Tamro finns ett löfte – om trygghet, tillgänglighet och ansvar. Vi rör oss ofta i det tysta, men vårt arbete märks i många människors vardag, varje dag. I över 100 år har vi säkerställt att läkemedel ska finnas där de behövs, när de behövs. Med erfarenheten och innovation som grund och blicken ständigt framåt, bygger vi logistiklösningar som håller över tid. Det är vad vi kallar för ett livsviktigt uppdrag.

Tamro är en del av PHOENIX Group, en familjeägd europeisk koncern med 49 000 anställda i 29 länder och 214 distributionscentraler. I Sverige är vi ca 700 medarbetare och vi finns i Göteborg, Eskilstuna, Stockholm och Umeå.
Är du nyfiken, ansvarstagande och gillar att samarbeta? Då kanske ett vikariat på vårt lager i Stockholm vara något för dig!

4403199249,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/product-data-analyst-9-to-12-month-enabling-phase-se-at-strawberry-4403199249?position=8&pageNum=2&refId=52Qq6%2BXhTCIqCui9ev6naw%3D%3D&trackingId=rsYv6%2BVpQlpZO27Ym%2BmbbA%3D%3D,Product Data Analyst (9 to 12-month enabling phase) SE,Strawberry,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","We are Strawberry. With over 245 hotels, 120 restaurants, 20 spas and more, we create thousands of experiences every day. With urban Comfort Hotel®, warm Quality Hotel™, stylish Clarion Hotel® and cosy Home Hotel and more than 40 unique independent hotels, our guests can pick and choose. Our team of 20,000 passionate individuals from more than 120 countries is what makes us grow! Strawberry is built on our core values: energy, courage and enthusiasm.
Shape the Future of Hospitality at Strawberry

At Strawberry, we're not just a hotel company anymore – we're building a universe of experiences. We're transforming the hospitality landscape through innovation and technology, and 2023 marked a major milestone with the launch of our new mother brand, Strawberry. This exciting evolution creates countless opportunities for growth and impact, and we're looking for a passionate and skilled Product Data Analyst to join our Strawberry Data & Insights Team and help us shape the future of travel.

Our main mission is to establish a scalable foundation for how we work with product analytics across our tech product portfolio, spanning HQ operations, hotel operations, and our independent websites for our Luxury, Lifestyle & Resort division. As part of our team, you will establish frameworks, ways of working, and taxonomy structures that our teams can continue to build and execute on. This role is a crucial bridge between our Product Managers and their stakeholders, to make sure we build products that have the impact that we expect them to have, empowering the product teams to make better data-informed decisions independently.

As a Product Data Analyst, you'll play a crucial role in empowering Strawberry to become a truly data-driven organization. You'll be instrumental in developing and maintaining robust analytical solutions, supporting the full product development lifecycle, and moving us toward self-serve analytics.

Product Analytics & Insights: Support the full product development lifecycle by partnering with PMs to identify key levers, analyzing funnels and cohorts, and translating data into actionable recommendations.
Metrics & Reporting: Define and maintain product KPIs, OKRs, and success metrics, and build dashboards and automated reporting.
Data Quality: Improve and create new  event taxonomy, tracking documentation, and data quality standards, collaborating with Product and BI teams on semantic models.
Frameworks & Enablement: Establish scalable frameworks and ways of working for product analytics, including taxonomy structures and experimentation practices.
Culture Champion: Actively coach product teams in data-driven methods and metrics, contributing to a self-serve insights culture day-to-day.
Solid background in product analytics, including funnel analysis, cohort analysis, and behavioural modelling.
A product-minded approach that balances speed with rigour and communicates clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Proven ability to coach and enable PMs, designers, and engineers to work more data-driven independently.
Comfort navigating ambiguity and legacy systems while building toward a scalable future state.
Proficiency in web analytics and event-based models (primarily GA4) and expert-level tag management (e.g., Google Tag Manager).
Strong understanding of experimentation design and hands-on experience with A/B testing platforms.
Proficiency in PowerBI and experience building semantic models (including structuring them to be AI-ready).
Advanced SQL skills and familiarity with Cloud Data Warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery).
Bonus: Python or R experience, familiarity with dbt, and experience with AI products or ML-adjacent environments.

What We Offer: This is a 9- to 12-month role for now to get us started. Strawberry is in a transformation journey and we have to be careful about our long term commitments. We however want to invest in you and offer you:
Real Impact: Be a part of a dynamic transformation and contribute directly to our success story.
Empowerment: Work in a flat organization with trust, autonomy, and the opportunity to make a real difference.
Growth & Development: Access professional development opportunities to enhance your skills and advance your career.
Fun & Community: Enjoy a vibrant work environment with plenty of social events and team-building activities.
Fantastic Perks: Benefit from great employee discounts, including staff and friends & family rates at our hotels.
Travel included: This role begins with a 9 to 12-month contract with your base at our HQ in Solna, Stockholm or Oslo (some travel between locations expected).
Summer vacation: We have summer code freeze June 29th to August 3rd so we expect you to take 3-4 weeks vacation in that period.

4309270484,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/management-consultant-data-analytics-at-bearingpoint-4309270484?position=2&pageNum=5&refId=PRONnVWiMFOhOn4q5eh09w%3D%3D&trackingId=iIo71cTqZiLGqdsiVn1j4w%3D%3D,Management Consultant - Data & Analytics,BearingPoint,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Your role
Our Data & Analytics (D&A) team combine broad business understanding with technical expertise to help our clients make better data-driven decisions.

Expect to participate in projects ranging from advisory services to hands-on development of D&A solutions. We help our clients define a strategy, organize their D&A capabilities, and implement data platforms and analytical solutions. The analytical work ranges from business intelligence to Machine Learning and Generative AI.

Together with an ambitious and multidisciplinary team of colleagues, you will help our clients understand their current situation, risks, and opportunities and advise how they can use technology to solve their business needs. The type of projects will be varied, and you will have access to experts throughout our international community of consultants.

Your professional and personal growth will be supported by a Development Manager. As you deepen your expertise and grow as a consultant, you will take on greater responsibilities in our client engagements and our internal business activities. Such as team development, employer branding, developing new offerings, pitching to clients, and other activities.
What We Are Looking For In Our New Colleagues

You may have a background as a developer and now want to combine technical work with more business‑focused and strategic responsibilities. Alternatively, you may work in Consulting, have a strong technical interest, and want to deepen your expertise within data and AI.
Experience in data collection and analysis, along with a general understanding of business processes.
Familiarity with Business Intelligence solutions and Generative AI development, as well as programming proficiency in Python and SQL.
Some experience with Database design, ETL, and Data Visualization is also desirable.
Experience with Microsoft´s Data & Analytics tools is a plus (Fabric, PowerBI, SQL Server, Data Factory, etc.)
Ability to balance analytical rigor with practical, implementable recommendations.
Structured communication, adapting style to managers and operational teams and an ability to establish credibility quickly in new environments.
A master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or other relevant field.
Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint to support daily business activities.
Excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English, spoken and written.

BearingPoint is a great place to work. We can offer you the opportunity to work for a company with a global reach and a unique culture that allows everyone to reach their highest potential. We also offer team-specific competency development opportunities and employee benefits such as:
Professional and personal growth, supported by your Development Manager.
An international, dynamic, and inspiring work environment.
A steep learning curve in client-facing roles with a high impact on the business performance of our clients.
Competitive compensation and benefits package, including but not limited to an occupational pension, health insurance, wellness grants, expanded parental benefits, and more.
Competency development opportunities include our First 1000-day program and other Nordic & Firm-wide training and boot camps (Grow with BearingPoint).
An annual curriculum of various social events and sports activities.
Send us your resume in either Swedish or English, and we´ll get back to you shortly. A cover letter is optional, and Interviews will be held continuously.
During the interview process, you will meet various colleagues from the team and conduct case interviews.
Want to find out more about the team? Meet some of our colleagues here (Meet our Sweden team), or read an article with our D&A team lead Marcus Ehrndal here.

As part of our recruitment process, candidates may be subject to background checks. These checks help us ensure a safe and trustworthy workplace and may include verification of identity, employment history, education, and any relevant criminal records, in accordance with applicable laws.

  • Ability to balance analytical rigor with practical, implementable recommendations.

    • What We Offer

    BearingPoint is a great place to work. We can offer you the opportunity to work for a company with a global reach and a unique culture that allows everyone to reach their highest potential. We also offer team-specific competency development opportunities and employee benefits such as:

    • Professional and personal growth, supported by your Development Manager.
    • An international, dynamic, and inspiring work environment.
    • A steep learning curve in client-facing roles with a high impact on the business performance of our clients.
    • Competitive compensation and benefits package, including but not limited to an occupational pension, health insurance, wellness grants, expanded parental benefits, and more.
    • Competency development opportunities include our First 1000-day program and other Nordic &
column7:
Firm-wide training and boot camps (Grow with BearingPoint).
  • An annual curriculum of various social events and sports activities.

    • What Now?

    Send us your resume in either Swedish or English, and we´ll get back to you shortly. A cover letter is optional, and Interviews will be held continuously.

    During the interview process, you will meet various colleagues from the team and conduct case interviews.

    Want to find out more about the team? Meet some of our colleagues here (Meet our Sweden team), or read an article with our D&

4358754099,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-bi-engineer-at-sogeti-4358754099?position=3&pageNum=5&refId=PRONnVWiMFOhOn4q5eh09w%3D%3D&trackingId=5gVYR3DomaHFRioba8HXOA%3D%3D,Data & BI Engineer,Sogeti,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","På Sogeti tror vi att det bästa finns inom oss alla. Oavsett om du är i början av din karriär eller redan på toppen av ditt spel, kommer vi att uppmuntra dig att nå din fulla potential och bli bättre. Genom vår gemensamma passion för teknik, vår entreprenöriella kultur och vårt fokus på kontinuerligt lärande, kommer vi att ge dig allt du behöver för att göra ditt bästa arbete och bli den bästa versionen av dig själv.
Vi söker flera medarbetare som älskar att jobba med data från ax till limpa, dvs. från Data Engineering, pipelines och modellering till konkreta insikter och visualiseringar genom BI.

Vi behöver även fler seniora konsulter som vill utvecklas mot att bli Data Architects och Data platform Architects och som kan använda sin expertis inom Azure Data Services och Databricks för att skapa skalbara och säkra lösningar som driver kundernas digitala transformation. Här får du kombinera leverans och affären och forma lösningarna.
I den här rollen kommer du att ha en nyckelroll i:
Bygga och leverera lösningar för stora datamängder baserat på kundens behov.
Arbeta nära andra i teamet och hitta nya sätt att göra saker.
Se möjligheter där andra ser problem och trivas i en dynamisk miljö där det alltid händer något.
Nätverka med andra inom Sogeti för att ständligt lära dig nya saker
Arbeta självständigt inom ditt område, samtidigt som du är nyfiken på att lära av andra och bidra till gruppens kunskap.
Sätta upp och utveckla nya lösningar för datalager och pipelines.
Driva migrering från on-prem till molnet och hantera enorma datamängder.
Högskoleutbildning inom IT eller relevant erfarenhet från arbetslivet i minst 3 år.
Minst 3 års erfarenhet som Data eller BI Engineer.
Minst 3 års erfarenhet av arbete med Big Data och flera datakällor, varav åtminstone ett par år med Azure
Erfarenhet av teknologier inom Data och BI, t ex: SQL, Python, Spark, Azure Data Services, Power BI, Databricks, Snowflake, Unity Catalog för att bara nämna några.
Erfarenhet av arbete med ETL coh ELT från källa till landning, transform och modell.
Flytande i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.
Erfarenhet av arbete med CI/CD-pipelines (t.ex. Jenkins, Azure DevOps).
Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas:
Innovativ miljö: Hos oss får du chansen att jobba med den senaste tekniken och vara en del av ett dynamiskt team som älskar att skapa värde genom innovation.
Karriärutveckling: Vi bryr oss om din framtid och erbjuder utvecklingsprogram och certifieringar i de senaste teknologierna, så att du kan växa och bli den bästa versionen av dig själv.
Flexibelt arbete: Vi förstår att livet är mer än bara jobb. Därför erbjuder vi möjligheter till distansarbete och flexibla arbetstider för att du ska må bra och trivas.
Teamkänsla: Vi är som en stor familj med en härlig teamkänsla. Genom att leva efter våra värdeord som mod, passion, laganda och entreprenörskap - skapar vi en arbetsmiljö där alla kan trivas.
Vi välkomnar varmt din ansökan idag, urvalet sker löpande! Om du har frågor om tjänsten eller rekryteringsprocessen, vänligen kontakta Team Manager, Emma Hägg på emma.hagg@sogeti.se
Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov.

Sogeti Sverige, en del av Capgemini-koncernen, med 19 kontor och 1200 medarbetare, skapar affärsvärde med teknologi för organisationer som behöver införa innovation snabbt och som vill ha en lokal partner med global skalbarhet. Med en hands-on-kultur och närhet till kunderna, implementerar Sogeti lösningar som hjälper organisationer att arbeta snabbare, bättre och smartare. Genom att kombinera agilitet och snabb implementation via en DevOps-ansats, levererar Sogeti innovativa lösningar inom test- och kvalitetssäkring, molnet och systemutveckling, förstärkta av AI, data och automation.

På Sogeti tror vi att det bästa finns inom oss alla. Oavsett om du är i början av din karriär eller redan på toppen av ditt spel, kommer vi att uppmuntra dig att nå din fulla potential och bli bättre. Genom vår gemensamma passion för teknik, vår entreprenöriella kultur och vårt fokus på kontinuerligt lärande, kommer vi att ge dig allt du behöver för att göra ditt bästa arbete och bli den bästa versionen av dig själv.

4412935236,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analytics-engineer-at-hemnet-4412935236?position=5&pageNum=5&refId=PRONnVWiMFOhOn4q5eh09w%3D%3D&trackingId=SQULlRBZ7kyaBqPDCD8mcQ%3D%3D,Analytics Engineer,Hemnet,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Are you a skilled Analytics Engineer with a passion for transforming raw data into high-quality, actionable insights? Join Sweden's largest property portal to design the data solutions that power our business decisions.

As the most popular real estate portal in Sweden, Hemnet receives over 40 million visits every month from people looking for their next home. We are a data-heavy organization, and we strive to make data-informed decisions the standard for everything we do.

As an Analytics Engineer, you will be a key player in our journey to empower the organization to become more data-driven. Sitting within the Analytics team, you will act as a vital bridge between raw data and meaningful business logic. Because this role connects technical infrastructure with business needs, strong communication is essential.

By focusing on the ""back end"" of business intelligence to ensure our data is clean, reliable, and well-modeled, you will play a crucial part in helping Hemnet continuously improve and become better for everyone.
Build and develop data models, taking responsibility for structuring the data that forms the foundation for analysis, reporting, and future AI solutions.
Conduct analysis and ad-hoc explorations to answer complex business questions, helping stakeholders across Hemnet turn raw data into insights.
Improve our self-service capabilities, enabling more colleagues to find answers independently through robust data architecture.
Occasionally support the development of essential reports and dashboards to ensure data is visualized effectively.

In order to be successful in this role we believe that you have an educational background with a strong technical focus and a few years of experience working as an Analytics Engineer, Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Developer. As a bridge between technical and business teams, you excel at tailoring your communication and insights for everyone from Sales and Business Development to the leadership group.
Strong SQL and sound judgement in data modelling.
Passionate about using your data models to solve real-world business problems alongside your stakeholders.
Experience partnering across departments and strong business acumen.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Swedish.
Bonus if you have experience in:

My name is Thomas Hanusek. I joined Hemnet about four years ago. Prior to that, I drove analytics for more than 20 years across different companies. My passion at work is really about seeing how Data & analytics can drive product and business development in the best way possible.

As a leader, I am a huge fan of seeing how a team can come together and, through their different skills, create magic. My aim is to be supportive and humble, and to create an environment where you feel trusted, challenged, and empowered to grow.

At Hemnet, we believe that diversity in gender, sexual orientation, ability, neurodiversity, cultural background and life experience makes us stronger. We welcome applicants of all identities and backgrounds.
If you meet many but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value curiosity, a strong sense of purpose and the motivation to learn and grow, alongside your skills and experiences.

Our office in central Stockholm is the true hub of our company culture. We are proudly an office-first team because we deeply value face-to-face collaboration and believe our best work happens when we are together. While we understand the need for occasional flexibility, we strongly prioritize in-person teamwork over remote work. Because our office is the center of our daily operations, it is essential that you are based in or near Stockholm.
Please note that we're unable to provide relocation assistance for this opportunity.

4405135441,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-northmill-bank-4405135441?position=6&pageNum=5&refId=PRONnVWiMFOhOn4q5eh09w%3D%3D&trackingId=5s%2BRMVlDETrkksk%2BTJ01dA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Northmill Bank,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Northmill Bank is a challenger bank at the intersection of technology and finance, committed to revolutionizing the way people manage and protect their financial well-being. We are creating a different kind of banking experience, digital yet personal.

Northmill Bank was founded in 2006 and have grown to over 200 employees in 3 countries, 4 000 merchants and 600 000 end users. We use the latest technology to develop safe, smart, and user-friendly products for our customers. They are the sole reason why we do what we do. We are a 100% cloud-based product company where technology is the driver to create smarter banking products.
Grab this opportunity to be a part of us and our journey!

As a Data Engineer at Northmill Bank you will be part of the data lifecycle, focus on transforming/modeling the data to fit final business requirements and serving data for BI/reporting. You will be a part of the cross-functional Analytics team consisting of Data Engineers, Data Scientists and Analysts who serve the whole bank in terms of data for insights, predictive modeling, reporting and all other potential use cases.
Utilize DBT to model and transform data, ensuring it's clean, structured, and ready for analysis, reporting and other operational systems.
Continuously monitor and optimize data flows to proactively drive us forward.
Support data needs in relation to specific analytical tasks.
Collaborate with product and product engineering teams to acquire and build data models for new business and products data modelling.
Collaborate with the Platform team on building and maintaining ingestion pipeline, guarantee high quality data into Snowflake.
Stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and emerging technologies in data engineering.
2+ years of experience in a data engineering role
Proficiency in SQL/Snowflake, DBT and Python.
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Experience with cloud platforms, especially AWS, is a plus.
Experience with using Agentic AI in IDE is a plus.
Have an interest in the underlying business and its data needs.
A fantastic office in a prime Stockholm location with great spaces and views
An independent role with the opportunity to make a real impact
Breakfast and fruits every day, as well as ""holy fika” each Friday
Regular after work and celebrated successes at the office
Apply today and be a part of Northmill!
Northmill Bank is a tech company with a banking license aiming to improve financial life, offer merchants smart solutions and erase the borders between in-store and online.
Northmill creates a more personal and relevant experience for people and companies, helping more than 2,500 merchants and 250,000 end users.

Northmill Bank is a challenger bank at the intersection of technology and finance, committed to revolutionizing the way people manage and protect their financial well-being. We are creating a different kind of banking experience, digital yet personal.

Northmill Bank was founded in 2006 and have grown to over 200 employees in 3 countries, 4 000 merchants and 600 000 end users. We use the latest technology to develop safe, smart, and user-friendly products for our customers. They are the sole reason why we do what we do. We are a 100% cloud-based product company where technology is the driver to create smarter banking products.

Grab this opportunity to be a part of us and our journey!

About Our Tech Stack


  • Snowflake
  • DBT
  • Dagster
  • AWS

About The Role

As a Data Engineer at Northmill Bank you will be part of the data lifecycle, focus on transforming/modeling the data to fit final business requirements and serving data for BI/reporting. Responsibilities


  • Utilize DBT to model and transform data, ensuring it's clean, structured, and ready for analysis, reporting and other operational systems.
  • Continuously monitor and optimize data flows to proactively drive us forward.
  • Support data needs in relation to specific analytical tasks.
  • Collaborate with product and product engineering teams to acquire and build data models for new business and products data modelling.
  • Collaborate with the Platform team on building and maintaining ingestion pipeline, guarantee high quality data into Snowflake.
  • Stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and emerging technologies in data engineering.


Qualifications


  • 2+ years of experience in a data engineering role
  • Proficiency in SQL/Snowflake, DBT and Python.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Experience with analytical data modelling.
  • Experience with cloud platforms, especially AWS, is a plus.
  • Experience with using Agentic AI in IDE is a plus.
  • Have an interest in the underlying business and its data needs.


What We Offer


  • A fantastic office in a prime Stockholm location with great spaces and views
  • An independent role with the opportunity to make a real impact
  • Great opportunities for professional development
  • Health - 5 000 kr health care allowance
  • Conference abroad every other year
  • Breakfast and fruits every day, as well as ""holy fika” each Friday
  • Regular after work and celebrated successes at the office

4389725414,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-kayak-4389725414?position=1&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=s0xW2i1hxHWp5eM0haycZg%3D%3D,Data Analyst,KAYAK,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.
This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.
Receive and scope data requests from PR and local media, then deliver clean, press-ready datasets.
Work with PR and communications partners to shape data angles and check feasibility.
Build and maintain automated SQL/Python pipelines and templates for recurring requests.
Create and maintain self‑service dashboards and lightweight tools (e.g., Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau) so teams can quickly explore trends.
Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.
A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
Familiarity with visualization tools (Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau, or similar).
Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.
Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.
Experience working with marketing, search, or travel data.
Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year
Focus on mental health and well-being:
Company-wide week off a year - the whole team fully recharges (and returns without a pile-up of work!)
6 weeks paid vacation + a day off for your birthday
Access to thousands of on-demand e-learnings
Lunch provided five days per week & breakfast once a week
Awesome office in the city centre of Copenhagen (Rådhuspladsen)
Monthly social events, yoga, Thursday bars

At KAYAK, we want everyone to have the space to grow, share ideas and do great work. That’s why we’re focused on hiring the best talent from all walks of life and experiences, supporting them well and making sure no one feels like they have to fit a mold to belong here.
If you need accommodations during the application or interview process, or on the job, we’re here to support you. Please reach out to your recruiter to request any accommodations.

KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.

This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.

In This Role, You Will

  • Receive and scope data requests from PR and local media, then deliver clean, press-ready datasets.
  • Work with PR and communications partners to shape data angles and check feasibility.
  • Build and maintain automated SQL/Python pipelines and templates for recurring requests.
  • Create and maintain self‑service dashboards and lightweight tools (e.g., Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau) so teams can quickly explore trends.
  • Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
  • Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.

Please Apply If You Have

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
  • 2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
  • Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
  • Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
  • Familiarity with visualization tools (Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau, or similar).
  • Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
  • Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.

Nice-to-have

  • Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.
  • Experience working with marketing, search, or travel data.
  • Experience building lightweight self-serve dashboards.

Benefits And Perks

  • Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year
  • Focus on mental health and well-being:
    • Company-paid therapy sessions through SpringHealth
    • Company-paid subscription to HeadSpace
    • Company-wide week off a year - the whole team fully recharges (and returns without a pile-up of work!)
  • No meeting Fridays
  • Paid parental leave
  • 6 weeks paid vacation + a day off for your birthday
  • Paid volunteer time
  • Focus on your career growth:
    • Development Dollars
    • Leadership development
    • Access to thousands of on-demand e-learnings

  • Pension plan contributions

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences along with how you will contribute to our working culture. Even if you don’t feel you meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 300 million reviews and 64 million monthly active users on average across the globe, with 140 billion annual Trustbox impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1,000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas.

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.
Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

You will shape marketing triangulation, combining Multi-Touch Attribution (MTA), Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM), and rigorous incrementality testing to provide a 360-degree view of our ROI.

What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Marketing Triangulation: Guide and own the methodology for measuring marketing effectiveness. You will move beyond surface-level metrics to develop a sophisticated ""source of truth"" using MMM, MTA, and lift studies.
  • Advanced Data Analysis: Design and execute complex analyses, including cohort analysis, customer segmentation, and predictive modeling to identify growth levers.
  • Collaborative Experimentation: Partner with Marketing Leads to design, implement, and analyze A/B and multivariate tests, fostering a culture of ""test and learn.""
  • Capability Development: Support and scale our analytics infrastructure. This includes developing solutions in R/Python and integrating them into our data stack.
  • Stakeholder Influence: Provide actionable insights to help stakeholders make data-informed decisions and drive data literacy around marketing optimization.
  • Actionable Reporting: Create intuitive dashboards in Looker that don't just show data, but provide actionable insights for stakeholders ranging from Channel Managers to the CMO.
  • Funnel Strategy: Guide the analytical strategy for the end-to-end GTM funnel. Analyze the sales funnel to increase lead generation, accelerate conversion rates, and drive revenue expansion by identifying bottlenecks and opportunities for improvements.
  • Data Engineering Collaboration: Work closely with our data engineering team using dbt to ensure our marketing data pipelines are robust, efficient, and reliable.

Who You Are:

  • Highly Skilled Technician: You are fluent in SQL and R or Python and have a deep-rooted understanding of regression models, clustering, and statistical significance with an ability to apply them to marketing problems.

About KaffeK

At KaffeK, we serve coffee lovers across multiple European markets — offering a wide selection of coffee products at fair prices. Data isn't an afterthought here — it's embedded in how we make decisions, from pricing and forecasting to understanding our customers and running our operations. We capture data at every step of the customer journey — from the very first touchpoint to the moment a package lands at the door — across a scale that few companies can match. There is no shortage of data to work with. We've built something we're genuinely proud of, and data is already one of our sharpest competitive edges. We're looking for someone who wants to work at the centre of that — and leave a real mark on it.


The Role

Our data capability is advanced, well-structured, and already driving real business value.

You'll step into a strong foundation and have the freedom and mandate to build on it. You'll own everything from the warehouse layer upwards — designing and evolving how data is structured, ensuring it's reliable and well-modelled, and turning it into analytics and insights that drive decisions across the business. SQL is your primary tool, and big data analytics is your domain. Internal tech handles ingestion
column2:
everything after that is yours.

You'll work closely with commercial and operational stakeholders, collaborate with our Data Scientist on ML-related data needs, and report directly to the Executive VP of Commerce and Technology.

If you have the skills and ambition but feel held back in your current role, this is the opportunity to finally put them to use.


What We Expect of You

You are technically deep — SQL and Python are second languages to you, and you're as comfortable reasoning about warehouse architecture as writing a complex query from scratch.

You're driven by curiosity. You don't wait to be asked — you dig into the data, find the answers, and proactively surface insights that the business didn't even know it needed. The best version of this role isn't just someone who responds to requests — it's someone who also walks into a room and shows the organisation something about itself it hadn't seen before.

You're self-driven and structured. We have plans and priorities — what we need is someone who can take them and run without needing to be micromanaged. You investigate source data critically, form a clear view on what's reliable, and make pragmatic decisions about how to model and use it. And you care deeply about outcomes — data that doesn't lead to better decisions isn't good enough for you.


You likely have:

  • 6+ years of experience working with data in complex, data-intensive environments
  • Deep expertise in SQL and data warehouse architecture — this is your core craft
  • Strong grasp of data modelling, data quality, and governance practices
  • A proven ability to bridge business needs and data solutions
  • Experience with Python, R, or similar languages for data processing and automation
  • A track record of delivering with a high degree of autonomy


Nice to have:

  • Background in e-commerce, retail, or similarly commercial environments
  • Comfortable working alongside ML specialists and data scientists


What You'll Be Responsible For

  • Our data warehouse — structure, modelling, quality, and evolution
  • Big data analytics that power commercial and operational decisions
  • The data foundation that feeds our ML models
  • Data governance — standards, quality, and trust across the organisation
  • The bridge between what the business needs to know and what the data can tell it


What We Offer

  • Full ownership of a mature, advanced data setup — you're not starting from scratch, you're taking the wheel
  • Real autonomy — at KaffeK, if you take ownership and deliver within the overall goals and principles of the company, you get it. We don't hold people back
  • Millions of data points spanning the full customer journey — from first click to delivery
  • Interesting technical challenges spanning multiple markets and cultures
  • A fast-moving, ambitious company where your work has visible impact from day one
  • A supportive, coffee-loving team that takes data seriously

4407208796,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/business-analyst-data-reporting-at-pandora-4407208796?position=5&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=kHaNZX4eTtGyVvOZnRX1dA%3D%3D,"Business Analyst, Data & Reporting",Pandora,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Ready to turn data into dependable insights that shape Pandora’s global store network?

This is an ideal role for a detail-oriented person who enjoys structured processes and working with data, and who is curious about the business context, stakeholder interaction, and strengthening the reliability of business-critical reporting.

As our new Business Analyst, Reporting & Analytics, you’ll play a key role in supporting the day‑to‑day management of data processes underpinning Pandora’s global retail store portfolio. Based at our Copenhagen HQ, this role offers an exciting opportunity to combine analytical rigor with operational impact - ensuring our network data is accurate, reliable, and actionable for teams across the business.

In this role, you will be responsible for improving, consolidating, updating, and maintaining a range of recurring reports that support Pandora’s Network & Franchise Management activities. You will ensure data quality, consistency, and governance by performing structured data checks and keeping records accurate across systems and tools.

A core part of the role is close collaboration with cluster counterparts across markets. You’ll actively follow up on inputs, resolve discrepancies through structured dialogue, and ensure data submissions are delivered on time and to the required quality standards. Your work will enable confident decision‑making across global and local teams by providing a trusted, up‑to‑date view of Pandora’s store network performance and plans.

This role is ideal if you are detail-oriented, enjoy structured work and working with data, and are curious about the business behind the numbers. It also involves stakeholder interaction and improving the reliability of business-critical reporting.
Consolidating and refreshing recurring store portfolio reports on a regular cadence
Performing structured data quality checks and ensuring adherence to data governance standards
Maintaining accurate and consistent data records across reporting tools and files
Proactively identifying opportunities to improve data processes and reporting efficiency
Tracking network action plans, including new openings, refits, relocations, closures, and FWI initiatives
Collecting inputs from local network teams and sharing outputs cross functionally to support planning
Supporting network expansion reporting, including store counts, net openings, and financial impact
Tracking lease renewal savings and one‑off savings across markets
Owning the creation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of Power BI dashboards
Supporting the transition from Excel‑based reporting to Power BI where relevant
Delivering technical updates and enhancements to existing files and tools
To Succeed In This Role, You’ll Bring
2 - 4 years of experience in a business analyst, reporting, data, or finance role
Experience working with structured data processes, including consolidation, validation, and quality management
Strong analytical skills and a structured, detail‑oriented approach to recurring reporting
Ability to identify data inconsistencies and resolve them through constructive, process‑driven dialogue
Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain data and processes clearly
A proactive mindset with ownership of defined reporting and process areas

column2: experience with Power BI and Power Query

column1: Exposure to retail and/or real estate environments is a plus
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in data analytics, information management, finance, or a related field

You’ll join the Reporting & Analytics team within Network & Franchise Management - a highly collaborative team delivering reliable data and insights across Pandora. Working closely with your manager, teammates, and a broad range of stakeholders, you’ll play a key role in supporting data-driven decisions.

You will be working out of our beautiful Head Office in Copenhagen V, established in 2016 and designed to unite and inspire our teams. Besides our beautiful interior design we are also proud of our LEED Gold Certification for Energy and Environmental Design. This means that we use as few natural resources as possible and that we use them with respect. The result is a professional and dynamic space that is unique, comfortable and inspirational – and delivers a true Pandora experience to anyone who works here or comes to visit.

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Pandora jewellery is sold in more than 100 countries through 7,000 points of sale, including more than 2,800 concept stores.

Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs around 39,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery with 100% recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 32.5 billion (EUR 4.4 billion) in 2025.

The life sciences industry plays a significant role in advancing healthcare and improving the quality of life - in many cases, saving millions of lives globally. At BASE life science, this is the motivation for everything we do, aimed at pushing boundaries, seeking excellence, and making a lasting difference through consulting expertise and data-driven solutions.

We are a fast-growing consultancy in the life sciences industry and are looking for innovative, enthusiastic, and versatile data engineers to join us. This is an exciting opportunity to work on cutting-edge projects and gain experience in a dynamic and fast-paced environment.

From our headquarters in Denmark and offices in France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, and the United States of America, we serve customers across the globe. With a deep understanding of the life sciences industry, we work hand in hand with customers to identify, develop, and implement strategic initiatives that drive growth, efficiency, and overall success through tailored solutions.

Our partnership with industry-leading solution providers, including Veeva, Salesforce, IQVIA, Benchling, and MediSpend ensures that our clients harness the full potential of the most innovative solutions on the market.
We are looking for a proactive, enthusiastic, and versatile Data Consultant to join our team in Spain.

In this role, you will work at the intersection of technology, business, and the life sciences domains, with a strong focus on data migration projects. You will help translate customer requirements into structured technical logic and support the execution of migration and data-related activities in a controlled and compliant way.

You will work with tools and platforms such as Python (Pandas, Prefect, Requests, ...) SQL, Excel, Ubuntu/Linux, VS Code, Azure DevOps, GitHub, PuTTY/WinSCP, Jira, and Confluence to analyse data, define transformation logic, document approaches, and support project delivery. At the same time, success in the role depends on your ability to collaborate closely with clients and colleagues, communicate clearly, and challenge constructively when needed.
This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys combining technical problem-solving with client interaction, and who wants to grow in a dynamic consulting environment within life sciences.
As a Data Consultant, your responsibilities will include:
Translating business and customer requirements into clear technical logic and structured migration or data-handling activities
Supporting the design and execution of data migration and data processing tasks in a controlled, traceable, and compliant manner
Working with data, logic, and processes to ensure tasks are performed accurately and in a controlled manner
Collaborating with clients and internal teams to understand needs, challenge assumptions, and propose the right approach
Presenting technical solutions and explaining complex topics in a clear and accessible way
Contributing to data quality, documentation, and governance best practices
We offer this position in Denmark.
What makes you a great fit for this role?
We are looking for someone who is comfortable working across both technical and business-focused tasks, and who enjoys being in the middle of collaboration, problem-solving, and delivery.
To succeed in the role, you likely bring:
A Master’s degree in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) or another quantitative field
Strong proficiency in Python, including experience with version control, code sharing, and team-based development practices
Advanced experience skills in Microsoft Office, including very good Excel skills and solid presentation skills in PowerPoint
A structured and analytical mindset, with the ability to turn requirements into clear logic and practical solutions
Confidence in working directly with customers, including asking questions, challenging constructively, and presenting solutions
Curiosity, a willingness to learn, and motivation to grow your experience
High attention to detail and a quality-focused way of working
A proactive, team-oriented attitude and the ability to work independently when needed
Additionally, it is an advantage if you have experience with:
Working in regulated environments, including GxP
Life sciences domains such as clinical, quality, regulatory, laboratory, safety, and commercial
Data migration, data handling, or controlled process execution
Consulting, client-facing work, or other roles where business and technology come together
What makes us the right employer for you?
We are proud to represent more than 35 nationalities across BASE life science, and we believe diverse perspectives are essential to delivering innovative solutions and achieving excellence.

Our culture is built on five core values: team spirit, trust, openness, ambition, and execution. We offer a flexible working environment where people are trusted to take ownership of their work while maintaining a healthy work-life balance. We value cross-functional collaboration, specialized expertise, and people who are willing to challenge the status quo.
Remote work flexibility, with an expectation to be in the office at least once a week
Support for physical, social, and emotional wellbeing
Home office setup with laptop and other electronic devices
The opportunity to make a difference for patients around the world
If this opportunity sounds interesting, we encourage you to submit your application in English as soon as possible. We review applications on an ongoing basis and look forward to hearing from you.

4405276748,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/junior-bi-analyst-at-desmi-4405276748?position=12&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=9SbsrB1DZcTDl235XN5PQQ%3D%3D,Junior BI Analyst,DESMI,"Nørresundby, North Denmark Region, Denmark","Are you our new Junior BI Analyst with a passion for data, business support, and developing Insight?

At DESMI, we are strengthening our data and analytics capabilities to support better decision-making across the organization. We are looking for a curious and analytical Junior BI Analyst who thrives on transforming data into meaningful insights and supporting the business with high-quality analysis. In this role, you will play a key part in running our BI support function, ensuring that stakeholders across DESMI have access to reliable data, insightful reporting, and clear answers to their business questions.

DESMI is a dynamic and innovative company where collaboration, continuous learning, and professional development are at the core of our culture. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with modern BI tools, work closely with stakeholders across functions, and contribute to strengthening our data-driven culture.

As our Junior BI Analyst, you will be the responsible for delivering business intelligence support and analysis. You will work closely with colleagues across departments, helping them understand data, answer key business questions, and translate requirements into impactful Power BI solutions.
You will be part of a dedicated Finance & BI environment and collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders, contributing to the continued development of our DESMI Insights platform.
Running the BI support function: Act as the first point of contact for BI-related questions, supporting users and ensuring they get value from our data and reports
Developing Power BI reports: Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide clear and actionable insights
Delivering business analysis: Conduct thorough and structured analyses to support decision-making across the organization
Translating business needs into data solutions: Work closely with stakeholders to understand their requirements and turn them into scalable BI solutions
Ensuring data quality: Validate and reconcile data to ensure consistency, reliability, and trust in reporting
Supporting continuous improvement: Contribute to developing and optimizing our BI platform, data models, and reporting standards Your next opportunity starts here

We are looking for a structured and curious analyst who enjoys diving into data and turning complexity into clarity. You are motivated by understanding the business behind the numbers and delivering insights that make a real impact.
Hold a master’s degree in economics, business administration (e.g. Cand.merc), with a strong understanding of business strategy or performance management.
Have a solid understanding of how data supports business decision-making, including financial and operational metrics
Have experience with or a strong interest in Power BI both analysis and report development
Are analytical and detail-oriented, with the ability to perform thorough and well-documented analyses
Are proactive and service-minded, enjoying close collaboration with stakeholders across the organization
Communicate complex data insights in a clear and understandable way
Have a structured approach to problem-solving and a strong sense of ownership

Whether you are at the beginning of your career or have gained some initial experience, this role offers a unique opportunity to develop your skills in a global organization and become a key player in driving data-driven decision-making at DESMI.
The perks of being at DESMI
Flexible working hours – including possibilities for remote work, but on-site attendance is encouraged
Global career prospects – work with colleagues across Europe, Asia, and the U.S.
Skills development and career growth – Training, job rotation, and internal mobility
On-site canteen – light breakfast, lunch buffet, and snacks
Company sports & social activities – from badminton and bowling to art clubs and summer parties
But we’re more than just pumps. At DESMI, we believe teamwork and innovation drive success.
When You Join DESMI, You Can Expect
A collaborative and supportive team environment.
Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development.
A chance to contribute your ideas and make a real impact.
A company that values sustainability and innovation.

We are a flat organization with an open-door policy, where decisions are made together, and ideas are always welcome. Here, doers thrive – if you see an opportunity for improvement, you have the freedom to act on it.

As innovators in reliable and sustainable flow solutions, we serve a wide range of industries from Aquaculture and Defense to Green Technologies – the list goes on. Sustainability is highly appreciated while developing high-performance and long-lasting technologies that help industries adapt to evolving markets while reducing environmental impact.
If you're a dedicated and motivated individual seeking a challenging and rewarding career where you can contribute to a more sustainable future, we encourage you to apply!

To learn more about this opportunity or to submit your application, please contact Lead BI Manager Mathias Dichmann-Schack (mdsc@desmi.com). We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as the position will be filled once the right candidate is found.

Are you our new Junior BI Analyst with a passion for data, business support, and developing Insight?

At DESMI, we are strengthening our data and analytics capabilities to support better decision-making across the organization. We are looking for a curious and analytical Junior BI Analyst who thrives on transforming data into meaningful insights and supporting the business with high-quality analysis. In this role, you will play a key part in running our BI support function, ensuring that stakeholders across DESMI have access to reliable data, insightful reporting, and clear answers to their business questions.

DESMI is a dynamic and innovative company where collaboration, continuous learning, and professional development are at the core of our culture. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with modern BI tools, work closely with stakeholders across functions, and contribute to strengthening our data-driven culture.

A glimpse into the role

As our Junior BI Analyst, you will be the responsible for delivering business intelligence support and analysis. You will work closely with colleagues across departments, helping them understand data, answer key business questions, and translate requirements into impactful Power BI solutions.

You will be part of a dedicated Finance &
column2:
BI environment and collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders, contributing to the continued development of our DESMI Insights platform.

Your Primary Responsibilities Will Include

  • Running the BI support function: Act as the first point of contact for BI-related questions, supporting users and ensuring they get value from our data and reports
  • Developing Power BI reports: Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide clear and actionable insights
  • Delivering business analysis: Conduct thorough and structured analyses to support decision-making across the organization
  • Translating business needs into data solutions: Work closely with stakeholders to understand their requirements and turn them into scalable BI solutions
  • Ensuring data quality: Validate and reconcile data to ensure consistency, reliability, and trust in reporting
  • Supporting continuous improvement: Contribute to developing and optimizing our BI platform, data models, and reporting standards Your next opportunity starts here

We are looking for a structured and curious analyst who enjoys diving into data and turning complexity into clarity. You are motivated by understanding the business behind the numbers and delivering insights that make a real impact.

We also imagine that you:

  • Hold a master’s degree in economics, business administration (e.g. Cand.merc), with a strong understanding of business strategy or performance management.
  • Have a solid understanding of how data supports business decision-making, including financial and operational metrics
  • Have experience with or a strong interest in Power BI both analysis and report development
  • Are analytical and detail-oriented, with the ability to perform thorough and well-documented analyses
  • Are proactive and service-minded, enjoying close collaboration with stakeholders across the organization
  • Communicate complex data insights in a clear and understandable way
  • Have a structured approach to problem-solving and a strong sense of ownership

Whether you are at the beginning of your career or have gained some initial experience, this role offers a unique opportunity to develop your skills in a global organization and become a key player in driving data-driven decision-making at DESMI.

The perks of being at DESMI

  • Flexible working hours – including possibilities for remote work, but on-site attendance is encouraged
  • Global career prospects – work with colleagues across Europe, Asia, and the U.S.
  • Skills development and career growth – Training, job rotation, and internal mobility
  • Pension – attractive employer contributions
  • Private healthcare and life insurance
  • On-site canteen – light breakfast, lunch buffet, and snacks
  • Company sports &
column3:
social activities – from badminton and bowling to art clubs and summer parties

What We’re About

But we’re more than just pumps. At DESMI, we believe teamwork and innovation drive success.

When You Join DESMI, You Can Expect

  • A collaborative and supportive team environment.
  • Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development.
  • A chance to contribute your ideas and make a real impact.
  • A company that values sustainability and innovation.

We are a flat organization with an open-door policy, where decisions are made together, and ideas are always welcome. Here, doers thrive – if you see an opportunity for improvement, you have the freedom to act on it.

As innovators in reliable and sustainable flow solutions, we serve a wide range of industries from Aquaculture and Defense to Green Technologies – the list goes on. Sustainability is highly appreciated while developing high-performance and long-lasting technologies that help industries adapt to evolving markets while reducing environmental impact.

Ready to apply?

If you're a dedicated and motivated individual seeking a challenging and rewarding career where you can contribute to a more sustainable future, we encourage you to apply!

To learn more about this opportunity or to submit your application, please contact Lead BI Manager Mathias Dichmann-Schack (mdsc@desmi.com). We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as the position will be filled once the right candidate is found.

Are you a talented digital analyst seeking an exciting Nordic opportunity? In this role, you'll have the opportunity to use and develop your expertise within digital sales and marketing analytics which will have a strong impact on our business in the Nordics. If you have a data and insights driven mindset with strong communication skills and a proactive approach to digital analysis, then you should apply for this position.
Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.

As a Digital Analyst, you will develop and support the business by providing insights into the success of digital sales and marketing, and play a crucial role in the unfolding of our ambitious digital customer acquisition strategy. In this role, you will work closely with your team and internal stakeholders in Finland, Denmark, Norway and Sweden. Your responsibilities will include analysing and optimizing digital traffic, journeys, sales and behaviour as well as providing reporting to support the business. Experience with Adobe Analytics and/or Adobe Journey Optimizer and your passion for utilizing analytics to develop the business will ensure your success in this role.

The Nordic Digital Customer Acquisition team has team members in all Nordic locations such as Helsinki, Copenhagen, Stockholm and Oslo, and therefore you can have your workplace in any of these locations.
Analysis and optimization of traffic, conversions, customer journeys and sales
Work together with content managers as well as Marketing automation specialists to optimise the customer journeys
Develop and maintain reporting to stakeholders and management
Data management and analysis to support the business
Proficiency in Adobe Analytics, Excel and PowerPoint.
Knowledge of Adobe Customer Journey Analytics.
Excellent in communicating, visualising and presenting your results and analytics assignments
Bachelor’s or Master´s degree in a relevant field
Fluent in both written and spoken English.
It would be ideal if you also:
A team player who can contribute with ideas, feedback and implement new ideas
A Nordic role where you work with our Business Banking digital activities in Finland, Denmark, Sweden and Norway
Develop and set the direction for our analytics, reporting and Adobe Analytics and Adobe Customer Journey Analytics usage
A supportive and friendly team environment where we focus on collaboration and job-satisfaction

People come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.

A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.

Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
If this sounds like you, get in touch!
Submit your application no later than 21/05/2026. Please notice that we might start interviews already during the application period.
For more information, you’re welcome to contact Stefan Brieghel on stefan.brieghel@nordea.com.
We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.
We build relationships . We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.

We learn and develop. We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.

We lead change . We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.

We are Nordea . We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
Only for candidates in Finland: A security clearance will be performed for the person selected for this position.
Only for candidates in Sweden: For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com .
