4411197242,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/associate-analytics-lead-bi-at-novo-nordisk-4411197242?position=15&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=KEdIoumeYhet2m%2FxgWWLQQ%3D%3D,Associate Analytics Lead - BI,Novo Nordisk,"Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Finance, Legal & Global Solutions

There has never been a better time to join indirect procurement at Novo Nordisk. We are driving a global cost-optimisation programme where analytics is a key enabler—converting complex data into clear insights and real business impact. If you want to build high-impact BI, elevate source-to-pay and category excellence, and succeed in a fast-moving, stakeholder-rich environment, this role is an exciting opportunity.

As an Associate Analytics Lead – BI, you will be part of the Advanced Analytics team and work in close collaboration with experienced data and BI professionals, procurement system, process specialists and category managers to generate meaningful insights from large and complex data sources. You will be responsible for turning data into actionable, timely, and impactful insights, enabling high-quality analytics and dashboards that support strategic procurement decisions and drive value creation.

Act as a trusted analytics and BI partner within Corporate Procurement, driving the development, rollout, and continuous improvement of Power BI dashboards, analytics products, and performance management.
Define, standardize, and govern procurement KPIs, ensuring transparent definitions, consistent measurement, and clear data lineage from source systems to dashboards.
Provide actionable insights across the end-to-end Source-to-Pay area to identify opportunities for process improvement, efficiency gains and user experience enhancement.
Support category management analytics, including spend and savings analysis, market intelligence, and preferred supplier usage, helping category managers drive value creation.

Corporate Procurement is the Centre of Excellence for procurement in Novo Nordisk. We are responsible for the global procurement strategy, processes, and systems. We define and drive the global procurement strategy with around 150 employees in Denmark and over 500 globally, acting as a strategic partner in executing the company’s strategy and enabling value at scale. A key enabler for taking Procurement to the next level will be to leverage holistic insights to drive value in and from procurement. The Advanced Analytics team is responsible for driving this journey.
Bachelor/Master’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, Computer Science, or a similar field.
5–7+ years of experience in BI and analytics or related fields. Experience in Procurement BI is a plus.

Extensive hands-on experience with Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, Power Query, semantic models, dashboard design, report optimisation, and deployment of scalable BI solutions. Understanding of modern BI and data platforms.

Experience with Microsoft Fabric, Azure Databricks is an advantage. You are curious, think outside the box, and are eager to explore how AI can transform procurement from passive reporting to proactive analytics that accelerate decision-making and enhance user experience.

You are structured in your problem-solving approach and able to break down complex business questions into clear analytical tasks. You are a strong team member with a can-do attitude and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.
You can navigate a complex stakeholder landscape and communicate effectively with stakeholders from different functions, seniority levels, cultures, and domain backgrounds.

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

There is, of course, more on offer here than the uniqueness of our culture and the extraordinary results we produce. Being part of a global healthcare company means opportunities to learn and develop are all around us, while our benefits are designed with your career and life stage in mind.
Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 651,000 to 956,900 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.
The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.
Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here
To ensure a fair and efficient recruitment process, please refrain from adding a photo in your CV or resume.
We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you be-come part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Moller - Maersk,"Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Maersk is a global leader in integrated logistics and have been industry pioneers for over a century. Through innovation and transformation, we are redefining the boundaries of possibility, continuously setting new standards for efficiency, sustainability, and excellence.

At Maersk, we believe in the power of diversity, collaboration, and continuous learning and we work hard to ensure that the people in our organisation reflect and understand the customers we exist to serve.
With over 100,000 employees across 130 countries, we work together to shape the future of global trade and logistics.
Join us as we harness cutting-edge technologies and unlock opportunities on a global scale. Together, let's sail towards a brighter, more sustainable future with Maersk.

As a Student Worker, you will work closely with our team to support data-driven decision-making. You will help transform raw data into meaningful insights, contribute to reporting, and assist in building scalable data solutions.
Assist with data collection, cleaning, and transformation
Support analysis and visualization of data to generate insights
Help build and maintain dashboards and reports
Contribute to automation of data processes and workflows
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs
Currently enrolled in a relevant education where working with data is a natural part (e.g., Data Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, IT, Business Analytics, or similar)
Strong interest in data, analytics, and problem-solving
Experience with data transformation, analysis, and visualization (through studies or work)
Comfortable working with structured and unstructured data
We look forward to your application.

Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Maersk is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, colour, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.

We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or an accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or to perform a job, please contact us by emailing accommodationrequests@maersk.com.

Student Worker

As a Student Worker, you will work closely with our team to support data-driven decision-making. You will help transform raw data into meaningful insights, contribute to reporting, and assist in building scalable data solutions.

Your Responsibilities

  • Assist with data collection, cleaning, and transformation
  • Support analysis and visualization of data to generate insights
  • Help build and maintain dashboards and reports
  • Contribute to automation of data processes and workflows
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs

Your profile

  • Currently enrolled in a relevant education where working with data is a natural part (e.g., Data Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, IT, Business Analytics, or similar)
  • Strong interest in data, analytics, and problem-solving
  • Experience with data transformation, analysis, and visualization (through studies or work)
  • Comfortable working with structured and unstructured data

Preferred Qualifications (a Plus)

  • Experience with:
    • Excel
    • Power BI
    • Dataflows / Power Query
    • Power Automate
    • Python
We look forward to your application.

4401339375,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/product-analyst-all-genders-at-hellofresh-4401339375?position=19&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=ak8I15yC9Ge%2BTckePDEVZw%3D%3D,Product Analyst (all genders),HelloFresh,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","The Role (What’s In The Box)

It’s a typical day in our Islands Brygge office. Data is being pulled, patterns are analyzed, and decisions are made on which recipes make it into the meal kits we send to thousands of customers every week. As a Product Analyst (all genders) in our Menu Strategy & Planning team, you use those insights to inform the development of our recipe database, a key component in how our physical product comes together.
Along The Way, a Simple Question Comes Up
What actually makes a recipe one that customers come back for?

It’s exactly this kind of question you will help answer. Your mission is to use your analytical expertise to facilitate the development of a broad pool of diverse, well-liked, and affordable recipes to excite and delight our customers, while supporting the planning needs of our menu.

Analyze customer and product data to generate insights and provide strategic direction for the development of our culinary product, ensuring a strong balance across cost, utilization, variety, retention, and performance
Translate product and menu composition strategy into clear and aligned recipe development plans
Manage the launch of product improvements (e.g. recipe concept launches) to our customers
Continuously improve and optimize recipe planning processes, ensuring strong coordination across teams (menu planning, editorial, recipe developers, and product marketing)
Contribute to high customer engagement by optimizing core menu signaletics and communication (e.g. tags, labels, categories, CRM)
Build and maintain a network of international counterparts to stay up to date with product planning best practices and collaborate across markets
Have ~2 years of hands-on data analysis experience, with strong SQL skills that enable you to uncover insights
Have strong problem-solving skills that help you find the right approach to tackle any analytical challenge
Easily find your way around spreadsheets and have experience working with Excel or Gsheet to efficiently merge, transform, visualize, and analyze data
Enjoy collaborating with peers and stakeholders, leveraging your analytics skills to support non-technical colleagues in their creative work
Are self-driven and can work independently to drive progress in your area always keeping an eye on quality and timeliness
Have strong analytical skills enabling you to identify patterns in large data sets, organize and make sense of multiple data sources
Have strong business acumen allowing you to identify and prioritize high-impact business opportunities within recipe planning

Above all, we are looking for people who will make HelloFresh better. There are many different ways of developing skills, and we love diverse experiences! So even if you don’t “tick all the boxes” but think you’d thrive in this role, we would really like to learn more about you.
HelloFresh Discounts – Enjoy 65% off your HelloFresh Meal Box and 40% off Factor_ subscriptions.
Learning and Development – Make the most of a dedicated Learning & Development budget and the HelloMint mentorship program.
Receive 25 days of paid vacation per year, + 5 days of feriefridage. Paid leave also provided for your child’s first sick day.
Parent Benefits – Benefit from a Parental Leave policy supporting your family needs.
Compensation and Perks – Includes a 5% company-paid pension and insurance package.
Health – Access to Spill online therapy, available within 24 hours of request.
Daily Comforts – Office perks include drinks and snacks, subsidized catered lunches, and breakfast every Friday.
Fitness and Well-being – Discounts at SATS gym, plus access to sports and exercise clubs.
If this sounds like a tasty opportunity, we’d be excited to hear from you. We aim to review your profile and respond within 5 business days.

4413922615,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/ai-data-science-intern-research-led-innovation-remote-first-at-flavorscale-4413922615?position=20&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=gjIztMmdSILwQJJbMTROGg%3D%3D,AI & Data Science Intern: Research-Led Innovation (Remote-First),FlavorScale,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","FlavorScale is seeking an AI/Data Scientist Intern to join our ""Engine Room."" While the rest of the team is on the streets of Amsterdam, you are in the data—building the predictive models and recommendation engines that make our platform feel like magic. This is a Research-Based role for the student who wants to see their academic theories applied to real-world, high-velocity F&B data.

Most AI internships have you cleaning spreadsheets in a corner. We’re giving you the keys to our data architecture. You will work directly with the CTO and CEO to turn raw ""Full-Flow"" data into the industry’s most sophisticated discovery engine.
Background: Currently pursuing a degree (BSc/MSc) in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
The Mathematical Architect: You don't just ""import libraries""
column2: you understand the logic behind the models. You’re obsessed with finding patterns in the noise.

column1: The Deep Researcher: You enjoy diving into academic papers and whitepapers to see how the latest LLM or recommendation system trends can be applied to ""Taste"" and ""Discovery.""
Data-Driven Storyteller: You can take a complex clustering model and explain to the CEO why it’s a game-changer for a restaurant owner in De Pijp.
Vibe: You are curious, rigorous, and tech-obsessed. You prefer a Jupyter Notebook over a spreadsheet and a ""What if we tried this?"" mindset over a ""How do I do this?"" one.
Flavor Modeling: Research and develop algorithms that quantify ""taste"" and ""vibe,"" helping us build the world’s first truly personalized F&B recommendation engine.
Predictive Analytics: Analyze our partner data to build models that predict consumer trends, peak hours, and ""next-big-thing"" dishes before they go viral.
NLP & LLM Integration: Explore how we can use Large Language Models to scrape and sentiment-analyze thousands of reviews and menu descriptions into actionable insights.
Data Scoping: Work with the BDR team to identify what ""Full-Flow"" data points we should be capturing from restaurants to make our AI even smarter.
Strategic Tech Input: Participate in technical syncs to discuss the scalability of our data pipeline as we head toward the 2026 Q3 funding round.

This is a research-based internship that offers a technical masterclass in startup growth. While we cannot promise permanent positions—as our key hires depend on 2026/2027 investment cycles—this role is our Core Tech Pipeline. Successful interns who build a model that moves the needle will be top-of-mind for Lead Scientist roles as the company levels up.
Location: Remote-First (Netherlands residency preferred for periodic tech-syncs in Amsterdam).
Tech Stack: Proficiency in Python (and R) (Pandas, Scikit-learn, PyTorch/TensorFlow). Experience with SQL and API integrations is a major plus.
Status: This must be a formal internship as part of your degree.
The ""Science"" Mindset: You are comfortable with ambiguity and have the patience to iterate on a model until it’s perfect.

Moller - Maersk,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","We are looking for a curious and data-savvy Student Assistant to join our Audits & Investigations (A&I) team. This role is ideal for a student who is passionate about data analytics, business intelligence, and digital enablement within a governance and risk context.
You will support the team in delivering data-driven insights, maintaining dashboards, and monitoring data quality to help ensure transparency, learning, and accountability across the organization.
Assist in the development and maintenance of dashboards (e.g., Power BI) to support audit & investigations planning, execution, and reporting.
Support data quality monitoring and validation routines to ensure reliability of engagement data.
Help transform raw data into actionable insights through structured analysis and visual storytelling.
Participate in transformation-related projects, especially those involving automation, AI use cases in audits & investigation lifecycles, or ML based pattern identification.
Provide administrative and ad-hoc support to the A&I team as needed.
A final year Bachelor’s student or a first year Master’s student at DTU, ITU or KU (e.g., Data Science, Engineering, Computer Science).
Comfortable working with data tools such as Power BI, databricks and ideally some exposure to SQL, Python, or R.
Interested in how data, risk, and governance intersect in a global business environment.
Structured, detail-oriented, and eager to learn from a team of experienced professionals.
Hands-on experience in data-driven internal auditing and investigations within a global organization.
Exposure to real-world applications of analytics in risk, compliance, and performance monitoring.
A collaborative and flexible work environment that supports your academic journey.
Opportunities to contribute to digital transformation and insight generation in a high-impact function.

4410479184,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-devoteam-4410479184?position=22&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=kYmXrxWQf9O9tff3sFMaGQ%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Devoteam,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Job Description

Are you a Data Engineer with 2-7 years of experience ready to take the next step? And would you like to work directly with clients while keeping up to date with the newest technologies? Then read on & apply to become part of a dynamic team of 60 data experts who help companies gain new insights using a wide palette of modern tools.
Location: Central Copenhagen, near Vesterport Station
The role requires you to be both a hands-on builder and a point of contact for our clients' data challenges. Specifically, you will:
Build robust data pipelines and models that streamline the ingestion, transformation, and delivery of data
Implement and develop modern data platforms through DevOps & CI/CD practices.
Collaborate with our clients’ engineers, analysts, & business users to solve business issues and ensure lasting value.
You will learn from our many data engineers with 7-20 years of experience, while taking on a mentoring role for our many less experienced engineers.
What You Bring To The Team
2-7 years of experience working as a data engineer.
Proficiency in Python, SQL, APIs, and/or software development.
An analytical approach to problem-solving, with a strong interest in how to drive business improvement using data.
Clear communication skills and the curiosity to engage with clients on projects.

Are you motivated by making a difference and contributing to tomorrow’s BI/data science solutions? In which case you might be our new colleague.
You will create a strong foundation for strategic decisions
In your Business Intelligence or data science role, you will be working with various solutions that support Systematic’s business areas.
This involves the development of products and solutions for our customers and other business units – all in close collaboration with highly skilled colleagues.
Some of the tasks you will be working with involve:
Identifying and understanding the needs of both the customer and the product organisation through close dialogue
Using data to create value for our customers
Developing, coding and implementing BI/data science solutions
Cleaning and analysing data, and implementing and developing machine learning models
Facilitating workshops and building data models that support domains, user behaviour and software
Being part of a dynamic working environment characterised by collaboration, development and quality.

You can expect to work with a range of technologies, such as Microsoft Power BI, Python, Azure Cloud, Microsoft SQL Server, T-SQL, Microsoft SSIS and SSAS, data warehousing, data modelling and analysis.
High level of interdisciplinary expertise and collaboration
We are committed to acquiring knowledge and developing methods based on a well-functioning group of BI/data science colleagues.
We enjoy a very high level of job satisfaction, and the dynamics between experienced experts and young talents contribute to a high level of development and progress.
We work closely together across the different disciplines and areas of expertise, with teams comprising project managers, consultants, data scientists, software architects and business developers.

A key characteristic of the teams at Systematic is that we dare to challenge and develop ourselves and each other. We focus on knowledge-sharing in our work, and always find more innovative ways of doing things.
You understand the importance of BI and data science
You are creative, good at spotting opportunities and think out of the box. You have a solid understanding of the interplay between people, technologies and processes.
You have strong communication skills combined with sound business acumen, and you enjoy being involved in a wide variety of assignments.
You stay calm when faced with numerous tasks and requirements, and you maintain an overview and understand how to prioritise the tasks at every stage of the project.
You are organised, analytical and you are driven by the quality of your work. You enjoy presenting new ideas and have what it takes to garner the support of your colleagues and business partners.
At the same time, you are a team player who enjoys the prospect of actively contributing to the team and participating in development activities.
You are fluent in English, both orally and in writing. It is an advantage if you speak Danish, but this is not a requirement.

We store your details for up to six months, and if in that period your profile matches our requirements for a new job, you will be contacted to arrange an interview.

The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The EIB, the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Group Information Systems Directorate (GIS), Data & Corporate Solutions Department (DCS), Data Management Division (DM), Analytics & Reporting Unit (AR) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, an Analytics Solution Officer*.
This is a full-time position at grade 5 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.
Panel interviews are anticipated for June/July 2026

The Analytics Solution Officer ensures the stable, efficient and high‑quality delivery, evolution and governance of business‑critical IT applications and services supporting EIB Group business processes. The role exists to safeguard continuity of service, strengthen service delivery governance, and support the Bank’s ongoing modernisation initiatives by coordinating internal teams and external service providers in line with IT strategy, policies and contractual commitments.
The role focuses on the ownership and continuous evolution of analytics solutions, ensuring reliable operations while supporting ongoing change.

The role sits within the Analytics and Reporting Unit, which is responsible for the end‑to‑end lifecycle of analytics and reporting solutions supporting EIB Group business processes. This includes solution ownership, service delivery, vendor coordination and contribution to change initiatives.
The post holder works closely with business stakeholders, IT counterparts, senior management and external providers, and reports directly to the Head of Unit.
Ensure end‑to‑end ownership and day‑to‑day delivery of analytics solutions and related services, maintaining availability, performance, security and compliance with internal standards.
Manage the full lifecycle of assigned solutions, from planning and sourcing through implementation, operation and controlled evolution, including roadmaps and service models.
Coordinate and steer external providers in a multi‑vendor setup, ensuring effective business‑as‑usual operations and measurable service performance through regular follow‑up, controls and reporting.
Support the definition and execution of externalisation initiatives, ensuring clear ownership, effective controls, service assurance and compliance with contractual obligations.
Liaise with business stakeholders, internal IT teams and external partners to translate functional needs into sustainable analytics solutions.

Contribute to project and delivery activities related to the acquisition of software and services, ensuring consistency with business priorities, enterprise architecture principles, IT security requirements and applicable policies.
Support continuous improvement of analytics platforms and services by identifying optimisation opportunities, promoting standardisation and reducing solution complexity where appropriate.
Maintain awareness of functional and technical evolutions of the platforms within the area of responsibility, advising stakeholders on relevant enhancements, options and implications.
Ensure appropriate governance, documentation, risk management and reporting across service delivery and change activities.
Support operational resilience through active involvement in incident handling, change coordination and issue escalation in collaboration with relevant parties.
University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in Engineering, Information Technology, Information Management, Economics/Finance or a related field.
At least 5 years of relevant professional experience in analytics solution delivery, analytics platforms or reporting environments, including solution lifecycle or delivery coordination.
Proven experience working with external service providers in a multi‑vendor or outsourced environment.
Hands‑on experience with Power BI, including report development and platform administration (experience with Microsoft Fabric is an asset).
Exposure to data, reporting or analytics platforms and environments.
Experience with other business intelligence and analytics tools such as SAP BusinessObjects, SAP Analytics Cloud or Tableau is considered an asset.
Good understanding of application lifecycle management, IT service management principles and delivery governance.
Experience in coordinating projects involving multiple stakeholders and disciplines.
Experience in Data Warehouse design and methodologies would be an asset.
Ability to work effectively in a multicultural, international organization.
Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (*) with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage).
Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here
To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here

(*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.

We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity.

If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately.

By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation.

We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity.

If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately.

By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation.

4410014605,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-cvation-part-of-skaylink-4410014605?position=29&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=eBpwlfLninwrSZuBubAb6w%3D%3D,Data Engineer,cVation - Part of Skaylink,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Azure Data Engineer

Som Data Engineer hos cVation bygger du innovative dataplatforme og datawarehouses i Azure. Du arbejder med ETL-processer og automatisering af dataflows, og du bygger skalerbare, driftssikre løsninger for vores kunder. Vi bruger teknologier som Fabric, Databricks, Spark, Azure Data Factory, Terraform, Git og CI/CD. Fokus er på data engineering, pipelines og cloud-infrastruktur.

cVation er et dansk udviklings- og konsulenthus, som bygger avancerede cloudløsninger på Azure med afsæt i markedets hurtigste og mest agile metoder. cVation blev stiftet i 2014, har modtaget tre Børsen Gazeller og vundet Microsofts Partner Award for App Innovation tre år i træk. Vi arbejder dedikeret med agile arbejdsmetoder, DevOps og Test Driven Development. Vi sætter en ære i at levere software af den højeste kvalitet, og tager desuden principperne fra DevOps og software engineering med i vores dataprojekter, hvilket betyder, at tests, Infrastructure as Code og automatisering er i fokus.

Du kommer til at arbejde på et af vores dataprojekter med et projektteam bestående af arkitekter, softwareudviklere og DevOps‑specialister. Du vil have fuldt fokus på ét projekt og være tilknyttet ét team ad gangen.
rådgive om og bygge datawarehouse- og dataplatformsløsninger i skyen
bidrage til design og implementering af den optimale Azure‑infrastruktur
sikre, at alt opsætning og provisionering sker som Infrastructure as Code, fx Terraform
arbejde med fuldautomatiseret overvågning, drift og skalering af løsninger
Du har en videregående uddannelse i software engineering, human centered AI el. lign. fra DTU, ITU eller KU samt erfaring i data engineering
Du har erfaring med at bygge datawarehouses og dataplatforme og har lyst til at arbejde med moderne data engineering

Du har erfaring med – og vil gerne blive klogere på – flere af følgende teknologier: Fabric, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Spark, Data Warehouse, Data Platform, Python, Azure DevOps, PowerShell, Azure CLI, YAML, Bicep, Terraform, Git og CI/CD
Du vil gerne arbejde med de nyeste teknologier i Azure
Du vil gerne indgå i et agilt setup, hvor krav og retning hurtigt kan ændres, men hvor medbestemmelse og frihed er i højsædet
Det vil være en fordel, hvis du er vant til at arbejde som konsulent i et udviklingsteam i tæt samarbejde med arkitekter og software udviklere
Du har evnen til at omsætte kundebehov til handling
Du er flydende i skrift og tale på dansk og engelsk, og trives med at have den daglige kontakt med kunden

Som person er du selvstændig, ansvarsbevidst, initiativrig, eksekverende og serviceminded. Du er drevet af at lære nyt og vild med at holde dig ajour med de nyeste metoder og teknologier. Du er engageret og motiveret i alt hvad du foretager dig. Hvis du også er glad for at sparre med dygtige kollegaer og trives med at løfte i flok, vil du passe perfekt ind hos os.

Du kommer til at indgå i vores voksende team, hvor høj faglighed, innovation og arbejdsglæde er i højsæde. Det høje faglige niveau blandt dine kollegaer giver en unik mulighed for at arbejde med de bedste, og vi tager din faglige udvikling alvorligt. Vi har en flad organisation med et minimum af administration, hvor ledelsens døre altid er åbne, og der er god plads til både bordfodbold, fredagsbar, frokost på kajen og et aktivt liv udenfor arbejdstid. Flere gange om året afholder vi hackathons, hvor vi i teams dykker ned i præcis de emner, som udviklerne finder interessante. En del af vores kultur er også, at vi gerne ser alle komme med input til hvordan vi kan optimere, udvikle og forbedre cVation.
Hvorfor vælge os som din nye arbejdsplads?

Vores fundament er løsninger bygget på Microsoft Azure Cloud. Alle vores medarbejdere mestrer cloud-native udvikling og DevOps. Vi arbejder i projekter og engagerer os hands-on i fælles teams med vores kunder for at sikre kvalitet og effektiv videndeling.

Vi er passionerede omkring at hjælpe vores kunder med at få det maksimale ud af cloudteknologien. For os er cloud ikke blot infrastruktur eller et nyt datacenter i skyen. Teknologien giver virksomheder mulighed for at skabe væsentligt mere værdi og arbejde med agilitet og innovation på en helt ny måde.

Vi har været med fra starten, da cloud blev udviklet og tilgængeligt. Vi tilbyder således vores årelange erfaring og dybe cloud ekspertise samt en værktøjskasse med alle vores best practices i form af accelerationsplatformen CADD. Den dækker over gennemprøvede værktøjer, rammeværk, effektive udviklingsmetoder og færdige automatiseringskomponenter. Alt dette for at sikre, at projekter kommer hurtigt og sikkert fra start.

Du vil blive en del af en virksomhed, som tror på, at alle har betydning, og at det skal være sjovt at gå på arbejde hver dag. Du vil få kollegaer, der er interesseret i at lære og har ønske om at blive de bedste til det, de gør. Vi tilbyder derfor gode muligheder for at udvikle dig.

Klik på Easy Apply knappen, hvis du vil arbejde med de nyeste cloud-teknologier. Hvis du har under 5 års erfaring, bedes du også vedhæfte dine karakterblade i samme fil som dit CV. Vi behandler din ansøgning fortroligt og arrangerer løbende interviews.
Har du spørgsmål til jobbet, eller vil du høre mere om cVation, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte:
Maiken Pelby, Head of People & Culture, T/ 42612828, map@cvation.com
Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!

4395477255,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/accelerated-computing-research-intern-at-nvidia-4395477255?position=30&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=bxhjOxm%2FYKBRvOdYdhkfcw%3D%3D,Accelerated Computing Research Intern,NVIDIA,"Roskilde, Region Zealand, Denmark","NVIDIA is searching for talented interns at the bachelor, master, or PhD level to join our growing software architecture team. The ideal candidates will be conducting innovative research at the intersection of Networking, Security, and Communications, and working alongside top authorities in these fields. With incredible resources at your disposal, you will be able to impact, contribute and advance these exciting domains. Topics include but are not limited to remote direct memory access, hardware offloading and acceleration, AI for networking and security, storage management, cryptography, and architecture. With its unique open culture, NVIDIA is one of the best industry labs to do Accelerated Computing research.

DPU Data-Processing Units are the smallest form of a data center, and provide the foundation to implement accelerated computing, where both processing and networking experience a performance boost through the utilization of dedicated processors. DPUs started out as the engine for accelerated networking, but now they can run AI inference and training on-board, deep packet inspection and cryptography accelerations among others thanks to onboard GPUs and acceleration engines. Join us to research next-generation accelerated data-center architectures during your internship with NVIDIA.
Be at the forefront of modern data center research
Partner and collaborate with forward-thinking industry leaders and external researchers
Participate and speak at conferences and events
Work with intelligent networking machines powered by AI systems that can learn, reason and interact with other network components
Currently pursuing a degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Math, Physics, Statistics, or a related field (bachelor, master, or PhD level)
General computer science background, preferably in algorithm design, system programming, networking, operating system, and computer architecture
Strong programming (C/C++/Python) and software development skills
Track record of technical excellence through coursework, projects, publications, or public repositories

NVIDIA is widely considered to be one of the technology world's most desirable employers. We have some of the most forward-thinking and hardworking people in the world working for us. Are you creative and autonomous? Do you love the challenge of crafting the highest performance & lowest power silicon possible? If so, we want to hear from you. Come, join our Networking Research Team and help build the real-time, cost-effective computing platform driving our success in this exciting and quickly growing field.

NVIDIA is searching for talented interns at the bachelor, master, or PhD level to join our growing software architecture team. The ideal candidates will be conducting innovative research at the intersection of Networking, Security, and Communications, and working alongside top authorities in these fields. With incredible resources at your disposal, you will be able to impact, contribute and advance these exciting domains. Topics include but are not limited to remote direct memory access, hardware offloading and acceleration, AI for networking and security, storage management, cryptography, and architecture. With its unique open culture, NVIDIA is one of the best industry labs to do Accelerated Computing research.

DPU Data-Processing Units are the smallest form of a data center, and provide the foundation to implement accelerated computing, where both processing and networking experience a performance boost through the utilization of dedicated processors. DPUs started out as the engine for accelerated networking, but now they can run AI inference and training on-board, deep packet inspection and cryptography accelerations among others thanks to onboard GPUs and acceleration engines. Join us to research next-generation accelerated data-center architectures during your internship with NVIDIA.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Be at the forefront of modern data center research
  • Partner and collaborate with forward-thinking industry leaders and external researchers
  • Participate and speak at conferences and events
  • Publish original research
  • Work with intelligent networking machines powered by AI systems that can learn, reason and interact with other network components

What We Need To See

  • Currently pursuing a degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Math, Physics, Statistics, or a related field (bachelor, master, or PhD level)
  • General computer science background, preferably in algorithm design, system programming, networking, operating system, and computer architecture
  • Strong programming (C/C++/Python) and software development skills
  • Track record of technical excellence through coursework, projects, publications, or public repositories
  • Good communication skills

4390312366,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-relocate-to-cyprus-at-scorewarrior-4390312366?position=33&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=sjcklLv%2FZgG%2Bnnul%2B5cFSQ%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus),Scorewarrior,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.

Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.

Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.

4403747329,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/portfolio-operations-student-worker-with-an-interest-in-power-app-and-power-bi-at-the-lego-group-4403747329?position=34&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=j24Y3Lqw5zat0D2RN13XVA%3D%3D,Portfolio Operations Student Worker with an Interest in Power App and Power BI,the LEGO Group,"Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Job Description

The Moulding Analytics Center (MAC) is a competence center for one of the LEGO Groups core capabilities Injection moulding and is responsible for extracting and analyzing data and providing a solid ground for data driven decision-making for engineering solutions related to the moulding process.
We are looking for an enthusiastic student worker to join the MAC Project and Portfolio Management team in Billund Denmark, who can support us on our journey towards data driven portfolio management.
Assist in the development, maintenance and continuously improvement of the Microsoft Power App based Portfolio Management tools
Maintain and operate relevant and new portfolio management and business performance management Power BI dash boards
Assist in the operations of the portfolio out of pocket (OOP) spending report and Power BI dash boards together with Finance
Support with analyzing portfolio and project data
Provide general support for ad-hoc tasks and projects
Play your part in our team succeeding

As part of the Project and Portfolio Management team in MAC, you will play an influential role in the operations and development of our portfolio management tools, that enables the prioritization of projects and activities in MAC. You will work closely together with the portfolio engineer and together you will be responsible for securing the flow of portfolio data all the way from project core data level to portfolio insights level. The data foundation you create is the key enabler for MAC to perform data driven portfolio management decisions and you will experience a high degree of power to impact and plenty of hands-on work. Furthermore, you will support the MAC organization building relevant PowerBI dashboards like waste performance dashboards, metrology performance dashboards, etc.
Do you have what it takes?
It is a prerequisite that you are enrolled in an educational institution during your employment as a student worker.
In terms of your background and skills, we envision
Strong IT skills across the Microsoft suite (e.g. Power Apps, Microsoft Lists, Power BI, SharePoint, Power Automate, etc.).
The ability to support the team’s needs for data transparency through strong Power BI skills including using Query Editor and understanding simple DAX calculation.
Confidence in working with Power Apps and experience within Microsoft 365.

Master level student or in the final year of your bachelor's degree. Educational focus is within the field of engineering such as Mechanical Engineer etc., or business related such as Business Administration, Business Insights, Analytics or equivalent.
Natural flair for data visualization and flow of data.
Analytical mindset with a passion for data crunching.
We fully respect and expect that you are dedicated to your studies. Thus, working hours will be adapted to this.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
Here is what you can expect
Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.
Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.
Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.
Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.
Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.
Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Job Description

The Moulding Analytics Center (MAC) is a competence center for one of the LEGO Groups core capabilities Injection moulding and is responsible for extracting and analyzing data and providing a solid ground for data driven decision-making for engineering solutions related to the moulding process.

We are looking for an enthusiastic student worker to join the MAC Project and Portfolio Management team in Billund Denmark, who can support us on our journey towards data driven portfolio management.


Core Responsibilities



  • Assist in the development, maintenance and continuously improvement of the Microsoft Power App based Portfolio Management tools
  • Maintain and operate relevant and new portfolio management and business performance management Power BI dash boards
  • Assist in the operations of the portfolio out of pocket (OOP) spending report and Power BI dash boards together with Finance
  • Support with analyzing portfolio and project data
  • Provide general support for ad-hoc tasks and projects



Play your part in our team succeeding

As part of the Project and Portfolio Management team in MAC, you will play an influential role in the operations and development of our portfolio management tools, that enables the prioritization of projects and activities in MAC. You will work closely together with the portfolio engineer and together you will be responsible for securing the flow of portfolio data all the way from project core data level to portfolio insights level. The data foundation you create is the key enabler for MAC to perform data driven portfolio management decisions and you will experience a high degree of power to impact and plenty of hands-on work. Furthermore, you will support the MAC organization building relevant PowerBI dashboards like waste performance dashboards, metrology performance dashboards, etc.

Do you have what it takes?

It is a prerequisite that you are enrolled in an educational institution during your employment as a student worker.

In terms of your background and skills, we envision



  • Strong IT skills across the Microsoft suite (e.g. Power Apps, Microsoft Lists, Power BI, SharePoint, Power Automate, etc.).
  • The ability to support the team’s needs for data transparency through strong Power BI skills including using Query Editor and understanding simple DAX calculation.
  • Confidence in working with Power Apps and experience within Microsoft 365.
  • Master level student or in the final year of your bachelor's degree. Educational focus is within the field of engineering such as Mechanical Engineer etc., or business related such as Business Administration, Business Insights, Analytics or equivalent.
  • Natural flair for data visualization and flow of data.
  • Analytical mindset with a passion for data crunching.
  • English at a professional level.



We fully respect and expect that you are dedicated to your studies. Thus, working hours will be adapted to this.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

4415369485,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/machine-learning-researcher-at-venture-up-4415369485?position=35&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=Y2dhQfnm%2FtfiYteGU3kC5Q%3D%3D,Machine Learning Researcher,Venture Up,Copenhagen Metropolitan Area,"Machine Learning Researcher — real feedback cycles!

Do you want to see your models make decisions in live financial markets — not sit in a notebook waiting for a quarterly review? Are you as comfortable thinking about data pipelines and calibration frameworks as you are about model architecture?

A market-leading quantitative trading firm is looking for a Machine Learning Researcher to join their modelling team in Copenhagen. They've been automating financial markets since 2008, and ML sits at the core of how they operate. This isn't a role where you hand off to an engineering team and wait. You'll own the work from data generation through to deployment — and in this environment, that cycle can be measured in hours, not months.
▸ Lead modelling workflow — build and improve the frameworks that turn massive financial datasets into predictive models that move with the markets.
▸ Develop and optimise — work on calibration and benchmarking frameworks, data preparation pipelines, and dataset generation at scale.
▸ Ship novel architectures — prototype and release new predictive models with a particular focus on time-series approaches.
▸ Work with real data, real stakes — your models get validated against live markets. Feedback is immediate and unambiguous.
▸ Collaborate closely — work alongside quant researchers, software developers and traders who all write code and understand the craft.

Eighteen quantitative researchers across six nationalities — thirteen PhDs and five MScs — with expertise spanning statistical analysis, mathematical modelling and machine learning. No red tape, no sales layer between you and your work. Technical discussions are valued, ownership is expected, and the team genuinely enjoys what it does. Successes are celebrated.

✓ Strong foundations in mathematics and statistics — you think rigorously, not just empirically. ✓ End-to-end ML experience — from data generation and wrangling through model calibration, validation and live monitoring. You've built systems that run in production, not just experiments that ran in a notebook.

✓ Research depth — whether from academia or industry, you can point to work where you designed experiments, validated results, and drew meaningful conclusions from data. Published research is a strong positive signal but not a requirement.
✓ Solid Python skills — data-wrangling, numerical programming, production-quality code.
✓ Computer science fundamentals — you understand what's happening under the hood.
✓ A scientific, inquisitive mindset — you ask why the model works, not just whether it does.

Financial markets knowledge is a plus but not a prerequisite — the team cares more about how you think and build than which domain you come from. If your background is in physical sciences, engineering, or applied mathematics and you've been working on hard prediction problems with real data, we want to hear from you.

Nice to have: If you have experience with time-series forecasting, out-of-core datasets, PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost or CatBoost, C# or C/C++, and a PhD or MSc in engineering, physics, computer science, mathematics or economics that is all awesome!
Pension, health insurance and 30 days of vacation
Hybrid working model - 4 days in the office, 1 day remote
Flat hierarchy — high trust, high autonomy, real freedom to make decisions
Short feedback cycles — from inception to deployment can be hours, not quarters
Strong team culture with company events and trips

4409416468,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-worker-in-data-analytics-at-kamstrup-4409416468?position=36&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=kGDi5VazI8tMDtnPOyCrNg%3D%3D,Student Worker in Data & Analytics,Kamstrup,"Skanderborg, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Do you want to gain hands-on experience with modern analytics tooling and real business-critical datasets? Are you motivated to work in an international green tech organization where your work contributes to a more sustainable future? Do you want to develop your professional skills in close collaboration with experienced and passionate colleagues?
Here Is What The Job Entails

As a Student Worker in Data & Analytics, you will support our Data & Analytics team in rebuilding and modernizing core business metrics and Power BI reporting, as the organization transitions from a legacy BI stack to a modern cloud-based data platform.
Developing and maintaining Power BI reports and semantic models aligned to core business metrics
Defining and documenting business metrics in collaboration with stakeholders to ensure consistent reporting
Supporting report validation and quality assurance across the Power BI environment
Contributing to self-service BI by creating user guides and documentation
Assisting with ad hoc analysis and report requests from business stakeholders

This role offers you the chance to develop your skills in Data & Analytics trough hands-on experience with modern tools like Power BI and Databricks. The role combines hands-on development, close collaboration with business stakeholders, and day-to-day support to ensure reliable, well-documented, and user-friendly analytics solutions.

With direct reference to our Head of Data & Analytics, you will join our newly formed Data & Analytics team currently consisting of 5 dedicated colleagues. Together, we have a clear mission to build a high-quality analytics foundation while modernizing how we deliver and report on data products. The team works closely with stakeholders across the value chain to translate business questions into clear metrics and robust data models that enable Kamstrup’s future growth. Furthermore, we foster a collaborative environment where sparring is highly encouraged, and student workers are seen as an integral and important part of the team.
Did you know? We are a highly diverse team with members from North America, Central America, Europe and Asia.
Less About Us, More About You

We are looking for a curious and motivated student worker with a strong interest in analytics and reporting that support real business decisions. You achieve work-related goals by working in a structured and service-oriented manner as well as being eager to learn from and collaborate with professional colleagues. As you develop in the role, you will also increasingly take ownership of meaningful projects.
In order to succeed we imagine that you:
Are currently studying a degree within Data Science, IT, Software Engineering, Business Analytics, or similar
Have hands-on experience with Power BI from studies, projects, or related student work
Have a solid understanding of data models and basic SQL skills (Databricks or Python experience is a plus)
Have an interest in business metrics, semantic modelling, and how data drives decisions
Bring an open and growth-oriented mindset
As we are an international organisation it is important that you are fluent in English. Danish is considered an advantage.
Whilst you may not meet all the qualifications listed above, you could still be a good candidate for the position. If you find the role intriguing, do not hesitate to apply.
We are more than an ordinary workplace

We cherish the good colleagueship which we all contribute to, the only limit is your engagement. As we are a big organization you are bound to find one or more colleagues who share the same interest as you. This might be in our board game club, on our cycling team, during our yearly summer festival, or on the padel court. For example, our Team lead, Service Delivery is also our volunteer padel instructor while one of our Software Test Engineers run weekly spinning classes.
Besides the best colleagues you could imagine, we also offer:
Work life balance through flexible working hours
Employee benefits including a healthy canteen, fitness facilities, in-house dental clinic, hairdresser, and a free health clinic
An active employee association that fosters a collaborative and supportive workplace community
Beneficial health care and insurance package
Do you want to be a part of Kamstrup?
Please submit your application by using the link below. Applications are reviewed on a continuous basis, and you can expect a response within six weeks.
The job will be located at Kamstrup's headquarters in Stilling, between Aarhus and Skanderborg.
If you have any questions about the position, please contact Head of Data & Analytics, Claire Bastholm Dooley on phone: +45 89 93 10 00.

At Kamstrup, you'll become part of a close-knit community that genuinely cares about one another, fostering connections both in and out of the workplace. Our team comprises innovators, dreamers, and doers, united by an unyielding passion to challenge the status quo, find better solutions, and constantly push the boundaries of what's possible by being driving by our purpose: We Make What You Measure Matter. With an unwavering commitment to growth, we offer opportunities for you to leverage your skills and drive our business to new heights, empowering you to leave your mark on our future and grow with us.
Explore more on our website, see our annual report, and stay updated through LinkedIn for the latest news and job openings.

  • Supporting report validation and quality assurance across the Power BI environment
  • Contributing to self-service BI by creating user guides and documentation
  • Assisting with ad hoc analysis and report requests from business stakeholders


This role offers you the chance to develop your skills in Data &
column4:
Analytics trough hands-on experience with modern tools like Power BI and Databricks. The role combines hands-on development, close collaboration with business stakeholders, and day-to-day support to ensure reliable, well-documented, and user-friendly analytics solutions.

Meet your future team

With direct reference to our Head of Data &
column5: Analytics, you will join our newly formed Data &amp
column6:
Analytics team currently consisting of 5 dedicated colleagues. Together, we have a clear mission to build a high-quality analytics foundation while modernizing how we deliver and report on data products. The team works closely with stakeholders across the value chain to translate business questions into clear metrics and robust data models that enable Kamstrup’s future growth. Furthermore, we foster a collaborative environment where sparring is highly encouraged, and student workers are seen as an integral and important part of the team.

Did you know? We are a highly diverse team with members from North America, Central America, Europe and Asia.

Less About Us, More About You

We are looking for a curious and motivated student worker with a strong interest in analytics and reporting that support real business decisions. You achieve work-related goals by working in a structured and service-oriented manner as well as being eager to learn from and collaborate with professional colleagues. As you develop in the role, you will also increasingly take ownership of meaningful projects.

In order to succeed we imagine that you:

  • Are currently studying a degree within Data Science, IT, Software Engineering, Business Analytics, or similar
  • Have hands-on experience with Power BI from studies, projects, or related student work
  • Have a solid understanding of data models and basic SQL skills (Databricks or Python experience is a plus)
  • Have an interest in business metrics, semantic modelling, and how data drives decisions
  • Bring an open and growth-oriented mindset


As we are an international organisation it is important that you are fluent in English. Danish is considered an advantage.

Whilst you may not meet all the qualifications listed above, you could still be a good candidate for the position. If you find the role intriguing, do not hesitate to apply.

We are more than an ordinary workplace

We cherish the good colleagueship which we all contribute to, the only limit is your engagement. As we are a big organization you are bound to find one or more colleagues who share the same interest as you. This might be in our board game club, on our cycling team, during our yearly summer festival, or on the padel court. For example, our Team lead, Service Delivery is also our volunteer padel instructor while one of our Software Test Engineers run weekly spinning classes.

Besides the best colleagues you could imagine, we also offer:

  • Work life balance through flexible working hours
  • Employee benefits including a healthy canteen, fitness facilities, in-house dental clinic, hairdresser, and a free health clinic
  • An active employee association that fosters a collaborative and supportive workplace community
  • Beneficial health care and insurance package


Do you want to be a part of Kamstrup?

Please submit your application by using the link below. Applications are reviewed on a continuous basis, and you can expect a response within six weeks.

The job will be located at Kamstrup's headquarters in Stilling, between Aarhus and Skanderborg.

If you have any questions about the position, please contact Head of Data &
column7:
Analytics, Claire Bastholm Dooley on phone: +45 89 93 10 00.

Get to know us

At Kamstrup, you'll become part of a close-knit community that genuinely cares about one another, fostering connections both in and out of the workplace. Our team comprises innovators, dreamers, and doers, united by an unyielding passion to challenge the status quo, find better solutions, and constantly push the boundaries of what's possible by being driving by our purpose: We Make What You Measure Matter. With an unwavering commitment to growth, we offer opportunities for you to leverage your skills and drive our business to new heights, empowering you to leave your mark on our future and grow with us.

 Explore more on our website, see our annual report, and stay updated through LinkedIn for the latest news and job openings.

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. The Agoda Content department is currently looking for a curious and questioning self-starter and proactive analyst, who will help drive decision making with insights from content, marketing and partner performance data.

About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Overview:

The Agoda Content department is currently looking for a curious and questioning self-starter and proactive analyst, who will help drive decision making with insights from content, marketing and partner performance data.

You will get the opportunity to own analytical projects to direct our department’s focus, and prioritize our actions. By joining Agoda, you will become part of a truly international team, with leading experts of different backgrounds coming from 50+ countries around the world. This is a genuine opportunity to gain valuable experience in a challenging and cutting-edge tech environment. The ideal candidate would have a passion for high-converting content, great user-experience, data and analysis tools and methods. This position reports to the Associate Director of Content Operations, in Bangkok, Thailand.

Main responsibilities:

  • Understand Content goals and KPIs and be able to align reports and insights based on them. Identify and investigate trends, anomalies and opportunities to improve Agoda’s Content strategy.
  • Identify content opportunities that drive customer value, bookings and conversion
  • Help build business cases around the opportunity and get buy-in from stakeholders
  • Ensure appropriate data/tools/dashboards to measure execution and enable deeper analysis
  • Track execution and report up in regular updates
  • Work with product, data/BI team and IT to create data resources and build appropriate reporting
  • Work with Content team leads to understand their business needs and identify opportunities in terms of team actions prioritization and focus.
  • Use multiple data sources to report Content projects insights and impact

  • Coordinate with other Cross Functional departments like Analytics, Partner Services, and Product Owners

Vi søger en nyuddannet økonom til en stilling som dataanalytiker.
Du Vil Arbejde Med Kvantitative Analyser Og Bidrage Til At Opbygge Et Solidt Vidensgrundlag. Dine Opgaver Vil Bl.a. Være At
analysere internationale datasæt, fx datasæt fra PISA-undersøgelserne
gennemføre registerdataanalyser, fx i forhold til fravær
bidrage til udvikling af nye analyser
Du er nyuddannet økonom (fx cand.polit., cand.oecon. eller lign.) og har:
erfaring med dataanalyse (fx fra studie eller speciale)
interesse for internationale data og analyser
det er en fordel, hvis du har arbejdet med analyser om faglige resultater, fravær, AI, skærmbrug eller kreativ tænkning

Kontor for Viden og Puljer arbejder med og rådgiver om puljer, evalueringer, analyser, undersøgelser og formidling af viden. Kontoret arbejder for at udvikle anvendelsen af viden og forskning i dagtilbud, skoler og uddannelser og for at gøre det lettere at arbejde data- og vidensbaseret i ministeriets sektorrettede opgaver. Kontoret varetager desuden den praktisk-administrative del af Børne- og Undervisningsministeriets puljeadministration.
Du bliver ansat som fuldmægtig efter gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten. Stillingen er på fuld tid med ansættelse fra 1. august 2026.
Lyder det som noget for dig?
Send din ansøgning, cv og eksamensbevis senest onsdag den 27. maj 2026.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte teamleder Rune Hejlskov Schjerbeck på telefon 24899432 eller e-mail rune.hejlskov.schjerbeck@stukuvm.dk. Du kan også læse mere om Børne- og Undervisningsministeriet på vores hjemmeside uvm.dk.
Læs mere omkring hvordan det er at være ansat og mød en medarbejder her: https://stukuvm.dk/job-i-styrelsen/moed-en-medarbejder/.
Vi ønsker, at medarbejdernes sammensætning afspejler samfundets og ser frem til din ansøgning uanset personlig baggrund – vi ser kun på kvalifikationer og kompetencer.
Som arbejdsplads er vi kendetegnet ved høj faglighed, stærke samarbejdsrelationer og et stort engagement i at skabe resultater, der kan mærkes i praksis.

Styrelsen for Undervisning og Kvalitet (STUK) er del af Børne- og Undervisningsministeriet. Vi bidrager til fagligt stærke uddannelser til alle. Vi fremmer kvalitetsudvikling på uddannelser og dagtilbud på Børne- og Undervisningsministeriets område, varetager tilsyn med kvaliteten på uddannelser og understøtter en effektiv institutionsdrift.

Vi understøtter den løbende implementering af politiske reformer ved at inddrage ny viden og data. I tæt dialog med skoler, institutioner, foreninger og arbejdsmarkedets parter arbejder vi for at gøre det lettere at give god undervisning og skabe gode dagtilbud.
Styrelsen er lokaliseret i København og Holbæk.
Vi ønsker, at medarbejdernes sammensætning afspejler samfundets og ser frem til din ansøgning uanset personlig baggrund – vi ser kun på kvalifikationer og kompetencer.

Dine arbejdsopgaver

Du Vil Arbejde Med Kvantitative Analyser Og Bidrage Til At Opbygge Et Solidt Vidensgrundlag. Dine Opgaver Vil Bl.a. Være At

  • analysere internationale datasæt, fx datasæt fra PISA-undersøgelserne
  • arbejde med surveydata og spørgeskemaundersøgelser
  • gennemføre registerdataanalyser, fx i forhold til fravær
  • udarbejde analyser og notater
  • bidrage til udvikling af nye analyser

Din profil

Du er nyuddannet økonom (fx cand.polit., cand.oecon. eller lign.) og har:

  • stærke kvantitative kompetencer
  • erfaring med dataanalyse (fx fra studie eller speciale)
  • interesse for internationale data og analyser
  • det er en fordel, hvis du har arbejdet med analyser om faglige resultater, fravær, AI, skærmbrug eller kreativ tænkning

Om kontoret

Kontor for Viden og Puljer arbejder med og rådgiver om puljer, evalueringer, analyser, undersøgelser og formidling af viden. Kontoret varetager desuden den praktisk-administrative del af Børne- og Undervisningsministeriets puljeadministration.

Du bliver ansat som fuldmægtig efter gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten. Stillingen er på fuld tid med ansættelse fra 1. august 2026.

Ansættelsessted: Tidemandsvej 1, 4300 Holbæk.

Lyder det som noget for dig?

Send din ansøgning, cv og eksamensbevis senest onsdag den 27. maj 2026.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte teamleder Rune Hejlskov Schjerbeck på telefon 24899432 eller e-mail rune.hejlskov.schjerbeck@stukuvm.dk. Du kan også læse mere om Børne- og Undervisningsministeriet på vores hjemmeside uvm.dk.

Som arbejdsplads er vi kendetegnet ved høj faglighed, stærke samarbejdsrelationer og et stort engagement i at skabe resultater, der kan mærkes i praksis.

Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:

  • Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
  • Label or classify content based on project guidelines
  • Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material

Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $22 per hour equivalent.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in any discipline
  • Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
  • Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
  • Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
  • Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence

Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?

  • Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
  • Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
  • Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise
