4403417785,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-worker-data-at-bankdata-4403417785?position=43&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=csmKiwj35IQ3UjIWjN9MZQ%3D%3D,Student Worker (Data),Bankdata,"Fredericia, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Vil du arbejde med data, storytelling og Power BI i en af Danmarks vigtigste FinTech‑virksomheder?

Vi søger to nysgerrige og engagerede studentermedhjælpere til vores Data Analytics Team. Her får du mulighed for at arbejde tæt sammen med erfarne Data Analysts og blive en del af et målrettet dataområde, der er i fuld gang med at opbygge et solidt datafællesskab – både internt i Bankdata og sammen med vores kunder.

Du bliver en del af et team, der i dag består af fem Data Analysts, hvis fornemste opgave er at få omsat data til indsigter, der kan understøtte forretningsudviklingen hos vores kunder, samt understøtte interne funktioner i Bankdata. Data er ikke bare data, og teamet arbejder med principper inden for visualisering, storytelling og datamodellering, for at sikre en høj kvalitet og anvendelighed af rapporterne. I teamet er hverdagen præget af faglig sparring, videndeling og ønsker om at skabe indsigter ud fra data.
Dit bidrag – data, visualisering og Power BI i praksis
Som studentermedhjælper i Data Analytics kommer du til at arbejde hands‑on med rapportering, storytelling og visualisering – og hjælpe både kolleger og kunder videre i brugen af data og Power BI.
Dine opgaver vil bl.a. være, at:
Benytte dine analytiske evner, Benytte dine analytiske evner, indsigt i brugerbehov og kommunikation til at videreudvikle og bygge nye Power BI‑løsninger (semantiske modeller og visualiseringer)
Være med til at gøre rapporter mere brugervenlige, overskuelige og værdiskabende ved at tænke storytelling ind
Understøtte andre teams i at komme godt videre med udvikling og forbedring af deres egne Power BI‑løsninger
Bidrage til flytning og modning af rapportering på vores nye Power BI cloud‑platform
På sigt forberede semantiske modeller til AI løsninger
Du skal ikke kunne det hele fra dag ét. Det vigtigste er, at du har gåpåmod, nysgerrighed og lyst til at blive klogere – så sørger vi for sparring og oplæring undervejs.
Er i gang med sidste år af din bachelor eller i begyndelsen af din kandidatuddannelse
Studerer inden for data, BI, IT, økonomi, matematik, datalogi eller lignende
Har interesse for data, metrikker, rapportering og visualisering
Har lyst til at hjælpe andre og trives med samarbejde
Arbejder struktureret og er nysgerrig på at lære nyt
Det er en fordel, hvis du har kendskab til Power BI, og hvis du har erfaring med AI, er det et plus – men ikke et krav. Motivation og læringslyst vægter højest.
Et godt fagligt og socialt fællesskab med dygtige og hjælpsomme kolleger
Mulighed for at omsætte teori til praksis i en forretningskritisk IT‑organisation
Reelt ansvar og opgaver, hvor du arbejder med forretningskritiske problemstillinger
Fleksibilitet, så studie og arbejde hænger godt sammen
Indblik i, hvordan Data og Analytics bruges i stor skala i finanssektoren
Vi holder samtaler løbende og ansætter, når vi har fundet de rette kandidater. Har du spørgsmål er du velkommen til at kontakte Manager for teamet, Ditte Mølgård Heide-Jørgensen, på 21 16 24 38.
Send din ansøgning og dit CV hurtigst muligt – vi ser frem til at høre fra dig.

Bankdata bygger software til finanssektoren. Vi er næsten 1000 kolleger på holdet, og vi udvikler digitale produkter, platforme og infrastruktur, som ca. hver tredje dansker, 200.000 virksomheder og flere tusinde bankansatte bruger hver dag.

Vi tror på struktureret personlig udvikling, et solidt kollegialt fællesskab og at ambitiøse medarbejdere med fleksible og bæredygtige arbejdsliv præsterer bedre. Bliv klogere på arbejdslivet i Bankdata her.

Hos Bankdata arbejder vi målrettet med at fremme mangfoldighed og diversitet blandt vores medarbejdere, og vi opfordrer derfor alle uanset kønsidentitet, alder og kulturel baggrund til at søge job hos os.
Bankdata er en af Danmarks vigtigste teknologivirksomheder. Vi går på arbejde for at finde teknologiske svar på finanssektorens forretningsproblemer. For at udvikle og bygge fremtidens digitale bank.

Hvem er du?

Vi forestiller os, at du:

  • Er i gang med sidste år af din bachelor eller i begyndelsen af din kandidatuddannelse

  • Har interesse for data, metrikker, rapportering og visualisering
  • Har lyst til at hjælpe andre og trives med samarbejde
  • Arbejder struktureret og er nysgerrig på at lære nyt


Det er en fordel, hvis du har kendskab til Power BI, og hvis du har erfaring med AI, er det et plus – men ikke et krav. Motivation og læringslyst vægter højest.

  • Reelt ansvar og opgaver, hvor du arbejder med forretningskritiske problemstillinger

4388690556,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/quantitative-analyst-at-enzee-commodities-a-s-4388690556?position=46&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=zwQKlhbu8ttZSnNur0HqEg%3D%3D,Quantitative Analyst,ENZEE Commodities A/S,"Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Do you want to gain a deep understanding of the power market and contribute directly to trading decisions? Do you thrive in a high‑paced, data‑driven environment where your analytical skills shape tomorrow’s strategies? If you’re ready for a role with real responsibility and impact, join our team as a Quantitative Analyst and help drive disciplined, high‑quality trading outcomes.

We are seeking a highly motivated individual with strong quantitative skills to join our team as a Quantitative Analyst. In this role, you will work closely with the team, applying data‑driven insights, modelling, and risk thinking to take ownership of disciplined trading decisions. Your ability to transform analysis and code into actionable insights will directly contribute to improving the quality and profitability of our trading decisions.

In this role, you will gain deep insights into our trading strategies and tools that provide a significant competitive advantage. You can expect a steep learning curve and growth opportunities in an environment where flat hierarchy and team spirit drive our success. Market Analysis: Conduct in-depth, data‑driven analysis of power markets including supply and demand fundamentals, regulatory developments, weather‑driven patterns, and geopolitical impacts to uncover actionable trading opportunities and support strategic decision‑making.

Risk Management: Implement robust risk management practices by monitoring position limits, conducting stress testing, and assessing exposure to volatile market conditions to ensure controlled and compliant trading activities.

Tool Development: Develop, maintain, and optimize a range of analytical, real‑time monitoring, and reporting tools that enhance trading decision‑making and operational efficiency. Build automated dashboards and data pipelines to produce accurate, timely reports on trading performance, risk metrics, and key market trends.
Model Development: Continuously improve trading models through back testing and market‑driven data analysis to strengthen and support profitable trading decisions.
Collaboration: Work closely with the team to enhance trading strategies and technical setup, ensuring clear and consistent communication across all team members.

Trading Application: Apply insights from analysis, modelling and risk assessment to confidently support and execute well‑founded trading decisions that meet all required risk, compliance, and performance standards.

Hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in mathematics‑economics, economics, statistics, engineering, or another quantitative field.

Demonstrate excellent problem‑solving skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to adapt quickly to changing market conditions.
Communicate clearly and effectively, presenting technical and analytical concepts in a way that is easy for both technical and non‑technical team members to understand.

4405324072,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-assistant-to-support-biomass-market-analysis-through-data-science-at-%C3%B8rsted-4405324072?position=48&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=JUbRd9Q5fZ1MwXLyoXqsSw%3D%3D,Student Assistant to support biomass market analysis through data science,Ørsted,"Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Join us in this role where you’ll help model data relevant for the energy markets, code analytics tools for data driven decision making and communicate results and conclusions to colleagues and management.

You’ll be part of the Fuel & Biocarbon Supply Team where you, together with your colleagues, will l provide analytical insights and support to a dedicated and collaborative team of traders, originators, planners, business developers and management responsible for sourcing biomass to Ørsted’s combined heat and power plants. English is the primary language in the department, and this position is scheduled for 20 hours weekly. As a team, we believe in collaboration and always help each other to achieve the best results. We have an informal and open culture where we learn from each other and appreciate open-mindedness and commitment.
You’ll play an important role in:
developing autonomous and efficient flows of data
building analytics tools and dashboards used for decision support
overseeing from backend to frontend our tools and models
communicating and collaborating with team members and external stakeholders
maintain databases and IT tools up to date
To succeed in the role, you:
are currently studying IT, data science, engineering, economics, business, mathematics, or another relevant discipline, and have at least two years left before graduating
possess good skills within data analysis and comfortable with code-based analytics
have experience with e.g., Python, R or SQL and a wish to improve your skills
are interested in visualizing and communicating results using dashboard tools such as Power BI
speak and write English fluently - understanding Danish both verbally and in writing will be an advantage however not a requirement.

Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.
Send your application and CV to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.
As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

4412776719,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/studentermedhj%C3%A6lper-til-data-og-bi-i-sampension-at-sampension-4412776719?position=49&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=47ZK6GfRvx5UtiNy77GMZA%3D%3D,Studentermedhjælper til Data og BI i Sampension,Sampension,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Studentermedhjælper til Data & BI i Sampension

Har du lyst til at arbejde med data, Power BI og AI, i en pensionsvirksomhed, hvor du får praktisk erfaring med data, analyser og moderne Microsoft-teknologi og hvor dit arbejde kan ses og bruges i en organisation, der arbejder målrettet med at blive mere datadrevet? Så har vi måske studiejobbet til dig.

I Data & BI teamet arbejder vi for, at Sampensions data er pålidelige, dokumenterede og let tilgængelige, og at de omsættes til indsigter. Vores dataplatform er bygget med en Microsoft-first tilgang og tager afsæt i teknologier som Azure, Fabric, Power BI, Copilot og Purview, alt dette er med til at skabe en reel forretningsværdi i Sampension.

Som studentermedhjælper bliver du en del, af et fagligt stærkt og hjælpsomt team med Data Engineers, BI-specialister og Data Governance-profiler, hvor vi prioriterer sparring, læring og ansvar, vi forventer at du har lyst til at lære også når det er svært.
Dine opgaver vil bl.a. være at:
Hjælpe med udrulning og governance af Copilot som AI-assistent i Sampension
Støtte udvikling og vedligehold af vores dataplatform i Fabric/Azure
Udvikle og vedligeholde Power BI-modeller og rapporter
Løse og lede mindre selvstændige data- og analyseopgaver

Arbejdstiden forventes at være 15-20 timer om ugen, og der vil være fleksible forhold i forbindelse med både eksaminer og ferie, ligesom der også vil være mulighed for at lægge yderligere timer i perioder, hvis det ønskes.

Du er i gang med en relevant videregående uddannelse inden for IT, data eller økonomi (fx datalogi, HA(it.) el.lign.) og har interesse for data, analyse og datadrevne beslutninger. Har du kendskab til Power BI, SQL, Python eller Copilot er det et plus, men ikke et krav.
går struktureret og ansvarligt til dine opgaver
er nysgerrig, opsøgende og trives med at samarbejde samt selvstændigt arbejde
motiveres af et travlt og resultatorienteret miljø
har gode analytiske evner og er god til skriftlig formidling
Bliv en del af en velfungerende organisation i udvikling

Vi tilbyder dig at blive del af en velfungerende, kvalitetsbevidst og løsningsorienteret organisation. Et miljø med fokus på faglig og personlig udvikling, og på at skabe resultater til fordel for kunderne.
Løn og ansættelsesvilkår fastsættes efter kvalifikationer og omfatter naturligvis en attraktiv pensionsordning betalt af Sampension.

Vi bor med udsigt til vandet i et lyst og åbent domicil tæt på byen. Derudover har vi en række personalegoder, fx idrætsforening, motionsrum, forskellige sociale arrangementer og en rigtig god personalerestaurant.
Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig
Vi indkalder løbende til samtaler. Så send hurtigst muligt dit CV og et par linjer om dig selv og hvorfor du skal være vores nye kollega.
Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte IT-leder Steen Norstrøm på mail SNS@sampension.dk eller BI Developer Niklas Sachs Tastum på mail NST@sampension.dk.

Sampension er et af Danmarks største pensionsselskaber med ca. 325.000 kunder og medlemmer. Vi er kundeejet og administrerer arbejdsmarkeds- og firmapensioner for Sampension Livsforsikring, Pensionskassen Arkitekter & Designere, Pensionskassen for Jordbrugsakademikere & Dyrlæger samt ISP Pension.

Brænder du for ansvar og direkte indflydelse på modellering og design af dataprodukter så de både tilgodeser rapportlæsere, analytikere og andre udviklere i Datadomænet?
Er du klar til at blive en del af vores rejse? Nu kan du blive data engineer i vores datadomæne, hvor der venter dig et udfordrende job.

Alm. Brand Group er inde i en rivende udvikling, hvor der er stor appetit på data løsninger. Vi ønsker at kunne tiltrække og fastholde de dygtigste medarbejdere, der i fællesskab leverer det bedste til vores kunder og sikrer et stærkt fundament for vækst og lønsomhed. Kendte for ordentlighed, professionalisme og for at kunne indfri vores kunders behov.

Vi har over de sidste par år bygget en ny Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) i Azure Cloud. Platformen er primært bygget vha. Azure Databricks og følger deres Lakehouse tilgang. Vi har haft fokus på at integrere Codan i vores systemlandskab, som vi er lykkedes med. Næste fase er at vi har kvalitetsdata til rådighed, så vores analytikere kan udnytte data optimalt, men også især den transformation, som vi står overfor i forbindelse med AI Agenter.
Datadomænet består af 3 afdelinger – Data Engineering, Data Solutions og AI. Data Engineering afdelingen, som du bliver ansat i, består af 11 danske data engineers og pt. 6 polske konsulenter.

I hverdagen indgår man i et team med 4-6 kollegaer fordelt på forskellige roller – manager, analytiker og data engineer. De forskellige teams er orienteret mod vores kerneområder som f.eks. skade, salg m.fl. Udover dette, så investerer vi også i fagligheden hos os selv via review møder, hvor vi modner løsninger og platformen i fællesskab.
Tæt kontakt til kravstillere i forretningen
Alsidige opgaver med fx workshops, datamodeller og udvikling af data pipelines

Et godt og ambitiøst arbejdsmiljø, hvor vi sætter faglighed højt

Hos Alm. Brand Group har vi fokus på det hele menneske og tilbyder dig en hel palette af medarbejdergoder: Det er bl.a. hybridarbejde, 5 feriefridage, sundhedsforsikring, en flot pensionsordning og bruttolønsordning.

Vi forestiller os, at du er ambitiøs og brænder for at forvandle rå data til indsigt. Du formår at balancere teori og praksis i en til tider hektisk hverdag. Samtidig er du nysgerrig – både på det forretningsmæssige og det tekniske. Du sidder i dag i en lignende stilling som konsulent eller intern og har bevist, at du både kan samarbejde og omdanne forretningskrav til brugbare dataløsninger.
Omsætte forretningskrav til løsningsdesign og data modeller.
Udvikle og vedligeholde data pipelines og data produkter.
Bidrage til vores fælles kodebase og tage ejerskab for elementer heri.
Sikre at vores løsninger er bygget på et godt pragmatisk fundament.

3-5 års erfaring med ETL, ELT, Data Warehouse og generel data-arkitektur i en moderne dataplatform.

Gode dansk- og engelskkundskaber, skriftligt og mundtligt
Det er et plus, hvis du har erfaring med en eller flere af de teknologier og metoder vi anvender: Azure, SQL, Spark, Git, Python, CI/CD, streaming og terraform.

4411772719,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analyst-rgm-promotional-excellence-interim-at-pandora-4411772719?position=52&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=6F8j4D9hGqveCAo67Xp3Hw%3D%3D,"Analyst, RGM, Promotional Excellence - Interim",Pandora,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","If you love working with market insights, consumer data, and advanced analytics to translate messy, large-scale data into decisions that move the business, this role puts you at the heart of one of Pandora's most strategically significant growth levers: Revenue Growth Management, with a focus on Promotional Excellence!

This is not a traditional analyst role. You'll own the analytical infrastructure and intelligence frameworks that shape how Pandora competes – working across competitor landscape monitoring, consumer behaviour during key promotional periods, and the data pipelines that make it all possible. Beyond analytics, there is real opportunity to extend the scope of the role into cross-functional workstreams.

You'll own Pandora's promotional competitor intelligence framework end-to-end: designing the data collection approach, building scalable pipelines in PBI and Alteryx, and producing structured monthly reporting on competitor promotional intensity, mechanics, and market dynamics. You'll surface patterns that others miss and translate them into forward-looking strategic recommendations for Merchandising and senior leadership to inform our promotional strategy.

You'll bring a consumer lens to promotional strategy. You'll be analysing customer response to promotional events (including basket behaviour, conversion, repeat purchase, and segment-level uplift) to understand what drives genuine value versus what simply pulls forward demand. You'll partner with Consumer Insights teams to develop transactional signals into a coherent view of the promotional consumer.

You'll lead quantitative evaluation of promotional campaigns: uplift modelling, elasticity analysis, ROD (Return on Discount) measurement, and A/B or quasi-experimental frameworks where data allows. You'll enhance the quality of insights in our KCP and quarterly post-mortems, going beyond reporting to generate testable hypotheses and clear recommendations.

You're curious about consumer behaviour and competitive dynamics, not just metrics. You know how to frame a market intelligence question, find creative ways to answer it with data, and communicate it in a way that shapes decisions. You have a structured and proactive approach to your work, and you're comfortable with ambiguity. You hold yourself to a high standard of analytical rigour and storytelling quality equally. You enjoy working cross-functionally, and you have the ability to manage various stakeholders.

You have approximately 3-4 years in an analytically intensive role: Commercial Analytics, Advanced Analytics, Consumer Insights, RGM/Pricing, or a data-forward Consulting background, with a track record of building things and owning outcomes, not just reporting on them. You are comfortable with causal inference concepts (uplift, incrementality, elasticity), consumer segmentation, and working with large, imperfect, real-world datasets. Bringing experience integrating multiple data sources (transactional, panel, survey, or third-party market data) would be a clear advantage.
Additionally, we hope that you have and can demonstrate experience in at least 2 of the below:
Power BI: Strong dashboard development skills – you build reports that are clean and navigable for reporting and decision-making. Familiarity with DAX, Power Query, and data model.
SQL: You are comfortable writing your own queries and can leverage AI and data engineer input to pull your own data.
Databricks: Confident working in a Databricks environment – querying large datasets, building and running notebooks, and collaborating on data pipelines at scale.
Alteryx: Hands-on experience building analytical workflows in Alteryx
column2: able to design processes to streamline workflows and enable data-driven insights.

We process applications on a continuous basis, which is why we encourage you to send your application as soon as possible. You can also read more about Pandora on our corporate site www.pandoragroup.com.

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs around 39,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery with 100% recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 32.5 billion (EUR 4.4 billion) in 2025.

4404764059,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-team-data-foundation-at-trackunit-4404764059?position=53&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=aIiTKbqUpXFs%2FEN6E0Gg0w%3D%3D,Data Engineer - Team Data Foundation,Trackunit,"Kolding, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Mission: Process massive IoT data volumes reliably and fast.

We're looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Data Foundation team at Trackunit. Our team builds and operates IrisX Analytics - a customer-facing data platform powered by Databricks that gives construction companies direct access to their fleet and equipment data. Every second, telemetry streams in from millions of devices worldwide, and the volumes are only growing.

The challenge ahead is exciting: we need to scale IrisX Analytics to serve many more customers, keep data latency low as volumes grow, and enable AI-driven insights on top of some of the largest telematics datasets in the industry. We work closely with Databricks to push the boundaries of what the platform can do - both for our internal data lake and for our customers.

You'll be attached to one of our hubs in Aarhus, Aalborg, Copenhagen, or Kolding. We work remote-first, but the data team is mainly in Aarhus and Aalborg - so expect to join us there from time to time.

If you're excited about building a data platform that turns massive real-time IoT streams into something customers can act on, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world's most essential industries!

A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.
International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.

We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.

Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.

Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.

You think of data not just as pipelines, but as a product. You care about who consumes the data - whether it's a customer running queries in their workspace or an internal team building on top of your platform - and you design with their experience in mind.

You bring a strong background in data engineering and are proficient in Python, SQL, and Spark. From streaming ingestion to historical backfills, from performance tuning to choosing the right partitioning strategy - these are the kinds of challenges that motivate you.
You have hands-on experience with Databricks and Delta Lake, or similar lakehouse technologies.
You understand the medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) and know how to model data for different consumption patterns.
You're curious about how AI and large language models can be enabled on top of large-scale data - and you're excited to help build the foundation that makes that possible.
Experience with Kafka, multi-tenant data architectures, or IoT/telematics data is a strong plus.

Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.
Shape the future of IrisX Analytics as a data product - you will have a direct impact on what our customers experience and the value they get from their data.

Design, build, and run scalable data pipelines (streaming and batch) that process large volumes of IoT telematics data. You build it, you run it - and you take pride in both the quality and the reliability of what you ship.
Take responsibility for our internal data lake and long-term data preservation - keeping the platform secure, governance automated, and data trustworthy at scale.
Tackle large-scale data challenges: optimizing pipeline performance, tuning Spark jobs, and handling deduplication and late-arriving data.

Own the operational health of the platform. We use modern observability and incident tooling (Axiom, Grafana, Prometheus, FireHydrant, Slack alerting) and we expect to know about problems before our customers do.
Engage across teams - from SRE on the platform side, to professional services and customer-facing teams - to ensure the data platform delivers end-to-end.
Don't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.
Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&T team.
Interview with your potential new leader and relevant people from the team, so you'll get to know them and the team better.

Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you'll also work closely with in the job.
If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.
Offer presentation and walk-through. We're lucky to have you!

We're committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it's a way of life at Trackunit. We're proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.
The question is: Are you in?

I initiatives here.

Who are you ideally?

  • You think of data not just as pipelines, but as a product. You care about who consumes the data - whether it's a customer running queries in their workspace or an internal team building on top of your platform - and you design with their experience in mind.
  • You bring a strong background in data engineering and are proficient in Python, SQL, and Spark. From streaming ingestion to historical backfills, from performance tuning to choosing the right partitioning strategy - these are the kinds of challenges that motivate you.
  • You have hands-on experience with Databricks and Delta Lake, or similar lakehouse technologies.
  • You understand the medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) and know how to model data for different consumption patterns.
  • You're curious about how AI and large language models can be enabled on top of large-scale data - and you're excited to help build the foundation that makes that possible.
  • Experience with Kafka, multi-tenant data architectures, or IoT/telematics data is a strong plus.

Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Primary Responsibilities

Shape the future of IrisX Analytics as a data product - you will have a direct impact on what our customers experience and the value they get from their data.

Design, build, and run scalable data pipelines (streaming and batch) that process large volumes of IoT telematics data. You build it, you run it - and you take pride in both the quality and the reliability of what you ship.

Take responsibility for our internal data lake and long-term data preservation - keeping the platform secure, governance automated, and data trustworthy at scale.

Tackle large-scale data challenges: optimizing pipeline performance, tuning Spark jobs, and handling deduplication and late-arriving data.

Own the operational health of the platform. We use modern observability and incident tooling (Axiom, Grafana, Prometheus, FireHydrant, Slack alerting) and we expect to know about problems before our customers do.

Engage across teams - from SRE on the platform side, to professional services and customer-facing teams - to ensure the data platform delivers end-to-end.

Our hiring process

Don't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.

Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&
column3:
T team.

Interview with your potential new leader and relevant people from the team, so you'll get to know them and the team better.

Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you'll also work closely with in the job.

If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.

Offer presentation and walk-through. We're lucky to have you!

Coming Together To Connect Construction

We're committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it's a way of life at Trackunit. We're proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.

The question is: Are you in?

Build the data backbone that powers financial decision-making across a global healthcare leader. Join our core backend team where your pipelines and data solutions will enable Finance teams to operate with speed, accuracy, and insight—ultimately supporting our mission to change lives for millions of patients.

As a Data Engineer in our core backend team, you'll build and operate the scalable data infrastructure that underpins our Finance Data Centre. Working primarily with Databricks, you'll build robust ETL/ELT solutions that transform raw data into reliable, timely insights for our global Finance organization. This is a hands-on technical role where your work directly enables finance teams to make data-driven decisions that matter.
Building, implementing, and operating scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT solutions in Databricks and Azure Data Factory

Operational support, production incident resolution to maintain high system reliability
Collaborating with product owners, analysts, and architects to translate business requirements into technical solutions that deliver measurable business value

Working in agile, cross-functional teams with a focus on delivering production-ready, high-quality solutions

You'll join a department, where we deliver a robust and scalable data foundation that enables the global Finance organization to operate efficiently and make insight-driven decisions. Our Finance Data Centre is strengthening its capabilities across our core technology stack—SAP BW/4HANA, Databricks, Azure and Microsoft Fabric. You'll work alongside solution architects, developers, analysts, and data engineers from diverse technical and multinational backgrounds. We design, build, and maintain data solutions that support core finance processes, with collaboration, business focus, and technical excellence at the heart of how we work. Our goal is to use data to gain new understandings, drive optimizations, and create true business value for Finance, Novo Nordisk, and the patients we serve.

In Finance, Legal & Global Solutions, we enable Novo Nordisk to fulfil our purpose by safeguarding the business, providing corporate infrastructure, and driving value creation. We are a team of 2,300 colleagues primarily based at our headquarters in Denmark and our Global Business Services centres in India and Mexico.

We're looking for an experienced Data Engineer with strong hands-on expertise in building production-grade data pipelines. You will work in cross-functional and multidisciplinary teams to deliver high-quality, production-ready solutions. You will furthermore collaborate with product owners, analysts and architects to translate business requirements into technical solutions that deliver measurable business value. Hence, bringing a collaborative mindset, understanding the business we support and engaging with stakeholders is a prerequisite.
You'll Thrive In This Role If You Bring
Strong hands-on experience with Databricks and Unity Catalog (Spark, Delta Lake, notebooks, jobs)
Proficiency in Python, SQL, and familiarity with Data Engineering frameworks
Experience with data modelling, ingestion patterns, and orchestration tools
Knowledge of cloud platform services (Azure/AWS/GCP) relevant to Databricks
Experience with data observability tools, infrastructure as code, and DevOps practices
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to translate finance needs into technical solutions
A collaborative mindset and willingness to work in agile, cross-functional teams
Strong quality and delivery focus, with the ability to produce robust, production-ready solutions
Commitment to data governance, security, and compliance
Experiences with Genie code or other relevant experiences for implementing skills
Interest in finance data and core finance processes — you'll work closely with Finance stakeholders, so business curiosity is valuable
Experience working in a regulated industry or financial organization

We are a proud life-science company, and life is our reason to exist. We’re inspired by life in all its forms and shapes, ups and downs, opportunities and challenges. For employees at Novo Nordisk, life means many things – from the building blocks of life that form the basis of ground-breaking scientific research, to our rich personal lives that motivate and energise us to perform our best at work. Ultimately, life is why we’re all here - to ensure that people can lead a life independent of chronic disease.

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Partner Development is a team of creative entrepreneurs that develop solutions for Agoda’s accommodation partners and promote Agoda’s top and bottom line growth. We design tailored business and product solutions with our partners and help them generate measurable value. Members of our team are empowered and supported to grow their market(s) or accounts. We develop win-win relationships and leverage Agoda’s unique accommodations portfolio and tech solutions to bring our partners the advantages they seek. Utilizing our strong brand and resources, we roll out new product to increase the visibility of Agoda, introduce more travelers to our great products and service and deliver significant revenues to the overall business.
In this role you'll get to

This role is designed to mainly growth opportunity identification among mid to long tail hotels, experiment execution and data tracking within Partner Development team for the global teams. You will get to work directly with the regional team to design and put in place global experimentation and initiatives to drive tangible impact in each market.
Key activities involved include but not limit to:
Apply your expertise in quantitative analysis, data mining, and the presentation of data to identify opportunities for business improvements and support your recommendations
Own end-to-end project or roll out new experiments to validate in-market impact of an initiative within Partner Development
Build dashboards, identify new and track key metrics to closely monitor team’s performance and identify quick and long term opportunities for improvements
Work closely with cross-functional teams of analysts, regional team leads, product managers and business owners to drive changes based on opportunities identified
Support global Partner Development related initiatives, including experimentation infrastructure-building and methodology standardization
Bachelor’s degree in science, computer science, statistics, economics, mathematics, or similar quantitative discipline
1-3 years experience working in a business analysis, data analysis, reporting or business strategy role
Excellent problem-solving skills including the ability to analyze and resolve complex problems using data
Team player with strong interpersonal, relationship-building, and stakeholder management skills
Coding skills for analytics and data manipulation (SQL, R, Python, Pandas, Scala)
Data visualization tool experience such as with Tableau or your weapon of choice.
Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced and rapidly changing environment
Excellent communication skills (both verbal and written in English), with proven ability to convey complex messages clearly and with conviction

4404761090,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-team-data-foundation-at-trackunit-4404761090?position=58&pageNum=0&refId=bQJR76atSXCLekn0IGL9oQ%3D%3D&trackingId=UHytSQCfANaYXprGoyAPUg%3D%3D,Data Engineer - Team Data Foundation,Trackunit,"Aalborg, North Denmark Region, Denmark","Mission: Process massive IoT data volumes reliably and fast.

We're looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Data Foundation team at Trackunit. Our team builds and operates IrisX Analytics - a customer-facing data platform powered by Databricks that gives construction companies direct access to their fleet and equipment data. Every second, telemetry streams in from millions of devices worldwide, and the volumes are only growing.

The challenge ahead is exciting: we need to scale IrisX Analytics to serve many more customers, keep data latency low as volumes grow, and enable AI-driven insights on top of some of the largest telematics datasets in the industry. We work closely with Databricks to push the boundaries of what the platform can do - both for our internal data lake and for our customers.

You'll be attached to one of our hubs in Aarhus, Aalborg, Copenhagen, or Kolding. We work remote-first, but the data team is mainly in Aarhus and Aalborg - so expect to join us there from time to time.

If you're excited about building a data platform that turns massive real-time IoT streams into something customers can act on, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world's most essential industries!

A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.
International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.

We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.

Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.

Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.

You think of data not just as pipelines, but as a product. You care about who consumes the data - whether it's a customer running queries in their workspace or an internal team building on top of your platform - and you design with their experience in mind.

You bring a strong background in data engineering and are proficient in Python, SQL, and Spark. From streaming ingestion to historical backfills, from performance tuning to choosing the right partitioning strategy - these are the kinds of challenges that motivate you.
You have hands-on experience with Databricks and Delta Lake, or similar lakehouse technologies.
You understand the medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) and know how to model data for different consumption patterns.
You're curious about how AI and large language models can be enabled on top of large-scale data - and you're excited to help build the foundation that makes that possible.
Experience with Kafka, multi-tenant data architectures, or IoT/telematics data is a strong plus.

Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.
Shape the future of IrisX Analytics as a data product - you will have a direct impact on what our customers experience and the value they get from their data.

Design, build, and run scalable data pipelines (streaming and batch) that process large volumes of IoT telematics data. You build it, you run it - and you take pride in both the quality and the reliability of what you ship.
Take responsibility for our internal data lake and long-term data preservation - keeping the platform secure, governance automated, and data trustworthy at scale.
Tackle large-scale data challenges: optimizing pipeline performance, tuning Spark jobs, and handling deduplication and late-arriving data.

Own the operational health of the platform. We use modern observability and incident tooling (Axiom, Grafana, Prometheus, FireHydrant, Slack alerting) and we expect to know about problems before our customers do.
Engage across teams - from SRE on the platform side, to professional services and customer-facing teams - to ensure the data platform delivers end-to-end.
Don't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.
Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&T team.
Interview with your potential new leader and relevant people from the team, so you'll get to know them and the team better.

Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you'll also work closely with in the job.
If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.
Offer presentation and walk-through. We're lucky to have you!

We're committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it's a way of life at Trackunit. We're proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.
The question is: Are you in?

4411197242,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/associate-analytics-lead-bi-at-novo-nordisk-4411197242?position=1&pageNum=2&refId=YzT97kHG2dY51cuxV2bh6g%3D%3D&trackingId=C0EjAyxZoH9a5wgR%2B%2FVH9g%3D%3D,Associate Analytics Lead - BI,Novo Nordisk,"Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Finance, Legal & Global Solutions

There has never been a better time to join indirect procurement at Novo Nordisk. We are driving a global cost-optimisation programme where analytics is a key enabler—converting complex data into clear insights and real business impact. If you want to build high-impact BI, elevate source-to-pay and category excellence, and succeed in a fast-moving, stakeholder-rich environment, this role is an exciting opportunity.

As an Associate Analytics Lead – BI, you will be part of the Advanced Analytics team and work in close collaboration with experienced data and BI professionals, procurement system, process specialists and category managers to generate meaningful insights from large and complex data sources. You will be responsible for turning data into actionable, timely, and impactful insights, enabling high-quality analytics and dashboards that support strategic procurement decisions and drive value creation.

Act as a trusted analytics and BI partner within Corporate Procurement, driving the development, rollout, and continuous improvement of Power BI dashboards, analytics products, and performance management.
Define, standardize, and govern procurement KPIs, ensuring transparent definitions, consistent measurement, and clear data lineage from source systems to dashboards.
Provide actionable insights across the end-to-end Source-to-Pay area to identify opportunities for process improvement, efficiency gains and user experience enhancement.
Support category management analytics, including spend and savings analysis, market intelligence, and preferred supplier usage, helping category managers drive value creation.

Corporate Procurement is the Centre of Excellence for procurement in Novo Nordisk. We are responsible for the global procurement strategy, processes, and systems. We define and drive the global procurement strategy with around 150 employees in Denmark and over 500 globally, acting as a strategic partner in executing the company’s strategy and enabling value at scale. A key enabler for taking Procurement to the next level will be to leverage holistic insights to drive value in and from procurement. The Advanced Analytics team is responsible for driving this journey.
Bachelor/Master’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, Computer Science, or a similar field.
5–7+ years of experience in BI and analytics or related fields. Experience in Procurement BI is a plus.

Extensive hands-on experience with Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, Power Query, semantic models, dashboard design, report optimisation, and deployment of scalable BI solutions. Understanding of modern BI and data platforms.

Experience with Microsoft Fabric, Azure Databricks is an advantage. You are curious, think outside the box, and are eager to explore how AI can transform procurement from passive reporting to proactive analytics that accelerate decision-making and enhance user experience.

You are structured in your problem-solving approach and able to break down complex business questions into clear analytical tasks. You are a strong team member with a can-do attitude and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.
You can navigate a complex stakeholder landscape and communicate effectively with stakeholders from different functions, seniority levels, cultures, and domain backgrounds.

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

There is, of course, more on offer here than the uniqueness of our culture and the extraordinary results we produce. Being part of a global healthcare company means opportunities to learn and develop are all around us, while our benefits are designed with your career and life stage in mind.
Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 651,000 to 956,900 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.
The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.
Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here
To ensure a fair and efficient recruitment process, please refrain from adding a photo in your CV or resume.
We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you be-come part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Rate per hour : 21 USD per hour
Time commitment per hour: 4 – 6 hours per day
This opportunity is ideal for linguists and language professionals interested in working on AI‑driven speech technologies, including Text‑to‑Speech (TTS) and Speech‑to‑Text (STT) systems.
Review and assess model outputs for TTS and STT quality, including accuracy, pronunciation, and naturalness.
Create and validate evaluation benchmarks aligned with local language usage, cultural context, and regional expectations.
Effort may increase based on project needs and quality requirements
Fluency in the Icelandic language and English proficiency.
Strong understanding of grammar, pronunciation, and regional variations
Prior experience in annotation, linguistic evaluation, QA, or AI/LLM evaluation preferred
Ability to complete structured evaluation tasks within defined timelines.

We are looking for Icelandic language speakers to support an upcoming freelancing project based on audio evaluation and multilingual benchmarking initiative.


Job Role : Data Annotator (Icelandic Language )

Rate per hour : 21 USD per hour

Time commitment per hour: 4 – 6 hours per day

Work Type: Freelancing


This opportunity is ideal for linguists and language professionals interested in working on AI‑driven speech technologies, including Text‑to‑Speech (TTS) and Speech‑to‑Text (STT) systems.


Scope of Work

✅ Audio Evaluation

  • Review and assess model outputs for TTS and STT quality, including accuracy, pronunciation, and naturalness.

✅ Multilingual Benchmarking

  • Create and validate evaluation benchmarks aligned with local language usage, cultural context, and regional expectations.

Effort Required

  • Effort may increase based on project needs and quality requirements


Ideal Candidate Profile

Kan du motivere branchens bedste kolleger og bidrage til, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar? Så er du måske vores nye souschef.

Hos Lidl bliver du en del af Europas største dagligvarekæde med 150+ butikker i Danmark. Sammen er vi drevet af et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, og på #teamlidl kommer du med på en succesfuld rejse –– på et hold med høje ambitioner til fælles. Vi har brug for stærke ledere, der giver vores medarbejdere retning og overskud, så vi sammen skaber rammerne for landets bedste indkøbsoplevelse.

Som souschef tager du aktiv del i det daglige arbejde i butikken, og får medansvar for butikkens drift og resultater. Hos os får du ansvar lige fra første arbejdsdag og bliver samtidig en del af et ambitiøst karriereudviklingsforløb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi nemlig høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen, med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig.

Som souschef i Lidl får du en ansvarsfuld rolle med spændende udfordringer og en konkurrencedygtig lønpakke. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, så du har forudsætningerne for at opnå succes i din nye stilling.

Som souschef er du med til at drive butikken og lede medarbejderne. Du har medansvar for, at butikken lever op til kundernes forventninger, så de bliver ved med at handle hos os. Ud over at bestille varer, fylde op og ekspedere kunderne ved kassen, får du også følgende opgaver, som du løser i tæt samarbejde med butikschefen:
Planlægning af ressourcer og prioritering af arbejdsopgaver
Oplæring af medarbejdere, så de efterlever Lidls værdier og butikskoncepter
Ledelse af medarbejdere så de lykkes i deres job
Sørge for, at vores butikskoncepter overholdes
Ansvar for butikken når butikschefen ikke er til stede

Du er en empatisk og stærk ledertype, der har nemt ved motivere dine medarbejdere. Du bliver respekteret for din faglighed, og du sætter en ære i at tage del i arbejdsopgaverne i den daglige drift, og være med til at sikre, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar.
Du er godt rustet til jobbet, hvis du:
Har brancheerfaring og gerne erfaring fra lignende stilling
Har gennemført et lederkursus eller er motiveret for at udvikle dig som leder
Har en venlig og professionel fremtoning
Inspirerer dine medarbejdere til at følge dit gode eksempel
Har overblik og fokus på detaljen
Ser butikken som et team og ikke er bleg for at give en hånd med, når en kollega har brug for hjælp
Har god forståelse for arbejdsprocesser, procedurer og koncepter
Som souschef i Lidl får du:
En udfordrende rolle, hvor tingene går stærkt
En konkurrencedygtig lønpakke på 26.000 – 32.500 kr. brutto om måneden
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår i henhold til gældende overenskomst med HK
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både personligt og fagligt
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.
Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.
Vores rekrutteringsproces består af en telefonsamtale og en fysisk samtale i butikken.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidloest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0635, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.
Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!

4408225422,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-database-engineer-at-envira-4408225422?position=5&pageNum=2&refId=YzT97kHG2dY51cuxV2bh6g%3D%3D&trackingId=Ak5pRenhX9jqZdarZKo%2Fkw%3D%3D,Student Database Engineer,Envira,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","About Envira

Envira turns satellite and environmental data into decision-ready intelligence for finance, government and agriculture. We combine Earth Observation, modern ML and AI, and regulatory expertise to deliver auditable, business-ready risk metrics from parcel-level agricultural insight to property-level flood scoring. You will join a small, technical team building tools that banks, insurers and public agencies use to make critical climate and financial decisions.

We are looking for an ambitious student to help us scale the data backbone behind our products. You will work closely with the core engineering and modelling team on the databases and pipelines that store, serve and process billions of satellite observations and millions of parcel- and property-level records. Though formally a student position, you will have real responsibility and be able to shape our technical development. We expect someone with relevant, hands-on experience who can contribute from day one. The focus is on building reliable, performant Postgres-based systems and on running large-scale computations efficiently against them.
Design, maintain and evolve our PostgreSQL/PostGIS schemas for geospatial and time-series data.
Tune queries, indexes and storage layouts so our pipelines run fast and predictably at scale.
Build and improve data ingestion and processing pipelines that move Earth Observation data from raw tiles to product-ready tables.
Support large-scale computation across our cloud environment (Google Cloud) and on-prem workstations.
Contribute to monitoring, backups and data quality checks so the team can move quickly with confidence.
Work collaboratively with the modelling and product teams and take ownership of defined tasks.
Currently enrolled in a BSc or MSc programme in computer science, software engineering, data engineering, or a related field.
Solid, hands-on experience with PostgreSQL: schema design, writing non-trivial SQL, understanding indexes and query plans.
Comfortable with Python and Git, and with building data pipelines or batch jobs.
Familiarity with large-scale or distributed computing concepts — e.g. parallel processing, cloud compute, working with datasets that do not fit in memory.
Good engineering practices: version control, testing, clear code, and the ability to debug systems beyond your own code.
Based in or near Copenhagen and available for regular in-person collaboration.

Nice to have: experience with PostGIS or other geo-spatial-temporal databases, raster/vector data formats (e.g. GeoTIFF, Parquet, Zarr), Google Cloud (GCS, BigQuery, Cloud Run), Docker, or workflow orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow, Prefect, Dagster).
Real responsibility on the data infrastructure that supports our customers.
Close mentorship from senior colleagues and direct exposure to how a climate/fintech product is built end-to-end.
Hands-on experience with large geospatial datasets in an applied, impactful setting.
Flexible hours to fit your studies and a collaborative office environment.

Apply on The Hub with: CV, grade transcripts, a short motivation (one page max) and links or attachments showcasing relevant work (GitHub, project reports, schemas or queries you are proud of). We prioritise demonstrated experience and practical results. If you have built or operated databases or data pipelines — in coursework, internships, side projects or work — we want to hear from you.

About Envira

Envira turns satellite and environmental data into decision-ready intelligence for finance, government and agriculture. We combine Earth Observation, modern ML and AI, and regulatory expertise to deliver auditable, business-ready risk metrics from parcel-level agricultural insight to property-level flood scoring. You will join a small, technical team building tools that banks, insurers and public agencies use to make critical climate and financial decisions.

The role

We are looking for an ambitious student to help us scale the data backbone behind our products. You will work closely with the core engineering and modelling team on the databases and pipelines that store, serve and process billions of satellite observations and millions of parcel- and property-level records. Though formally a student position, you will have real responsibility and be able to shape our technical development. We expect someone with relevant, hands-on experience who can contribute from day one. The focus is on building reliable, performant Postgres-based systems and on running large-scale computations efficiently against them.

What you will do

  • Design, maintain and evolve our PostgreSQL/PostGIS schemas for geospatial and time-series data.
  • Tune queries, indexes and storage layouts so our pipelines run fast and predictably at scale.
  • Build and improve data ingestion and processing pipelines that move Earth Observation data from raw tiles to product-ready tables.
  • Support large-scale computation across our cloud environment (Google Cloud) and on-prem workstations.
  • Contribute to monitoring, backups and data quality checks so the team can move quickly with confidence.
  • Work collaboratively with the modelling and product teams and take ownership of defined tasks.

What we are looking for

  • Currently enrolled in a BSc or MSc programme in computer science, software engineering, data engineering, or a related field.
  • Solid, hands-on experience with PostgreSQL: schema design, writing non-trivial SQL, understanding indexes and query plans.
  • Comfortable with Python and Git, and with building data pipelines or batch jobs.
  • Familiarity with large-scale or distributed computing concepts — e.g. parallel processing, cloud compute, working with datasets that do not fit in memory.
  • Good engineering practices: version control, testing, clear code, and the ability to debug systems beyond your own code.
  • Based in or near Copenhagen and available for regular in-person collaboration.
  • Nice to have: experience with PostGIS or other geo-spatial-temporal databases, raster/vector data formats (e.g. GeoTIFF, Parquet, Zarr), Google Cloud (GCS, BigQuery, Cloud Run), Docker, or workflow orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow, Prefect, Dagster).

Why join Envira

  • Real responsibility on the data infrastructure that supports our customers.
  • Close mentorship from senior colleagues and direct exposure to how a climate/fintech product is built end-to-end.
  • Hands-on experience with large geospatial datasets in an applied, impactful setting.
  • Flexible hours to fit your studies and a collaborative office environment.

How to apply

Apply on The Hub with: CV, grade transcripts, a short motivation (one page max) and links or attachments showcasing relevant work (GitHub, project reports, schemas or queries you are proud of). We prioritise demonstrated experience and practical results. If you have built or operated databases or data pipelines — in coursework, internships, side projects or work — we want to hear from you.

4413490652,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/laborant-kolding-at-danish-agro-4413490652?position=8&pageNum=2&refId=YzT97kHG2dY51cuxV2bh6g%3D%3D&trackingId=b1K4mw8NXqnagJi5H7Cqsw%3D%3D,Laborant - Kolding,Danish Agro,"Kolding, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Er du uddannet laborant, og vil du arbejde fokuseret med forskellige analysemetoder samt uddybe din faglige indsigt inden for grovvarer, har du nu muligheden. Vi søger en laborant til vores laboratorie i Kolding.

Danish Agro koncernen består af ca. 110 agro-industrielle selskaber i ind- og udland. Vi er sat i verden for at skabe værdi for de landmænd, som ejer os, og vi beskæftiger os primært med produktion og handel af grovvarer. Vores virksomhed aftager blandt andet et bredt udvalg af afgrøder fra dansk landbrug og producerer forskellige typer foder. Du vil indgå i teamet på laboratoriet i Kolding, hvor vi screener og analyserer korn-, foder- og råvare prøver samt miljø prøver fra vores foderfabrikker.
Her bliver du en del af et team på 3 laboranter, der er organiseret i kvalitetsafdelingen, som du vil have tæt samarbejde med.
Fuldt fokus på at foretage analyser

Som laborant er din kerneopgave analyse af de prøver der modtages på laboratoriet, herunder indgående prøver på korn fra landbruget. Med kornprøver fra forskellige partier, lagre og forarbejdningsinstanser anvender du dine analytiske og teknologiske kompetencer, når du analyserer kornet med fokus på kvalitet, prissætning og fremtidig produktion. Du vil blandt andet komme til at arbejde med NIT-, renheds-, faldtals- og toksinanalyser på Elisa. Herudover vil du sammen med kollegaer arbejde med NIR analyser til foder prøver, samt PCR til hygiejneprøver.
Høj faglighed og tempo i selskab med gode kolleger

Du træder ind i et åbent og omsorgsfuldt miljø, med en god omgangstone, hvor vi sørger godt for hinanden. Du vil opleve tæt sparring på tværs af virksomheden og mellem specialfunktioner som produktchefer, kvalitet, produktion og de øvrige laboratorier i virksomheden – også når der er fart på.
Arbejdet med afgrøder er sæsonbetinget, og du vil derfor opleve travlhed i høstperioden.

Det er vigtigt, at du har en struktureret og ansvarsbevidst tilgang til dit arbejde, da du kommer til at lave analyser og kvalitetsarbejde, der kræver høj præcision. Der vil være en del rutineopgaver forbundet med jobbet. Derfor er det afgørende, at du trives med denne type opgaver.
Du er laborant gerne med kendskab til plantebiologi.
Du er klar på en hverdag, hvor du arbejder stående, og som kan være fysisk krævende i perioder.
Du har gode IT-kundskaber og er hjemmevant i MS Word og Excel – vi lærer dig op i vores analyseprogrammer.
Du er fleksibel og klar til at give en ekstra indsats i spidsbelastninger så som høstperioden.

Hos Danish Agro bygger vores fundament på samarbejde, og vi har en grundlæggende tro på, at vi er stærkere sammen. Vi tilbyder således en arbejdsplads, hvor vi udvikler i fællesskab – på tværs af afdelinger, kompetencer og landegrænser. Medarbejderne er vores vigtigste ressource, og vi gør os umage med at skabe de bedste rammer for trivsel, udvikling og indflydelse.
Vi tilbyder her en spændende og udfordrende stilling med gode muligheder for personlig og faglig udvikling. Løn og ansættelsesforhold aftales på baggrund af den enkeltes kvalifikationer.

Synes du, at stillingen lyder spændende, så send os gerne din ansøgning i dag eller hurtigst muligt ved at klikke på ”Søg jobbet”. Vi forventer at afholde samtaler løbende, og vi stopper søgningen, når den rette kandidat er fundet. Vi tror på, at mangfoldighed styrker opgaveløsningen, og opfordrer derfor alle interesserede – uanset baggrund – at søge stillingen. Vi håber at høre fra dig!
Bliv en del af noget større

Danish Agro koncernen er en international landbrugskoncern med mere end 5.500 kollegaer fordelt på 100+ selskaber i 17 lande. Fra marken til skærmen er medarbejderne kernen i vores evne til at skabe værdi og udvikle fremtidens landbrug. De sikrer nemlig, at vi kan spille en afgørende rolle i branchens digitale og grønne omstilling – og at vi hver dag hjælper landmanden med at dyrke fødevarer i verdensklasse. Og vi har også brug for dig!

4388951366,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/it-student-assistant-at-falck-4388951366?position=9&pageNum=2&refId=YzT97kHG2dY51cuxV2bh6g%3D%3D&trackingId=yca13AAadtxUw3WJ4Zp9IA%3D%3D,IT Student Assistant,Falck,"Vejle, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Job Description

IT Field Support & Services, Europe department is looking for one student assistant. You will work in the Falck office in Vejle providing support for that office as well as cooperating with other Falck officed across Denmark.

You will be part of a team of IT supporters, where you share both the front-end and back-end tasks including administrative tasks related to mobile phones / mobile subscriptions. We expect you to be extremely service-minded with a problem-solving mindset and with a good understanding of IT environments. An interest in IT and technology as well as service management is essential for the role and you will be working with a wide range of systems, technologies and internal processes.

As a student assistant you will be expected to work between 16-20 hours each week, with the majority of these hours placed between 8-16 during a normal work week. There is some degree of flexibility regarding which days and how you place the hours, which can be agreed with your manager. This is especially important when your studies require more than normal from you, for instance in regards to exam periods.

IT Field Support & Services, Europe is a department under Global IT that provide IT services in all countries where Falck has larger operations. In Denmark, we are a team of 3 full-time resources and 3 student assistants and we are now looking into expanding.
Have an interest and experience in standard PC environment
Are in the beginning or middle or relevant IT education
Have understanding for PC hardware, how it connects and operates
Are comfortable to work in outlook, excel and word
Are well structured and interested in learning, understanding and improving internal processes
Fluency in written and spoken English and Danish is required.

At Falck we build on a legacy of more than one hundred years in healthcare and emergency services. With a presence in 25 countries, our 25,000 skilled professionals delivered more than 9.5 million services last year. We are committed to care, and to care for others, we need to take care of ourselves. That is why we continuously promote engagement, diversity, health, and safety among our employees. Every day, we work to advance sustainable healthcare. And every day, we turn care into action.

Our global functions are integral to enabling the organisation to live our purpose. Through dedication and expertise , these teams support Falck in delivering care and making a meaningful difference in people's lives. If you are motivated by contributing to something greater, Falck is the place to bring care into your career .

4404762088,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-team-data-foundation-at-trackunit-4404762088?position=2&pageNum=5&refId=ewWO9beimGC24JWn5nrfuA%3D%3D&trackingId=TQ%2F5SPkxXxQyxv85GpBFTg%3D%3D,Data Engineer - Team Data Foundation,Trackunit,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Mission: Process massive IoT data volumes reliably and fast.

We're looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Data Foundation team at Trackunit. Our team builds and operates IrisX Analytics - a customer-facing data platform powered by Databricks that gives construction companies direct access to their fleet and equipment data. Every second, telemetry streams in from millions of devices worldwide, and the volumes are only growing.

The challenge ahead is exciting: we need to scale IrisX Analytics to serve many more customers, keep data latency low as volumes grow, and enable AI-driven insights on top of some of the largest telematics datasets in the industry. We work closely with Databricks to push the boundaries of what the platform can do - both for our internal data lake and for our customers.

You'll be attached to one of our hubs in Aarhus, Aalborg, Copenhagen, or Kolding. We work remote-first, but the data team is mainly in Aarhus and Aalborg - so expect to join us there from time to time.

If you're excited about building a data platform that turns massive real-time IoT streams into something customers can act on, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world's most essential industries!

A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.
International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.

We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.

Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.

Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.

This role sits between customers' data and our platform. You will be the person to architect and implement how customers' raw subscription data gets transformed into clean, trusted data models that power our platform and ML pipelines.
work directly with the founding team, our customers, and our data engineers.
own customer onboarding from a data perspective. Understand each customer's data, model it in dbt, and ship it into production.
build and maintain the dbt layer that powers our product, our ML features, and our internal analytics.
become the technical point of contact for customers on data questions, joining calls, scoping integrations, and translating their business logic into models.
write tests and quality checks so we catch data issues before customers do.
work in a lean, high-performing team where you will have a significant impact on the product and assume numerous responsibilities.
bring your opinions and experiences on how to scale our setup from 10+ customers to 100+ customers.
Tech stack: SQL, dbt, BigQuery, Python, GCP.

you have a strong background as an analytics engineer, or as a data analyst who has grown into modeling and pipeline work.
you have used SQL and dbt to build models that other people depend on.
you're comfortable talking to non-technical stakeholders — running calls, asking the right questions, explaining tradeoffs.
you've modeled messy, real-world business data (subscriptions, transactions, events, finance) into clean shapes.
you have strong opinions about what good (and bad) data foundations look like.
you have an analytical mindset that thrives in fast-paced, loosely defined environments.
you have an ""anti-meeting"" culture mindset and value focused work time.
you prefer a primarily in-office way of working.
company card for all things office food/snacks
great breakfast and lunch (and dinner when working late in the office)
